config) : config_(config) {}\\n101: \\n102: FaultFilter::~FaultFilter() {\\n103:   ASSERT(delay_timer_ == nullptr);\\n104:   ASSERT(response_limiter_ == nullptr || response_limiter_->destroyed());\\n105: }\\n106: \\n107: // Delays and aborts are independent events. One can inject a delay\\n108: // followed by an abort or inject just a delay or abort. In this callback,\\n109: // if we inject a delay, then we will inject the abort in the delay timer\\n110: // callback.\\n111: Http::FilterHeadersStatus FaultFilter::decodeHeaders(Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers, bool) {\\n112:   // Route-level configuration overrides filter-level configuration\\n113:   // NOTE: We should not use runtime when reading from route-level\\n114:   // faults. In other words, runtime is supported only when faults are\\n115:   // configured at the filter level.\\n116:   fault_settings_ = config_->settings();\\n117:   const auto* per_route_settings =\\n118:       Http::Utility::resolveMostSpecificPerFilterConfig<FaultSettings>(decoder_callbacks_);\\n119:   fault_settings_ = per_route_settings ? per_route_settings : fault_settings_;\\n120: \\n121:   if (!matchesTargetUpstreamCluster()) {\\n122:     return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\\n123:   }\\n124: \\n125:   if (!matchesDownstreamNodes(headers)) {\\n126:     return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\\n127:   }\\n128: \\n129:   // Check for header matches\\n130:   if (!Http::HeaderUtility::matchHeaders(headers, fault_settings_->filterHeaders())) {\\n131:     return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\\n132:   }\\n133: \\n134:   if (headers.EnvoyDownstreamServiceCluster()) {\\n135:     downstream_cluster_ = std::string(headers.getEnvoyDownstreamServiceClusterValue());\\n136:     if (!downstream_cluster_.empty() && !fault_settings_->disableDownstreamClusterStats()) {\\n137:       downstream_cluster_storage_ = std::make_unique<Stats::StatNameDynamicStorage>(\\n138:           downstream_cluster_, config_->scope().symbolTable());\\n139:     }\\n140: \\n141:     downstream_cluster_delay_percent_key_ =\\n142:         fmt::format(\\\"fault.http.{}.delay.fixed_delay_percent\\\", downstream_cluster_);\\n143:     downstream_cluster_abort_percent_key_ =\\n144:         fmt::format(\\\"fault.http.{}.abort.abort_percent\\\", downstream_cluster_);\\n145:     downstream_cluster_delay_duration_key_ =\\n146:         fmt::format(\\\"fault.http.{}.delay.fixed_duration_ms\\\", downstream_cluster_);\\n147:     downstream_cluster_abort_http_status_key_ =\\n148:         fmt::format(\\\"fault.http.{}.abort.http_status\\\", downstream_cluster_);\\n149:     downstream_cluster_abort_grpc_status_key_ =\\n150:         fmt::format(\\\"fault.http.{}.abort.grpc_status\\\", downstream_cluster_);\\n151:   }\\n152: \\n153:   maybeSetupResponseRateLimit(headers);\\n154: \\n155:   if (maybeSetupDelay(headers)) {\\n156:     return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\\n157:   }\\n158: \\n159:   if (maybeDoAbort(headers)) {\\n160:     return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\\n161:   }\\n162: \\n163:   return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\\n164: }\\n165: \\n166: bool FaultFilter::maybeSetupDelay(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\\n167:   absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds> duration = delayDuration(request_headers);\\n168:   if (duration.has_value() && tryIncActiveFaults()) {\\n169:     delay_timer_ = decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher().createTimer(\\n170:         [this, &request_headers]() -> void { postDelayInjection(request_headers); });\\n171:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \\\"fault: delaying request {}ms\\\", duration.value().count());\\n172:     delay_timer_->enableTimer(duration.value(), &decoder_callbacks_->scope());\\n173:     recordDelaysInjectedStats();\\n174:     decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::DelayInjected);\\n175:     auto& dynamic_metadata = decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().dynamicMetadata();\\n176:     (*dynamic_metadata.mutable_filter_metadata())[decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName()] =\\n177:         fault_settings_->filterMetadata();\\n178:     return true;\\n179:   }\\n180:   return false;\\n181: }\\n182: \\n183: bool FaultFilter::maybeDoAbort(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\\n184:   absl::optional<Http::Code> http_status;\\n185:   absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status;\\n186:   std::tie(http_status, grpc_status) = abortStatus(request_headers);\\n187: \\n188:   if (http_status.has_value() && tryIncActiveFaults()) {\\n189:     abortWithStatus(http_status.value(), grpc_status);\\n190:     return true;\\n191:   }\\n192: \\n193:   return false;\\n194: }\\n195: \\n196: void FaultFilter::maybeSetupResponseRateLimit(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\\n197:   if (!isResponseRateLimitEnabled(request_headers)) {\\n198:     return;\\n199:   }\\n200: \\n201:   absl::optional<uint64_t> rate_kbps =\\n202:       fault_settings_->responseRateLimit()->rateKbps(&request_headers);\\n203:   if (!rate_kbps.has_value()) {\\n204:     return;\\n205:   }\\n206: \\n207:   if (!tryIncActiveFaults()) {\\n208:     return;\\n209:   }\\n210: \\n211:   config_->stats().response_rl_injected_.inc();\\n212: \\n213:   response_limiter_ = std::make_unique<Envoy::Extensions::HttpFilters::Common::StreamRateLimiter>(\\n214:       rate_kbps.value(), encoder_callbacks_->bufferLimit(),\\n215:       [this] { encoder_callbacks_->onEncoderFilterAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark(); },\\n216:       [this] { encoder_callbacks_->onEncoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark(); },\\n217:       [this](Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) {\\n218:         encoder_callbacks_->injectEncodedDataToFilterChain(data, end_stream);\\n219:       },\\n220:       [this] { encoder_callbacks_->continueEncoding(); },\\n221:       [](uint64_t, bool, std::chrono::milliseconds) {\\n222:         // write stats callback.\\n223:       },\\n224:       config_->timeSource(), decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher(), decoder_callbacks_->scope());\\n225: }\\n226: \\n227: bool FaultFilter::faultOverflow() {\\n228:   const uint64_t max_faults = config_->runtime().snapshot().getInteger(\\n229:       fault_settings_->maxActiveFaultsRuntime(), fault_settings_->maxActiveFaults().has_value()\\n230:                                                      ? fault_settings_->maxActiveFaults().value()\\n231:                                                      : std::numeric_limits<uint64_t>::max());\\n232:   // Note: Since we don't compare/swap here this is a fuzzy limit which is similar to how the\\n233:   // other circuit breakers work.\\n234:   if (config_->stats().active_faults_.value() >= max_faults) {\\n235:     config_->stats().faults_overflow_.inc();\\n236:     return true;\\n237:   }\\n238: \\n239:   return false;\\n240: }\\n241: \\n242: bool FaultFilter::isDelayEnabled(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\\n243:   const auto request_delay = fault_settings_->requestDelay();\\n244:   if (request_delay == nullptr) {\\n245:     return false;\\n246:   }\\n247: \\n248:   if (!downstream_cluster_delay_percent_key_.empty()) {\\n249:     return config_->runtime().snapshot().featureEnabled(\\n250:         downstream_cluster_delay_percent_key_, request_delay->percentage(&request_headers));\\n251:   }\\n252:   return config_->runtime().snapshot().featureEnabled(fault_settings_->delayPercentRuntime(),\\n253:                                                       request_delay->percentage(&request_headers));\\n254: }\\n255: \\n256: bool FaultFilter::isAbortEnabled(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\\n257:   const auto request_abort = fault_settings_->requestAbort();\\n258:   if (request_abort == nullptr) {\\n259:     return false;\\n260:   }\\n261: \\n262:   if (!downstream_cluster_abort_percent_key_.empty()) {\\n263:     return config_->runtime().snapshot().featureEnabled(\\n264:         downstream_cluster_abort_percent_key_, request_abort->percentage(&request_headers));\\n265:   }\\n266:   return config_->runtime().snapshot().featureEnabled(fault_settings_->abortPercentRuntime(),\\n267:                                                       request_abort->percentage(&request_headers));\\n268: }\\n269: \\n270: bool FaultFilter::isResponseRateLimitEnabled(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\\n271:   if (!isResponseRateLimitConfigured()) {\\n272:     return false;\\n273:   }\\n274: \\n275:   // TODO(mattklein123): Allow runtime override via downstream cluster similar to the other keys.\\n276:   return config_->runtime().snapshot().featureEnabled(\\n277:       fault_settings_->responseRateLimitPercentRuntime(),\\n278:       fault_settings_->responseRateLimit()->percentage(&request_headers));\\n279: }\\n280: \\n281: absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds>\\n282: FaultFilter::delayDuration(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\\n283:   absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds> ret;\\n284: \\n285:   if (!isDelayEnabled(request_headers)) {\\n286:     return ret;\\n287:   }\\n288: \\n289:   // See if the configured delay provider has a default delay, if not there is no delay (e.g.,\\n290:   // header configuration and no/invalid header).\\n291:   auto config_duration = fault_settings_->requestDelay()->duration(&request_headers);\\n292:   if (!config_duration.has_value()) {\\n293:     return ret;\\n294:   }\\n295: \\n296:   std::chrono::milliseconds duration =\\n297:       std::chrono::milliseconds(config_->runtime().snapshot().getInteger(\\n298:           fault_settings_->delayDurationRuntime(), config_duration.value().count()));\\n299:   if (!downstream_cluster_delay_duration_key_.empty()) {\\n300:     duration = std::chrono::milliseconds(config_->runtime().snapshot().getInteger(\\n301:         downstream_cluster_delay_duration_key_, duration.count()));\\n302:   }\\n303: \\n304:   // Delay only if the duration is >0ms\\n305:   if (duration.count() > 0) {\\n306:     ret = duration;\\n307:   }\\n308: \\n309:   return ret;\\n310: }\\n311: \\n312: AbortHttpAndGrpcStatus FaultFilter::abortStatus(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\\n313:   if (!isAbortEnabled(request_headers)) {\\n314:     return AbortHttpAndGrpcStatus{absl::nullopt, absl::nullopt};\\n315:   }\\n316: \\n317:   auto http_status = abortHttpStatus(request_headers);\\n318:   // If http status code is set, then gRPC status won't be used.\\n319:   if (http_status.has_value()) {\\n320:     return AbortHttpAndGrpcStatus{http_status, absl::nullopt};\\n321:   }\\n322: \\n323:   auto grpc_status = abortGrpcStatus(request_headers);\\n324:   // If gRPC status code is set, then http status will be set to Http::Code::OK (200)\\n325:   if (grpc_status.has_value()) {\\n326:     return AbortHttpAndGrpcStatus{Http::Code::OK, grpc_status};\\n327:   }\\n328: \\n329:   return AbortHttpAndGrpcStatus{absl::nullopt, absl::nullopt};\\n330: }\\n331: \\n332: absl::optional<Http::Code>\\n333: FaultFilter::abortHttpStatus(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\\n334:   // See if the configured abort provider has a default status code, if not there is no abort status\\n335:   // code (e.g., header configuration and no/invalid header).\\n336:   auto http_status = fault_settings_->requestAbort()->httpStatusCode(&request_headers);\\n337:   if (!http_status.has_value()) {\\n338:     return absl::nullopt;\\n339:   }\\n340: \\n341:   auto default_http_status_code = static_cast<uint64_t>(http_status.value());\\n342:   auto runtime_http_status_code = config_->runtime().snapshot().getInteger(\\n343:       fault_settings_->abortHttpStatusRuntime(), default_http_status_code);\\n344: \\n345:   if (!downstream_cluster_abort_http_status_key_.empty()) {\\n346:     runtime_http_status_code = config_->runtime().snapshot().getInteger(\\n347:         downstream_cluster_abort_http_status_key_, default_http_status_code);\\n348:   }\\n349: \\n350:   return static_cast<Http::Code>(runtime_http_status_code);\\n351: }\\n352: \\n353: absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus>\\n354: FaultFilter::abortGrpcStatus(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\\n355:   auto grpc_status = fault_settings_->requestAbort()->grpcStatusCode(&request_headers);\\n356:   if (!grpc_status.has_value()) {\\n357:     return absl::nullopt;\\n358:   }\\n359: \\n360:   auto default_grpc_status_code = static_cast<uint64_t>(grpc_status.value());\\n361:   auto runtime_grpc_status_code = config_->runtime().snapshot().getInteger(\\n362:       fault_settings_->abortGrpcStatusRuntime(), default_grpc_status_code);\\n363: \\n364:   if (!downstream_cluster_abort_grpc_status_key_.empty()) {\\n365:     runtime_grpc_status_code = config_->runtime().snapshot().getInteger(\\n366:         downstream_cluster_abort_grpc_status_key_, default_grpc_status_code);\\n367:   }\\n368: \\n369:   return static_cast<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus>(runtime_grpc_status_code);\\n370: }\\n371: \\n372: void FaultFilter::recordDelaysInjectedStats() {\\n373:   // Downstream specific stats.\\n374:   if (!downstream_cluster_.empty() && !fault_settings_->disableDownstreamClusterStats()) {\\n375:     config_->incDelays(downstream_cluster_storage_->statName());\\n376:   }\\n377: \\n378:   config_->stats().delays_injected_.inc();\\n379: }\\n380: \\n381: void FaultFilter::recordAbortsInjectedStats() {\\n382:   // Downstream specific stats.\\n383:   if (!downstream_cluster_.empty() && !fault_settings_->disableDownstreamClusterStats()) {\\n384:     config_->incAborts(downstream_cluster_storage_->statName());\\n385:   }\\n386: \\n387:   config_->stats().aborts_injected_.inc();\\n388: }\\n389: \\n390: Http::FilterDataStatus FaultFilter::decodeData(Buffer::Instance&, bool) {\\n391:   if (delay_timer_ == nullptr) {\\n392:     return Http::FilterDataStatus::Continue;\\n393:   }\\n394:   // If the request is too large, stop reading new data until the buffer drains.\\n395:   return Http::FilterDataStatus::StopIterationAndWatermark;\\n396: }\\n397: \\n398: Http::FilterTrailersStatus FaultFilter::decodeTrailers(Http::RequestTrailerMap&) {\\n399:   return delay_timer_ == nullptr ? Http::FilterTrailersStatus::Continue\\n400:                                  : Http::FilterTrailersStatus::StopIteration;\\n401: }\\n402: \\n403: FaultFilterStats FaultFilterConfig::generateStats(const std::string& prefix, Stats::Scope& scope) {\\n404:   const std::string final_prefix = prefix + \\\"fault.\\\";\\n405:   return {ALL_FAULT_FILTER_STATS(POOL_COUNTER_PREFIX(scope, final_prefix),\\n406:                                  POOL_GAUGE_PREFIX(scope, final_prefix))};\\n407: }\\n408: \\n409: bool FaultFilter::tryIncActiveFaults() {\\n410:   // Only charge 1 active fault per filter in case we are injecting multiple faults.\\n411:   // Since we count at most one active fault per filter, we also allow multiple faults\\n412:   // per filter without checking for overflow.\\n413:   if (fault_active_) {\\n414:     return true;\\n415:   }\\n416: \\n417:   // We only check for overflow when attempting to perform a fault. Note that this means that a\\n418:   // single request might increment the counter more than once if it tries to apply multiple faults,\\n419:   // and it is also possible for it to fail the first check then succeed on the second (should\\n420:   // another thread decrement the active fault gauge).\\n421:   if (faultOverflow()) {\\n422:     return false;\\n423:   }\\n424: \\n425:   // TODO(mattklein123): Consider per-fault type active fault gauges.\\n426:   config_->stats().active_faults_.inc();\\n427:   fault_active_ = true;\\n428: \\n429:   return true;\\n430: }\\n431: \\n432: void FaultFilter::onDestroy() {\\n433:   resetTimerState();\\n434:   if (response_limiter_ != nullptr) {\\n435:     response_limiter_->destroy();\\n436:   }\\n437:   if (fault_active_) {\\n438:     config_->stats().active_faults_.dec();\\n439:   }\\n440: }\\n441: \\n442: bool FaultFilter::isResponseRateLimitConfigured() {\\n443:   return fault_settings_->responseRateLimit() != nullptr;\\n444: }\\n445: \\n446: void FaultFilter::postDelayInjection(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\\n447:   resetTimerState();\\n448: \\n449:   // Delays can be followed by aborts\\n450:   absl::optional<Http::Code> http_status;\\n451:   absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status;\\n452:   std::tie(http_status, grpc_status) = abortStatus(request_headers);\\n453: \\n454:   if (http_status.has_value()) {\\n455:     abortWithStatus(http_status.value(), grpc_status);\\n456:   } else {\\n457:     // Should not continue to count as an active fault after the delay has elapsed if no other type\\n458:     // of fault is active. As the delay timer is always done at this point and followed abort faults\\n459:     // have been checked earlier, here we just check if there's a response rate limit configured.\\n460:     ASSERT(fault_active_);\\n461:     ASSERT(delay_timer_ == nullptr);\\n462:     if (!isResponseRateLimitConfigured()) {\\n463:       config_->stats().active_faults_.dec();\\n464:       fault_active_ = false;\\n465:     }\\n466: \\n467:     // Continue request processing.\\n468:     decoder_callbacks_->continueDecoding();\\n469:   }\\n470: }\\n471: \\n472: void FaultFilter::abortWithStatus(Http::Code http_status_code,\\n473:                                   absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status) {\\n474:   recordAbortsInjectedStats();\\n475:   decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::FaultInjected);\\n476:   auto& dynamic_metadata = decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().dynamicMetadata();\\n477:   (*dynamic_metadata.mutable_filter_metadata())[decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName()] =\\n478:       fault_settings_->filterMetadata();\\n479:   decoder_callbacks_->sendLocalReply(http_status_code, \\\"fault filter abort\\\", nullptr, grpc_status,\\n480:                                      RcDetails::get().FaultAbort);\\n481: }\\n482: \\n483: bool FaultFilter::matchesTargetUpstreamCluster() {\\n484:   bool matches = true;\\n485: \\n486:   if (!fault_settings_->upstreamCluster().empty()) {\\n487:     Router::RouteConstSharedPtr route = decoder_callbacks_->route();\\n488:     matches = route && route->routeEntry() &&\\n489:               (route->routeEntry()->clusterName() == fault_settings_->upstreamCluster());\\n490:   }\\n491: \\n492:   return matches;\\n493: }\\n494: \\n495: bool FaultFilter::matchesDownstreamNodes(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\\n496:   if (fault_settings_->downstreamNodes().empty()) {\\n497:     return true;\\n498:   }\\n499: \\n500:   if (!headers.EnvoyDownstreamServiceNode()) {\\n501:     return false;\\n502:   }\\n503: \\n504:   const absl::string_view downstream_node = headers.getEnvoyDownstreamServiceNodeValue();\\n505:   return fault_settings_->downstreamNodes().find(downstream_node) !=\\n506:          fault_settings_->downstreamNodes().end();\\n507: }\\n508: \\n509: void FaultFilter::resetTimerState() {\\n510:   if (delay_timer_) {\\n511:     delay_timer_->disableTimer();\\n512:     delay_timer_.reset();\\n513:   }\\n514: }\\n515: \\n516: Http::FilterDataStatus FaultFilter::encodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) {\\n517:   if (response_limiter_ != nullptr) {\\n518:     response_limiter_->writeData(data, end_stream);\\n519:     return Http::FilterDataStatus::StopIterationNoBuffer;\\n520:   }\\n521: \\n522:   return Http::FilterDataStatus::Continue;\\n523: }\\n524: \\n525: Http::FilterTrailersStatus FaultFilter::encodeTrailers(Http::ResponseTrailerMap&) {\\n526:   if (response_limiter_ != nullptr) {\\n527:     return response_limiter_->onTrailers() ? Http::FilterTrailersStatus::StopIteration\\n528:                                            : Http::FilterTrailersStatus::Continue;\\n529:   }\\n530: \\n531:   return Http::FilterTrailersStatus::Continue;\\n532: }\\n533: \\n534: } // namespace Fault\\n535: } // namespace HttpFilters\\n536: } // namespace Extensions\\n537: } // namespace Envoy\\n538: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/extensions/filters/http/fault/fault_filter.cc\"}"

route_typed_metadata_context_namespaces_(\\n130:           config.route_typed_metadata_context_namespaces().begin(),\\n131:           config.route_typed_metadata_context_namespaces().end()),\\n132:       include_peer_certificate_(config.include_peer_certificate()),\\n133:       include_tls_session_(config.include_tls_session()),\\n134:       charge_cluster_response_stats_(\\n135:           PROTOBUF_GET_WRAPPED_OR_DEFAULT(config, charge_cluster_response_stats, true)),\\n136:       stats_(generateStats(stats_prefix, config.stat_prefix(), scope)),\\n137:       ext_authz_ok_(pool_.add(createPoolStatName(config.stat_prefix(), \\\"ok\\\"))),\\n138:       ext_authz_denied_(pool_.add(createPoolStatName(config.stat_prefix(), \\\"denied\\\"))),\\n139:       ext_authz_error_(pool_.add(createPoolStatName(config.stat_prefix(), \\\"error\\\"))),\\n140:       ext_authz_invalid_(pool_.add(createPoolStatName(config.stat_prefix(), \\\"invalid\\\"))),\\n141:       ext_authz_failure_mode_allowed_(\\n142:           pool_.add(createPoolStatName(config.stat_prefix(), \\\"failure_mode_allowed\\\"))) {\\n143:   auto bootstrap = factory_context.bootstrap();\\n144:   auto labels_key_it =\\n145:       bootstrap.node().metadata().fields().find(config.bootstrap_metadata_labels_key());\\n146:   if (labels_key_it != bootstrap.node().metadata().fields().end()) {\\n147:     for (const auto& labels_it : labels_key_it->second.struct_value().fields()) {\\n148:       destination_labels_[labels_it.first] = labels_it.second.string_value();\\n149:     }\\n150:   }\\n151: \\n152:   if (config.has_allowed_headers() &&\\n153:       config.http_service().authorization_request().has_allowed_headers()) {\\n154:     throw EnvoyException(\\\"Invalid duplicate configuration for allowed_headers.\\\");\\n155:   }\\n156: \\n157:   // An unset request_headers_matchers_ means that all client request headers are allowed through\\n158:   // to the authz server; this is to preserve backwards compatibility when introducing\\n159:   // allowlisting of request headers for gRPC authz servers. Pre-existing support is for\\n160:   // HTTP authz servers only and defaults to blocking all but a few headers (i.e. Authorization,\\n161:   // Method, Path and Host).\\n162:   if (config.has_grpc_service() && config.has_allowed_headers()) {\\n163:     allowed_headers_matcher_ = Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckRequestUtils::toRequestMatchers(\\n164:         config.allowed_headers(), false, factory_context);\\n165:   } else if (config.has_http_service()) {\\n166:     if (config.http_service().authorization_request().has_allowed_headers()) {\\n167:       allowed_headers_matcher_ = Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckRequestUtils::toRequestMatchers(\\n168:           config.http_service().authorization_request().allowed_headers(), true, factory_context);\\n169:     } else {\\n170:       allowed_headers_matcher_ = Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckRequestUtils::toRequestMatchers(\\n171:           config.allowed_headers(), true, factory_context);\\n172:     }\\n173:   }\\n174:   if (config.has_disallowed_headers()) {\\n175:     disallowed_headers_matcher_ = Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckRequestUtils::toRequestMatchers(\\n176:         config.disallowed_headers(), false, factory_context);\\n177:   }\\n178: }\\n179: \\n180: void FilterConfigPerRoute::merge(const FilterConfigPerRoute& other) {\\n181:   // We only merge context extensions here, and leave boolean flags untouched since those flags are\\n182:   // not used from the merged config.\\n183:   auto begin_it = other.context_extensions_.begin();\\n184:   auto end_it = other.context_extensions_.end();\\n185:   for (auto it = begin_it; it != end_it; ++it) {\\n186:     context_extensions_[it->first] = it->second;\\n187:   }\\n188: }\\n189: \\n190: // Constructor used for merging configurations from different levels (vhost, route, etc.)\\n191: FilterConfigPerRoute::FilterConfigPerRoute(const FilterConfigPerRoute& less_specific,\\n192:                                            const FilterConfigPerRoute& more_specific)\\n193:     : context_extensions_(less_specific.context_extensions_),\\n194:       check_settings_(more_specific.check_settings_), disabled_(more_specific.disabled_),\\n195:       // Only use the most specific per-route override. Do not inherit overrides from less\\n196:       // specific configuration. If the more specific configuration has no override, leave both\\n197:       // unset so that the main filter configuration is used.\\n198:       grpc_service_(more_specific.grpc_service_.has_value() ? more_specific.grpc_service_\\n199:                                                             : absl::nullopt),\\n200:       http_service_(more_specific.http_service_.has_value() ? more_specific.http_service_\\n201:                                                             : absl::nullopt) {\\n202:   // Merge context extensions from more specific configuration, overriding less specific ones.\\n203:   for (const auto& extension : more_specific.context_extensions_) {\\n204:     context_extensions_[extension.first] = extension.second;\\n205:   }\\n206: }\\n207: \\n208: Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ClientPtr\\n209: Filter::createPerRouteGrpcClient(const envoy::config::core::v3::GrpcService& grpc_service) {\\n210:   if (server_context_ == nullptr) {\\n211:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\\n212:         debug, \\\"ext_authz filter: server context not available for per-route gRPC client creation.\\\",\\n213:         *decoder_callbacks_);\\n214:     return nullptr;\\n215:   }\\n216: \\n217:   // Use the timeout from the gRPC service configuration, use default if not specified.\\n218:   // A timeout of 0 means infinite (no timeout). Convert to nullopt in that case.\\n219:   const uint32_t timeout_ms =\\n220:       PROTOBUF_GET_MS_OR_DEFAULT(grpc_service, timeout, kDefaultPerRouteTimeoutMs);\\n221:   const absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds> timeout =\\n222:       timeout_ms == 0\\n223:           ? absl::nullopt\\n224:           : absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds>(std::chrono::milliseconds(timeout_ms));\\n225: \\n226:   // We can skip transport version check for per-route gRPC service here.\\n227:   // The transport version is already validated at the main configuration level.\\n228:   Envoy::Grpc::GrpcServiceConfigWithHashKey config_with_hash_key =\\n229:       Envoy::Grpc::GrpcServiceConfigWithHashKey(grpc_service);\\n230: \\n231:   auto client_or_error = server_context_->clusterManager()\\n232:                              .grpcAsyncClientManager()\\n233:                              .getOrCreateRawAsyncClientWithHashKey(config_with_hash_key,\\n234:                                                                    server_context_->scope(), true);\\n235:   if (!client_or_error.ok()) {\\n236:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(warn,\\n237:                      \\\"ext_authz filter: failed to create per-route gRPC client: {}. Falling back \\\"\\n238:                      \\\"to default client.\\\",\\n239:                      *decoder_callbacks_, client_or_error.status().ToString());\\n240:     return nullptr;\\n241:   }\\n242: \\n243:   ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \\\"ext_authz filter: created per-route gRPC client for cluster: {}.\\\",\\n244:                    *decoder_callbacks_,\\n245:                    grpc_service.has_envoy_grpc() ? grpc_service.envoy_grpc().cluster_name()\\n246:                                                  : \\\"google_grpc\\\");\\n247: \\n248:   return std::make_unique<Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::GrpcClientImpl>(client_or_error.value(),\\n249:                                                                      timeout);\\n250: }\\n251: \\n252: Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ClientPtr Filter::createPerRouteHttpClient(\\n253:     const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::HttpService& http_service) {\\n254:   if (server_context_ == nullptr) {\\n255:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\\n256:         debug, \\\"ext_authz filter: server context not available for per-route HTTP client creation.\\\",\\n257:         *decoder_callbacks_);\\n258:     return nullptr;\\n259:   }\\n260: \\n261:   // Use the timeout from the HTTP service configuration, use default if not specified.\\n262:   const uint32_t timeout_ms =\\n263:       PROTOBUF_GET_MS_OR_DEFAULT(http_service.server_uri(), timeout, kDefaultPerRouteTimeoutMs);\\n264: \\n265:   ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \\\"ext_authz filter: creating per-route HTTP client for URI: {}.\\\",\\n266:                    *decoder_callbacks_, http_service.server_uri().uri());\\n267: \\n268:   const auto client_config = std::make_shared<Extensions::Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ClientConfig>(\\n269:       http_service, config_->headersAsBytes(), timeout_ms, *server_context_);\\n270: \\n271:   return std::make_unique<Extensions::Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::RawHttpClientImpl>(\\n272:       server_context_->clusterManager(), client_config);\\n273: }\\n274: \\n275: void Filter::initiateCall(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\\n276:   if (filter_return_ == FilterReturn::StopDecoding) {\\n277:     return;\\n278:   }\\n279: \\n280:   // Now that we'll definitely be making the request, add filter state stats if configured to do so.\\n281:   const Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterStateSharedPtr& filter_state =\\n282:       decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().filterState();\\n283:   if ((config_->emitFilterStateStats() || config_->filterMetadata().has_value())) {\\n284:     if (!filter_state->hasData<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName())) {\\n285:       filter_state->setData(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName(),\\n286:                             std::make_shared<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(config_->filterMetadata()),\\n287:                             Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterState::StateType::Mutable,\\n288:                             Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterState::LifeSpan::Request);\\n289: \\n290:       // This may return nullptr (if there's a value at this name whose type doesn't match or isn't\\n291:       // mutable, for example), so we must check logging_info_ is not nullptr later.\\n292:       logging_info_ =\\n293:           filter_state->getDataMutable<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName());\\n294:     }\\n295:     if (logging_info_ == nullptr) {\\n296:       stats_.filter_state_name_collision_.inc();\\n297:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug,\\n298:                        \\\"Could not find logging info at {}! (Did another filter already put data \\\"\\n299:                        \\\"at this name?)\\\",\\n300:                        *decoder_callbacks_, decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName());\\n301:     }\\n302:   }\\n303: \\n304:   absl::optional<FilterConfigPerRoute> maybe_merged_per_route_config;\\n305:   for (const FilterConfigPerRoute& cfg :\\n306:        Http::Utility::getAllPerFilterConfig<FilterConfigPerRoute>(decoder_callbacks_)) {\\n307:     if (maybe_merged_per_route_config.has_value()) {\\n308:       FilterConfigPerRoute current_config = maybe_merged_per_route_config.value();\\n309:       maybe_merged_per_route_config.emplace(current_config, cfg);\\n310:     } else {\\n311:       maybe_merged_per_route_config.emplace(cfg);\\n312:     }\\n313:   }\\n314: \\n315:   Protobuf::Map<std::string, std::string> context_extensions;\\n316:   if (maybe_merged_per_route_config) {\\n317:     context_extensions = maybe_merged_per_route_config.value().takeContextExtensions();\\n318:   }\\n319: \\n320:   // Check if we need to use a per-route service override (gRPC or HTTP).\\n321:   if (maybe_merged_per_route_config) {\\n322:     if (maybe_merged_per_route_config->grpcService().has_value()) {\\n323:       const auto& grpc_service = maybe_merged_per_route_config->grpcService().value();\\n324:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \\\"ext_authz filter: using per-route gRPC service configuration.\\\",\\n325:                        *decoder_callbacks_);\\n326: \\n327:       // Create a new gRPC client for this route.\\n328:       auto per_route_client = createPerRouteGrpcClient(grpc_service);\\n329:       if (per_route_client != nullptr) {\\n330:         client_ = std::move(per_route_client);\\n331:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \\\"ext_authz filter: successfully created per-route gRPC client.\\\",\\n332:                          *decoder_callbacks_);\\n333:       } else {\\n334:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\\n335:             warn,\\n336:             \\\"ext_authz filter: failed to create per-route gRPC client, falling back to default.\\\",\\n337:             *decoder_callbacks_);\\n338:       }\\n339:     } else if (maybe_merged_per_route_config->httpService().has_value()) {\\n340:       const auto& http_service = maybe_merged_per_route_config->httpService().value();\\n341:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \\\"ext_authz filter: using per-route HTTP service configuration.\\\",\\n342:                        *decoder_callbacks_);\\n343: \\n344:       // Create a new HTTP client for this route.\\n345:       auto per_route_client = createPerRouteHttpClient(http_service);\\n346:       if (per_route_client != nullptr) {\\n347:         client_ = std::move(per_route_client);\\n348:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \\\"ext_authz filter: successfully created per-route HTTP client.\\\",\\n349:                          *decoder_callbacks_);\\n350:       } else {\\n351:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\\n352:             warn,\\n353:             \\\"ext_authz filter: failed to create per-route HTTP client, falling back to default.\\\",\\n354:             *decoder_callbacks_);\\n355:       }\\n356:     }\\n357:   }\\n358: \\n359:   // If metadata_context_namespaces or typed_metadata_context_namespaces is specified,\\n360:   // pass matching filter metadata to the ext_authz service.\\n361:   // If metadata key is set in both the connection and request metadata,\\n362:   // then the value will be the request metadata value.\\n363:   envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata metadata_context;\\n364:   fillMetadataContext({&decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().dynamicMetadata(),\\n365:                        &decoder_callbacks_->connection()->streamInfo().dynamicMetadata()},\\n366:                       config_->metadataContextNamespaces(),\\n367:                       config_->typedMetadataContextNamespaces(), metadata_context);\\n368: \\n369:   // Fill route_metadata_context from the selected route's metadata.\\n370:   envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata route_metadata_context;\\n371:   if (decoder_callbacks_->route() != nullptr) {\\n372:     fillMetadataContext({&decoder_callbacks_->route()->metadata()},\\n373:                         config_->routeMetadataContextNamespaces(),\\n374:                         config_->routeTypedMetadataContextNamespaces(), route_metadata_context);\\n375:   }\\n376: \\n377:   Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckRequestUtils::createHttpCheck(\\n378:       decoder_callbacks_, headers, std::move(context_extensions), std::move(metadata_context),\\n379:       std::move(route_metadata_context), check_request_, max_request_bytes_, config_->packAsBytes(),\\n380:       config_->headersAsBytes(), config_->includePeerCertificate(), config_->includeTLSSession(),\\n381:       config_->destinationLabels(), config_->allowedHeadersMatcher(),\\n382:       config_->disallowedHeadersMatcher());\\n383: \\n384:   ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"ext_authz filter calling authorization server.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_);\\n385:   // Store start time of ext_authz filter call\\n386:   start_time_ = decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime();\\n387: \\n388:   state_ = State::Calling;\\n389:   filter_return_ = FilterReturn::StopDecoding; // Don't let the filter chain continue as we are\\n390:                                                // going to invoke check call.\\n391:   cluster_ = decoder_callbacks_->clusterInfo();\\n392:   initiating_call_ = true;\\n393:   client_->check(*this, check_request_, decoder_callbacks_->activeSpan(),\\n394:                  decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo());\\n395:   initiating_call_ = false;\\n396: }\\n397: \\n398: Http::FilterHeadersStatus Filter::decodeHeaders(Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers, bool end_stream) {\\n399:   Router::RouteConstSharedPtr route = decoder_callbacks_->route();\\n400:   const auto per_route_flags = getPerRouteFlags(route);\\n401:   skip_check_ = per_route_flags.skip_check_;\\n402:   if (skip_check_) {\\n403:     return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\\n404:   }\\n405: \\n406:   if (!config_->filterEnabled(decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().dynamicMetadata())) {\\n407:     stats_.disabled_.inc();\\n408:     if (config_->denyAtDisable()) {\\n409:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"ext_authz filter is disabled. Deny the request.\\\",\\n410:                        *decoder_callbacks_);\\n411:       decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(\\n412:           StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::UnauthorizedExternalService);\\n413:       decoder_callbacks_->sendLocalReply(\\n414:           config_->statusOnError(), EMPTY_STRING, nullptr, absl::nullopt,\\n415:           Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponseCodeDetails::get().AuthzError);\\n416:       return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\\n417:     }\\n418:     return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\\n419:   }\\n420: \\n421:   request_headers_ = &headers;\\n422:   const auto& check_settings = per_route_flags.check_settings_;\\n423:   buffer_data_ = (config_->withRequestBody() || check_settings.has_with_request_body()) &&\\n424:                  !check_settings.disable_request_body_buffering() &&\\n425:                  !(end_stream || Http::Utility::isWebSocketUpgradeRequest(headers) ||\\n426:                    Http::Utility::isH2UpgradeRequest(headers));\\n427: \\n428:   max_request_bytes_ = config_->maxRequestBytes();\\n429:   if (buffer_data_) {\\n430:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \\\"ext_authz filter is buffering the request\\\", *decoder_callbacks_);\\n431: \\n432:     allow_partial_message_ = check_settings.has_with_request_body()\\n433:                                  ? check_settings.with_request_body().allow_partial_message()\\n434:                                  : config_->allowPartialMessage();\\n435:     if (check_settings.has_with_request_body()) {\\n436:       max_request_bytes_ = check_settings.with_request_body().max_request_bytes();\\n437:     }\\n438:     if (!allow_partial_message_) {\\n439:       decoder_callbacks_->setBufferLimit(max_request_bytes_);\\n440:     }\\n441:     return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\\n442:   }\\n443: \\n444:   // Initiate a call to the authorization server since we are not disabled.\\n445:   initiateCall(headers);\\n446:   return filter_return_ == FilterReturn::StopDecoding\\n447:              ? Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopAllIterationAndWatermark\\n448:              : Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\\n449: }\\n450: \\n451: Http::FilterDataStatus Filter::decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) {\\n452:   if (buffer_data_ && !skip_check_) {\\n453:     const bool buffer_is_full = isBufferFull(data.length());\\n454:     if (end_stream || buffer_is_full) {\\n455:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \\\"ext_authz filter finished buffering the request since {}\\\",\\n456:                        *decoder_callbacks_, buffer_is_full ? \\\"buffer is full\\\" : \\\"stream is ended\\\");\\n457:       // Make sure data is available in initiateCall.\\n458:       decoder_callbacks_->addDecodedData(data, true);\\n459:       initiateCall(*request_headers_);\\n460:       return filter_return_ == FilterReturn::StopDecoding\\n461:                  ? Http::FilterDataStatus::StopIterationAndWatermark\\n462:                  : Http::FilterDataStatus::Continue;\\n463:     } else {\\n464:       return Http::FilterDataStatus::StopIterationAndBuffer;\\n465:     }\\n466:   }\\n467: \\n468:   return Http::FilterDataStatus::Continue;\\n469: }\\n470: \\n471: Http::FilterTrailersStatus Filter::decodeTrailers(Http::RequestTrailerMap&) {\\n472:   if (buffer_data_ && !skip_check_) {\\n473:     if (filter_return_ != FilterReturn::StopDecoding) {\\n474:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \\\"ext_authz filter finished buffering the request\\\",\\n475:                        *decoder_callbacks_);\\n476:       initiateCall(*request_headers_);\\n477:     }\\n478:     return filter_return_ == FilterReturn::StopDecoding ? Http::FilterTrailersStatus::StopIteration\\n479:                                                         : Http::FilterTrailersStatus::Continue;\\n480:   }\\n481: \\n482:   return Http::FilterTrailersStatus::Continue;\\n483: }\\n484: \\n485: Http::Filter1xxHeadersStatus Filter::encode1xxHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMap&) {\\n486:   return Http::Filter1xxHeadersStatus::Continue;\\n487: }\\n488: \\n489: Http::FilterHeadersStatus Filter::encodeHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMap& headers, bool) {\\n490:   ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\\n491:                    \\\"ext_authz filter has {} response header(s) to add and {} response header(s) to \\\"\\n492:                    \\\"set to the encoded response:\\\",\\n493:                    *encoder_callbacks_, response_headers_to_add_.size(),\\n494:                    response_headers_to_set_.size());\\n495:   if (!response_headers_to_add_.empty()) {\\n496:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\\n497:         trace, \\\"ext_authz filter added header(s) to the encoded response:\\\", *encoder_callbacks_);\\n498:     for (const auto& [key, value] : response_headers_to_add_) {\\n499:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"'{}':'{}'\\\", *encoder_callbacks_, key.get(), value);\\n500:       headers.addCopy(key, value);\\n501:       if (config_->enforceResponseHeaderLimits() && !headersWithinLimits(headers)) {\\n502:         responseHeaderLimitsReached();\\n503:         return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\\n504:       }\\n505:     }\\n506:   }\\n507: \\n508:   if (!response_headers_to_set_.empty()) {\\n509:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\\n510:         trace, \\\"ext_authz filter set header(s) to the encoded response:\\\", *encoder_callbacks_);\\n511:     for (const auto& [key, value] : response_headers_to_set_) {\\n512:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"'{}':'{}'\\\", *encoder_callbacks_, key.get(), value);\\n513:       headers.setCopy(key, value);\\n514:       if (config_->enforceResponseHeaderLimits() && !headersWithinLimits(headers)) {\\n515:         responseHeaderLimitsReached();\\n516:         return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\\n517:       }\\n518:     }\\n519:   }\\n520: \\n521:   if (!response_headers_to_add_if_absent_.empty()) {\\n522:     for (const auto& [key, value] : response_headers_to_add_if_absent_) {\\n523:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"'{}':'{}'\\\", *encoder_callbacks_, key.get(), value);\\n524:       if (auto header_entry = headers.get(key); header_entry.empty()) {\\n525:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"ext_authz filter added header(s) to the encoded response:\\\",\\n526:                          *encoder_callbacks_);\\n527:         headers.addCopy(key, value);\\n528:         if (config_->enforceResponseHeaderLimits() && !headersWithinLimits(headers)) {\\n529:           responseHeaderLimitsReached();\\n530:           return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\\n531:         }\\n532:       }\\n533:     }\\n534:   }\\n535: \\n536:   if (!response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists_.empty()) {\\n537:     for (const auto& [key, value] : response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists_) {\\n538:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"'{}':'{}'\\\", *encoder_callbacks_, key, value);\\n539:       if (auto header_entry = headers.get(key); !header_entry.empty()) {\\n540:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\\n541:             trace, \\\"ext_authz filter set header(s) to the encoded response:\\\", *encoder_callbacks_);\\n542:         headers.setCopy(key, value);\\n543:         if (config_->enforceResponseHeaderLimits() && !headersWithinLimits(headers)) {\\n544:           responseHeaderLimitsReached();\\n545:           return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\\n546:         }\\n547:       }\\n548:     }\\n549:   }\\n550: \\n551:   return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\\n552: }\\n553: \\n554: Http::FilterDataStatus Filter::encodeData(Buffer::Instance&, bool) {\\n555:   return Http::FilterDataStatus::Continue;\\n556: }\\n557: \\n558: Http::FilterTrailersStatus Filter::encodeTrailers(Http::ResponseTrailerMap&) {\\n559:   return Http::FilterTrailersStatus::Continue;\\n560: }\\n561: \\n562: Http::FilterMetadataStatus Filter::encodeMetadata(Http::MetadataMap&) {\\n563:   return Http::FilterMetadataStatus::Continue;\\n564: }\\n565: \\n566: void Filter::setDecoderFilterCallbacks(Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) {\\n567:   decoder_callbacks_ = &callbacks;\\n568: }\\n569: \\n570: void Filter::updateLoggingInfo(const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus>& grpc_status) {\\n571:   if (!config_->emitFilterStateStats()) {\\n572:     return;\\n573:   }\\n574: \\n575:   if (logging_info_ == nullptr) {\\n576:     return;\\n577:   }\\n578: \\n579:   if (grpc_status.has_value()) {\\n580:     logging_info_->setGrpcStatus(grpc_status.value());\\n581:   }\\n582: \\n583:   // Latency is the only stat available if we aren't using envoy grpc.\\n584:   if (start_time_.has_value()) {\\n585:     logging_info_->setLatency(std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::microseconds>(\\n586:         decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime() - start_time_.value()));\\n587:   }\\n588: \\n589:   auto const* stream_info = client_->streamInfo();\\n590:   if (stream_info == nullptr) {\\n591:     return;\\n592:   }\\n593: \\n594:   const auto& bytes_meter = stream_info->getUpstreamBytesMeter();\\n595:   if (bytes_meter != nullptr) {\\n596:     logging_info_->setBytesSent(bytes_meter->wireBytesSent());\\n597:     logging_info_->setBytesReceived(bytes_meter->wireBytesReceived());\\n598:   }\\n599:   if (stream_info->upstreamInfo().has_value()) {\\n600:     logging_info_->setUpstreamHost(stream_info->upstreamInfo()->upstreamHost());\\n601:   }\\n602:   absl::optional<Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr> cluster_info =\\n603:       stream_info->upstreamClusterInfo();\\n604:   if (cluster_info) {\\n605:     logging_info_->setClusterInfo(std::move(*cluster_info));\\n606:   }\\n607: }\\n608: \\n609: void Filter::setEncoderFilterCallbacks(Http::StreamEncoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) {\\n610:   encoder_callbacks_ = &callbacks;\\n611: }\\n612: \\n613: void Filter::onDestroy() {\\n614:   if (state_ == State::Calling) {\\n615:     state_ = State::Complete;\\n616:     client_->cancel();\\n617:   }\\n618: }\\n619: \\n620: CheckResult Filter::validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\\n621:     Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation operation, absl::string_view key,\\n622:     absl::string_view value) const {\\n623:   if (config_->validateMutations() && (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\\n624:                                        !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value))) {\\n625:     return CheckResult::FAIL;\\n626:   }\\n627:   // Check header mutation is valid according to configured decoder mutation rules.\\n628:   return config_->checkDecoderHeaderMutation(operation, Http::LowerCaseString(key), value);\\n629: }\\n630: \\n631: void Filter::onComplete(Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponsePtr&& response) {\\n632:   state_ = State::Complete;\\n633:   using Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus;\\n634:   Stats::StatName empty_stat_name;\\n635: \\n636:   updateLoggingInfo(response->grpc_status);\\n637: \\n638:   if (response->saw_invalid_append_actions) {\\n639:     if (config_->validateMutations()) {\\n640:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Rejecting response with invalid header append action.\\\",\\n641:                        *decoder_callbacks_);\\n642:       rejectResponse();\\n643:       return;\\n644:     }\\n645:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Ignoring response headers with invalid header append action.\\\",\\n646:                      *decoder_callbacks_);\\n647:   }\\n648: \\n649:   if (!response->dynamic_metadata.fields().empty()) {\\n650:     if (!config_->enableDynamicMetadataIngestion()) {\\n651:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\\n652:                        \\\"Response is trying to inject dynamic metadata, but dynamic metadata \\\"\\n653:                        \\\"ingestion is disabled. Ignoring...\\\",\\n654:                        *decoder_callbacks_);\\n655:       stats_.ignored_dynamic_metadata_.inc();\\n656:     } else {\\n657:       // Add duration of call to dynamic metadata if applicable\\n658:       if (start_time_.has_value() && response->status == CheckStatus::OK) {\\n659:         Protobuf::Value ext_authz_duration_value;\\n660:         auto duration =\\n661:             decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime() - start_time_.value();\\n662:         ext_authz_duration_value.set_number_value(\\n663:             std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(duration).count());\\n664:         (*response->dynamic_metadata.mutable_fields())[\\\"ext_authz_duration\\\"] =\\n665:             ext_authz_duration_value;\\n666:       }\\n667:       decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setDynamicMetadata(\\\"envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\\\",\\n668:                                                           response->dynamic_metadata);\\n669:     }\\n670:   }\\n671: \\n672:   switch (response->status) {\\n673:   case CheckStatus::OK: {\\n674:     // Any changes to request headers or query parameters can affect how the request is going to be\\n675:     // routed. If we are changing the headers we also need to clear the route\\n676:     // cache.\\n677:     if (config_->clearRouteCache() &&\\n678:         (!response->headers_to_set.empty() || !response->headers_to_append.empty() ||\\n679:          !response->headers_to_remove.empty() || !response->query_parameters_to_set.empty() ||\\n680:          !response->query_parameters_to_remove.empty())) {\\n681:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \\\"ext_authz is clearing route cache\\\", *decoder_callbacks_);\\n682:       decoder_callbacks_->downstreamCallbacks()->clearRouteCache();\\n683:     }\\n684: \\n685:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\\n686:                      \\\"ext_authz filter added header(s) to the request:\\\", *decoder_callbacks_);\\n687:     for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_set) {\\n688:       CheckResult check_result = validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\\n689:           Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation::SET, key, value);\\n690:       switch (check_result) {\\n691:       case CheckResult::OK:\\n692:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"'{}':'{}'\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\\n693:         request_headers_->setCopy(Http::LowerCaseString(key), value);\\n694:         if (!headersWithinLimits(*request_headers_)) {\\n695:           stats_.request_header_limits_reached_.inc();\\n696:           rejectResponse();\\n697:           return;\\n698:         }\\n699:         break;\\n700:       case CheckResult::IGNORE:\\n701:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Ignoring invalid header to set '{}':'{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_,\\n702:                          key, value);\\n703:         break;\\n704:       case CheckResult::FAIL:\\n705:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Rejecting invalid header to set '{}':'{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_,\\n706:                          key, value);\\n707:         rejectResponse();\\n708:         return;\\n709:       }\\n710:     }\\n711:     for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_add) {\\n712:       CheckResult check_result = validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\\n713:           Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation::SET, key, value);\\n714:       switch (check_result) {\\n715:       case CheckResult::OK:\\n716:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"'{}':'{}'\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\\n717:         request_headers_->addCopy(Http::LowerCaseString(key), value);\\n718:         if (!headersWithinLimits(*request_headers_)) {\\n719:           stats_.request_header_limits_reached_.inc();\\n720:           rejectResponse();\\n721:           return;\\n722:         }\\n723:         break;\\n724:       case CheckResult::IGNORE:\\n725:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Ignoring invalid header to add '{}':'{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_,\\n726:                          key, value);\\n727:         break;\\n728:       case CheckResult::FAIL:\\n729:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Rejecting invalid header to add '{}':'{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_,\\n730:                          key, value);\\n731:         rejectResponse();\\n732:         return;\\n733:       }\\n734:     }\\n735:     for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_append) {\\n736:       CheckResult check_result = validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\\n737:           Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation::APPEND, key, value);\\n738:       switch (check_result) {\\n739:       case CheckResult::OK: {\\n740:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"'{}':'{}'\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\\n741:         Http::LowerCaseString lowercase_key(key);\\n742:         const auto header_to_modify = request_headers_->get(lowercase_key);\\n743:         // TODO(dio): Add a flag to allow appending non-existent headers, without setting it\\n744:         // first (via `headers_to_add`). For example, given:\\n745:         // 1. Original headers {\\\"original\\\": \\\"true\\\"}\\n746:         // 2. Response headers from the authorization servers {{\\\"append\\\": \\\"1\\\"}, {\\\"append\\\":\\n747:         // \\\"2\\\"}}\\n748:         //\\n749:         // Currently it is not possible to add {{\\\"append\\\": \\\"1\\\"}, {\\\"append\\\": \\\"2\\\"}} (the\\n750:         // intended combined headers: {{\\\"original\\\": \\\"true\\\"}, {\\\"append\\\": \\\"1\\\"}, {\\\"append\\\":\\n751:         // \\\"2\\\"}}) to the request to upstream server by only sets `headers_to_append`.\\n752:         if (!header_to_modify.empty()) {\\n753:           ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"'{}':'{}'\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\\n754:           // The current behavior of appending is by combining entries with the same key,\\n755:           // into one entry. The value of that combined entry is separated by \\\",\\\".\\n756:           // TODO(dio): Consider to use addCopy instead.\\n757:           request_headers_->appendCopy(lowercase_key, value);\\n758:           if (!headersWithinLimits(*request_headers_)) {\\n759:             stats_.request_header_limits_reached_.inc();\\n760:             rejectResponse();\\n761:             return;\\n762:           }\\n763:         }\\n764:         break;\\n765:       }\\n766:       case CheckResult::IGNORE:\\n767:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Ignoring invalid header to append '{}':'{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_,\\n768:                          key, value);\\n769:         break;\\n770:       case CheckResult::FAIL:\\n771:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Rejecting invalid header to append '{}':'{}'.\\\",\\n772:                          *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\\n773:         rejectResponse();\\n774:         return;\\n775:       }\\n776:     }\\n777: \\n778:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\\n779:                      \\\"ext_authz filter removed header(s) from the request:\\\", *decoder_callbacks_);\\n780:     for (const auto& key : response->headers_to_remove) {\\n781:       // If the response contains an invalid header to remove, it's the same as trying to remove a\\n782:       // header that doesn't exist, so just ignore it.\\n783:       if (config_->validateMutations() && !Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key)) {\\n784:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Ignoring invalid header removal '{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key);\\n785:         continue;\\n786:       }\\n787:       // We don't allow removing any :-prefixed headers, nor Host, as removing them would make the\\n788:       // request malformed. checkDecoderHeaderMutation also performs this check, however, so only\\n789:       // perform this check explicitly if decoder header mutation rules is empty.\\n790:       if (!config_->hasDecoderHeaderMutationRules() &&\\n791:           !Http::HeaderUtility::isRemovableHeader(key)) {\\n792:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Ignoring invalid header removal '{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key);\\n793:         continue;\\n794:       }\\n795:       // Check header mutation is valid according to configured decoder header mutation rules.\\n796:       Http::LowerCaseString lowercase_key(key);\\n797:       switch (config_->checkDecoderHeaderMutation(CheckOperation::REMOVE, lowercase_key,\\n798:                                                   EMPTY_STRING)) {\\n799:       case CheckResult::OK:\\n800:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"'{}'\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key);\\n801:         request_headers_->remove(lowercase_key);\\n802:         if (!headersWithinLimits(*request_headers_)) {\\n803:           stats_.request_header_limits_reached_.inc();\\n804:           rejectResponse();\\n805:           return;\\n806:         }\\n807:         break;\\n808:       case CheckResult::IGNORE:\\n809:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Ignoring disallowed header removal '{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_,\\n810:                          key);\\n811:         break;\\n812:       case CheckResult::FAIL:\\n813:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Rejecting disallowed header removal '{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_,\\n814:                          key);\\n815:         rejectResponse();\\n816:         return;\\n817:       }\\n818:     }\\n819: \\n820:     if (!response->response_headers_to_add.empty()) {\\n821:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"ext_authz filter saving {} header(s) to add to the response:\\\",\\n822:                        *decoder_callbacks_, response->response_headers_to_add.size());\\n823:       for (const auto& [key, value] : response->response_headers_to_add) {\\n824:         if (config_->validateMutations() && (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\\n825:                                              !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value))) {\\n826:           ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Rejected invalid header '{}':'{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key,\\n827:                            value);\\n828:           rejectResponse();\\n829:           return;\\n830:         }\\n831:       }\\n832:       response_headers_to_add_ = Http::HeaderVector{response->response_headers_to_add.begin(),\\n833:                                                     response->response_headers_to_add.end()};\\n834:     }\\n835: \\n836:     if (!response->response_headers_to_set.empty()) {\\n837:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"ext_authz filter saving {} header(s) to set to the response:\\\",\\n838:                        *decoder_callbacks_, response->response_headers_to_set.size());\\n839:       for (const auto& [key, value] : response->response_headers_to_set) {\\n840:         if (config_->validateMutations() && (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\\n841:                                              !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value))) {\\n842:           ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Rejected invalid header '{}':'{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key,\\n843:                            value);\\n844:           rejectResponse();\\n845:           return;\\n846:         }\\n847:       }\\n848:       response_headers_to_set_ = Http::HeaderVector{response->response_headers_to_set.begin(),\\n849:                                                     response->response_headers_to_set.end()};\\n850:     }\\n851: \\n852:     if (!response->response_headers_to_add_if_absent.empty()) {\\n853:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"ext_authz filter saving {} header(s) to add to the response:\\\",\\n854:                        *decoder_callbacks_, response->response_headers_to_add_if_absent.size());\\n855:       for (const auto& [key, value] : response->response_headers_to_add_if_absent) {\\n856:         if (config_->validateMutations() && (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\\n857:                                              !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value))) {\\n858:           ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Rejected invalid header '{}':'{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key,\\n859:                            value);\\n860:           rejectResponse();\\n861:           return;\\n862:         }\\n863:       }\\n864:       response_headers_to_add_if_absent_ =\\n865:           Http::HeaderVector{response->response_headers_to_add_if_absent.begin(),\\n866:                              response->response_headers_to_add_if_absent.end()};\\n867:     }\\n868: \\n869:     if (!response->response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists.empty()) {\\n870:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"ext_authz filter saving {} header(s) to set to the response:\\\",\\n871:                        *decoder_callbacks_,\\n872:                        response->response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists.size());\\n873:       for (const auto& [key, value] : response->response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists) {\\n874:         if (config_->validateMutations() && (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\\n875:                                              !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value))) {\\n876:           ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Rejected invalid header '{}':'{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key,\\n877:                            value);\\n878:           rejectResponse();\\n879:           return;\\n880:         }\\n881:       }\\n882:       response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists_ =\\n883:           Http::HeaderVector{response->response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists.begin(),\\n884:                              response->response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists.end()};\\n885:     }\\n886: \\n887:     absl::optional<Http::Utility::QueryParamsMulti> modified_query_parameters;\\n888:     if (!response->query_parameters_to_set.empty()) {\\n889:       modified_query_parameters = Http::Utility::QueryParamsMulti::parseQueryString(\\n890:           request_headers_->Path()->value().getStringView());\\n891:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\\n892:           trace, \\\"ext_authz filter set query parameter(s) on the request:\\\", *decoder_callbacks_);\\n893:       for (const auto& [key, value] : response->query_parameters_to_set) {\\n894:         if (config_->validateMutations() &&\\n895:             (!Http::Utility::PercentEncoding::queryParameterIsUrlEncoded(key) ||\\n896:              !Http::Utility::PercentEncoding::queryParameterIsUrlEncoded(value))) {\\n897:           ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Rejected invalid query parameter {}={}.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_,\\n898:                            key, value);\\n899:           rejectResponse();\\n900:           return;\\n901:         }\\n902:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"'{}={}'\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\\n903:         modified_query_parameters->overwrite(key, value);\\n904:       }\\n905:     }\\n906: \\n907:     if (!response->query_parameters_to_remove.empty()) {\\n908:       if (!modified_query_parameters) {\\n909:         modified_query_parameters = Http::Utility::QueryParamsMulti::parseQueryString(\\n910:             request_headers_->Path()->value().getStringView());\\n911:       }\\n912:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"ext_authz filter removed query parameter(s) from the request:\\\",\\n913:                        *decoder_callbacks_);\\n914:       for (const auto& key : response->query_parameters_to_remove) {\\n915:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"'{}'\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key);\\n916:         modified_query_parameters->remove(key);\\n917:       }\\n918:     }\\n919: \\n920:     // We modified the query parameters in some way, so regenerate the `path` header and set it\\n921:     // here.\\n922:     if (modified_query_parameters) {\\n923:       const auto new_path =\\n924:           modified_query_parameters->replaceQueryString(request_headers_->Path()->value());\\n925:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\\n926:           trace, \\\"ext_authz filter modified query parameter(s), using new path for request: {}\\\",\\n927:           *decoder_callbacks_, new_path);\\n928:       request_headers_->setPath(new_path);\\n929:       if (!headersWithinLimits(*request_headers_)) {\\n930:         stats_.request_header_limits_reached_.inc();\\n931:         rejectResponse();\\n932:         return;\\n933:       }\\n934:     }\\n935: \\n936:     if (cluster_) {\\n937:       config_->incCounter(cluster_->statsScope(), config_->ext_authz_ok_);\\n938:     }\\n939:     stats_.ok_.inc();\\n940:     continueDecoding();\\n941:     break;\\n942:   }\\n943: \\n944:   case CheckStatus::Denied: {\\n945:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"ext_authz filter rejected the request. Response status code: '{}'\\\",\\n946:                      *decoder_callbacks_, enumToInt(response->status_code));\\n947:     stats_.denied_.inc();\\n948: \\n949:     if (cluster_) {\\n950:       config_->incCounter(cluster_->statsScope(), config_->ext_authz_denied_);\\n951:       if (config_->chargeClusterResponseStats()) {\\n952:         Http::CodeStats::ResponseStatInfo info{config_->scope(),\\n953:                                                cluster_->statsScope(),\\n954:                                                empty_stat_name,\\n955:                                                enumToInt(response->status_code),\\n956:                                                true,\\n957:                                                empty_stat_name,\\n958:                                                empty_stat_name,\\n959:                                                empty_stat_name,\\n960:                                                empty_stat_name,\\n961:                                                empty_stat_name,\\n962:                                                false};\\n963:         config_->httpContext().codeStats().chargeResponseStat(info, false);\\n964:       }\\n965:     }\\n966: \\n967:     // Check headers are valid.\\n968:     if (config_->validateMutations()) {\\n969:       for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_set) {\\n970:         if (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\\n971:             !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value)) {\\n972:           ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Rejected invalid header '{}':'{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key,\\n973:                            value);\\n974:           rejectResponse();\\n975:           return;\\n976:         }\\n977:       }\\n978:     }\\n979: \\n980:     if (config_->maxDeniedResponseBodyBytes() > 0 &&\\n981:         response->body.length() > config_->maxDeniedResponseBodyBytes()) {\\n982:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\\n983:           trace, \\\"ext_authz filter is truncating the response body from {} to {} bytes.\\\",\\n984:           *decoder_callbacks_, response->body.length(), config_->maxDeniedResponseBodyBytes());\\n985:       response->body.resize(config_->maxDeniedResponseBodyBytes());\\n986:     }\\n987: \\n988:     // setResponseFlag must be called before sendLocalReply\\n989:     decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(\\n990:         StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::UnauthorizedExternalService);\\n991:     decoder_callbacks_->sendLocalReply(\\n992:         response->status_code, response->body,\\n993:         [&headers = response->headers_to_set, &callbacks = *decoder_callbacks_,\\n994:          this](Http::HeaderMap& response_headers) -> void {\\n995:           ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\\n996:                            \\\"ext_authz filter added header(s) to the local response:\\\", callbacks);\\n997:           // Firstly, remove all headers requested by the ext_authz filter, to ensure that they will\\n998:           // override existing headers.\\n999:           for (const auto& [key, _] : headers) {\\n1000:             response_headers.remove(Http::LowerCaseString(key));\\n1001:           }\\n1002:           // Then set all of the requested headers, allowing the same header to be set multiple\\n1003:           // times, e.g. `Set-Cookie`.\\n1004:           for (const auto& [key, value] : headers) {\\n1005:             if (config_->enforceResponseHeaderLimits() &&\\n1006:                 response_headers.size() >= response_headers.maxHeadersCount()) {\\n1007:               stats_.omitted_response_headers_.inc();\\n1008:               ENVOY_LOG_EVERY_POW_2(\\n1009:                   warn,\\n1010:                   \\\"Some ext_authz response headers weren't added because the header map was full.\\\");\\n1011:               break;\\n1012:             }\\n1013:             ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\" '{}':'{}'\\\", callbacks, key, value);\\n1014:             response_headers.addCopy(Http::LowerCaseString(key), value);\\n1015:           }\\n1016:         },\\n1017:         absl::nullopt, Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponseCodeDetails::get().AuthzDenied);\\n1018:     break;\\n1019:   }\\n1020: \\n1021:   case CheckStatus::Error: {\\n1022:     if (cluster_) {\\n1023:       config_->incCounter(cluster_->statsScope(), config_->ext_authz_error_);\\n1024:     }\\n1025:     stats_.error_.inc();\\n1026: \\n1027:     // Validate error response headers and clear custom attributes if invalid.\\n1028:     validateAndClearInvalidErrorResponseAttributes(response);\\n1029: \\n1030:     // Apply max_denied_response_body_bytes limit to error response body as well.\\n1031:     if (config_->maxDeniedResponseBodyBytes() > 0 &&\\n1032:         response->body.length() > config_->maxDeniedResponseBodyBytes()) {\\n1033:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\\n1034:           trace, \\\"ext_authz filter is truncating the error response body from {} to {} bytes.\\\",\\n1035:           *decoder_callbacks_, response->body.length(), config_->maxDeniedResponseBodyBytes());\\n1036:       response->body.resize(config_->maxDeniedResponseBodyBytes());\\n1037:     }\\n1038: \\n1039:     if (config_->failureModeAllow()) {\\n1040:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"ext_authz filter allowed the request with error\\\",\\n1041:                        *decoder_callbacks_);\\n1042:       stats_.failure_mode_allowed_.inc();\\n1043:       if (cluster_) {\\n1044:         config_->incCounter(cluster_->statsScope(), config_->ext_authz_failure_mode_allowed_);\\n1045:       }\\n1046:       if (config_->failureModeAllowHeaderAdd()) {\\n1047:         request_headers_->addReferenceKey(\\n1048:             Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::Headers::get().EnvoyAuthFailureModeAllowed, \\\"true\\\");\\n1049:       }\\n1050:       continueDecoding();\\n1051:     } else {\\n1052:       // Use custom status code from error_response if provided, otherwise use status_on_error.\\n1053:       // Status code 0 means not set.\\n1054:       const Http::Code status_code = response->status_code != static_cast<Http::Code>(0)\\n1055:                                          ? response->status_code\\n1056:                                          : config_->statusOnError();\\n1057:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\\n1058:           trace, \\\"ext_authz filter rejected the request with an error. Response status code: {}\\\",\\n1059:           *decoder_callbacks_, enumToInt(status_code));\\n1060:       decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(\\n1061:           StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::UnauthorizedExternalService);\\n1062: \\n1063:       decoder_callbacks_->sendLocalReply(\\n1064:           status_code, response->body,\\n1065:           [&headers_to_set = response->headers_to_set,\\n1066:            &headers_to_append = response->headers_to_append,\\n1067:            this](Http::HeaderMap& response_headers) -> void {\\n1068:             addErrorResponseHeaders(response_headers, headers_to_set, headers_to_append);\\n1069:           },\\n1070:           absl::nullopt, Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponseCodeDetails::get().AuthzError);\\n1071:     }\\n1072:     break;\\n1073:   }\\n1074:   }\\n1075: }\\n1076: \\n1077: void Filter::responseHeaderLimitsReached() {\\n1078:   const Http::Code status = Http::Code::InternalServerError;\\n1079:   ENVOY_LOG_EVERY_POW_2(warn,\\n1080:                         \\\"ext_authz filter couldn't add all response header mutations. \\\"\\n1081:                         \\\"Sending local reply with response status code: {}\\\",\\n1082:                         enumToInt(status));\\n1083:   stats_.response_header_limits_reached_.inc();\\n1084:   encoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(\\n1085:       StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::UnauthorizedExternalService);\\n1086:   encoder_callbacks_->sendLocalReply(\\n1087:       status, EMPTY_STRING, nullptr, absl::nullopt,\\n1088:       Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponseCodeDetails::get().AuthzInvalid);\\n1089: }\\n1090: \\n1091: void Filter::rejectResponse() {\\n1092:   const Http::Code status = Http::Code::InternalServerError;\\n1093:   ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"ext_authz filter invalidated the response. Response status code: {}\\\",\\n1094:                    *decoder_callbacks_, enumToInt(status));\\n1095:   if (cluster_) {\\n1096:     config_->incCounter(cluster_->statsScope(), config_->ext_authz_invalid_);\\n1097:   }\\n1098:   stats_.invalid_.inc();\\n1099:   decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(\\n1100:       StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::UnauthorizedExternalService);\\n1101:   decoder_callbacks_->sendLocalReply(\\n1102:       status, EMPTY_STRING, nullptr, absl::nullopt,\\n1103:       Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponseCodeDetails::get().AuthzInvalid);\\n1104: }\\n1105: \\n1106: bool Filter::isBufferFull(uint64_t num_bytes_processing) const {\\n1107:   if (!allow_partial_message_) {\\n1108:     return false;\\n1109:   }\\n1110: \\n1111:   uint64_t num_bytes_buffered = num_bytes_processing;\\n1112:   const auto* buffer = decoder_callbacks_->decodingBuffer();\\n1113:   if (buffer != nullptr) {\\n1114:     num_bytes_buffered += buffer->length();\\n1115:   }\\n1116: \\n1117:   return num_bytes_buffered >= max_request_bytes_;\\n1118: }\\n1119: \\n1120: void Filter::continueDecoding() {\\n1121:   // After sending the check request, we don't need to buffer the data anymore.\\n1122:   buffer_data_ = false;\\n1123: \\n1124:   filter_return_ = FilterReturn::ContinueDecoding;\\n1125:   if (!initiating_call_) {\\n1126:     decoder_callbacks_->continueDecoding();\\n1127:   }\\n1128: }\\n1129: \\n1130: Filter::PerRouteFlags Filter::getPerRouteFlags(const Router::RouteConstSharedPtr& route) const {\\n1131:   if (route == nullptr) {\\n1132:     return PerRouteFlags{true /*skip_check_*/, defaultCheckSettings()};\\n1133:   }\\n1134: \\n1135:   const auto* specific_check_settings =\\n1136:       Http::Utility::resolveMostSpecificPerFilterConfig<FilterConfigPerRoute>(decoder_callbacks_);\\n1137:   if (specific_check_settings != nullptr) {\\n1138:     return PerRouteFlags{specific_check_settings->disabled(),\\n1139:                          specific_check_settings->checkSettings()};\\n1140:   }\\n1141: \\n1142:   return PerRouteFlags{false /*skip_check_*/, defaultCheckSettings()};\\n1143: }\\n1144: \\n1145: bool Filter::validateAndClearInvalidErrorResponseAttributes(\\n1146:     Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponsePtr& response) {\\n1147:   if (!config_->validateMutations()) {\\n1148:     return true;\\n1149:   }\\n1150: \\n1151:   // Validate headers_to_set.\\n1152:   for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_set) {\\n1153:     if (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\\n1154:         !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value)) {\\n1155:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Rejected invalid error header '{}':'{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key,\\n1156:                        value);\\n1157:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(info,\\n1158:                        \\\"Custom error response from ext_authz will be ignored due to invalid \\\"\\n1159:                        \\\"header. Falling back to generic error response.\\\",\\n1160:                        *decoder_callbacks_);\\n1161:       // Fall back to generic error by clearing all custom attributes.\\n1162:       response->headers_to_set.clear();\\n1163:       response->headers_to_append.clear();\\n1164:       response->body.clear();\\n1165:       response->status_code = static_cast<Http::Code>(0); // Clear custom status.\\n1166:       return false;\\n1167:     }\\n1168:   }\\n1169: \\n1170:   // Validate headers_to_append.\\n1171:   for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_append) {\\n1172:     if (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\\n1173:         !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value)) {\\n1174:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"Rejected invalid error header '{}':'{}'.\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key,\\n1175:                        value);\\n1176:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(info,\\n1177:                        \\\"Custom error response from ext_authz will be ignored due to invalid \\\"\\n1178:                        \\\"header. Falling back to generic error response.\\\",\\n1179:                        *decoder_callbacks_);\\n1180:       // Fall back to generic error by clearing all custom attributes.\\n1181:       response->headers_to_set.clear();\\n1182:       response->headers_to_append.clear();\\n1183:       response->body.clear();\\n1184:       response->status_code = static_cast<Http::Code>(0); // Clear custom status.\\n1185:       return false;\\n1186:     }\\n1187:   }\\n1188: \\n1189:   return true;\\n1190: }\\n1191: \\n1192: bool Filter::canAddResponseHeader(Http::HeaderMap& response_headers) {\\n1193:   if (config_->enforceResponseHeaderLimits() &&\\n1194:       response_headers.size() >= response_headers.maxHeadersCount()) {\\n1195:     stats_.omitted_response_headers_.inc();\\n1196:     ENVOY_LOG_EVERY_POW_2(warn, \\\"Some ext_authz error response headers weren't added because the \\\"\\n1197:                                 \\\"header map was full.\\\");\\n1198:     return false;\\n1199:   }\\n1200:   return true;\\n1201: }\\n1202: \\n1203: void Filter::addErrorResponseHeaders(\\n1204:     Http::HeaderMap& response_headers,\\n1205:     const std::vector<std::pair<std::string, std::string>>& headers_to_set,\\n1206:     const std::vector<std::pair<std::string, std::string>>& headers_to_append) {\\n1207:   ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\\n1208:                    \\\"ext_authz filter added header(s) to the error response:\\\", *decoder_callbacks_);\\n1209: \\n1210:   // First, handle headers_to_set which should override existing headers.\\n1211:   for (const auto& [key, _] : headers_to_set) {\\n1212:     response_headers.remove(Http::LowerCaseString(key));\\n1213:   }\\n1214:   for (const auto& [key, value] : headers_to_set) {\\n1215:     if (!canAddResponseHeader(response_headers)) {\\n1216:       break;\\n1217:     }\\n1218:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\" '{}':'{}'\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\\n1219:     response_headers.addCopy(Http::LowerCaseString(key), value);\\n1220:   }\\n1221: \\n1222:   // Then, handle headers_to_append which should append to existing headers.\\n1223:   for (const auto& [key, value] : headers_to_append) {\\n1224:     if (!canAddResponseHeader(response_headers)) {\\n1225:       break;\\n1226:     }\\n1227:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\" '{}':'{}'\\\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\\n1228:     response_headers.addCopy(Http::LowerCaseString(key), value);\\n1229:   }\\n1230: }\\n1231: \\n1232: } // namespace ExtAuthz\\n1233: } // namespace HttpFilters\\n1234: } // namespace Extensions\\n1235: } // namespace Envoy\\n1236:
