HeaderUtility::matchHeaders(const HeaderMap& request_headers,\\n39:                                  const std::vector<HeaderDataPtr>& config_headers) {\\n40:   // No headers to match is considered a match.\\n41:   if (!config_headers.empty()) {\\n42:     for (const HeaderDataPtr& cfg_header_data : config_headers) {\\n43:       if (!cfg_header_data->matchesHeaders(request_headers)) {\\n44:         return false;\\n45:       }\\n46:     }\\n47:   }\\n48: \\n49:   return true;\\n50: }\\n51: \\n52: HeaderUtility::GetAllOfHeaderAsStringResult\\n53: HeaderUtility::getAllOfHeaderAsString(const HeaderMap::GetResult& header_value,\\n54:                                       absl::string_view separator) {\\n55:   GetAllOfHeaderAsStringResult result;\\n56:   // In this case we concatenate all found headers using a delimiter before performing the\\n57:   // final match. We use an InlinedVector of absl::string_view to invoke the optimized join\\n58:   // algorithm. This requires a copying phase before we invoke join. The 3 used as the inline\\n59:   // size has been arbitrarily chosen.\\n60:   // TODO(mattklein123): Do we need to normalize any whitespace here?\\n61:   absl::InlinedVector<absl::string_view, 3> string_view_vector;\\n62:   string_view_vector.reserve(header_value.size());\\n63:   for (size_t i = 0; i < header_value.size(); i++) {\\n64:     string_view_vector.push_back(header_value[i]->value().getStringView());\\n65:   }\\n66:   result.result_backing_string_ = absl::StrJoin(string_view_vector, separator);\\n67: \\n68:   return result;\\n69: }\\n70: \\n71: HeaderUtility::GetAllOfHeaderAsStringResult\\n72: HeaderUtility::getAllOfHeaderAsString(const HeaderMap& headers, const Http::LowerCaseString& key,\\n73:                                       absl::string_view separator) {\\n74:   GetAllOfHeaderAsStringResult result;\\n75:   const auto header_value = headers.get(key);\\n76: \\n77:   if (header_value.empty()) {\\n78:     // Empty for clarity. Avoid handling the empty case in the block below if the runtime feature\\n79:     // is disabled.\\n80:   } else if (header_value.size() == 1) {\\n81:     result.result_ = header_value[0]->value().getStringView();\\n82:   } else {\\n83:     return getAllOfHeaderAsString(header_value, separator);\\n84:   }\\n85: \\n86:   return result;\\n87: }\\n88: \\n89: HeaderUtility::HeaderDataPtr\\n90: HeaderUtility::createHeaderData(const envoy::config::route::v3::HeaderMatcher& config,\\n91:                                 Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& factory_context) {\\n92:   switch (config.header_match_specifier_case()) {\\n93:   case envoy::config::route::v3::HeaderMatcher::HeaderMatchSpecifierCase::kExactMatch:\\n94:     return std::make_unique<HeaderDataExactMatch>(config);\\n95:     break;\\n96:   case envoy::config::route::v3::HeaderMatcher::HeaderMatchSpecifierCase::kSafeRegexMatch:\\n97:     return THROW_OR_RETURN_VALUE(HeaderDataRegexMatch::create(config, factory_context),\\n98:                                  std::unique_ptr<HeaderDataRegexMatch>);\\n99:     break;\\n100:   case envoy::config::route::v3::HeaderMatcher::HeaderMatchSpecifierCase::kRangeMatch:\\n101:     return std::make_unique<HeaderDataRangeMatch>(config);\\n102:     break;\\n103:   case envoy::config::route::v3::HeaderMatcher::HeaderMatchSpecifierCase::kPresentMatch:\\n104:     return std::make_unique<HeaderDataPresentMatch>(config);\\n105:     break;\\n106:   case envoy::config::route::v3::HeaderMatcher::HeaderMatchSpecifierCase::kPrefixMatch:\\n107:     return std::make_unique<HeaderDataPrefixMatch>(config);\\n108:     break;\\n109:   case envoy::config::route::v3::HeaderMatcher::HeaderMatchSpecifierCase::kSuffixMatch:\\n110:     return std::make_unique<HeaderDataSuffixMatch>(config);\\n111:     break;\\n112:   case envoy::config::route::v3::HeaderMatcher::HeaderMatchSpecifierCase::kContainsMatch:\\n113:     return std::make_unique<HeaderDataContainsMatch>(config);\\n114:     break;\\n115:   case envoy::config::route::v3::HeaderMatcher::HeaderMatchSpecifierCase::kStringMatch:\\n116:     return std::make_unique<HeaderDataStringMatch>(config, factory_context);\\n117:     break;\\n118:   case envoy::config::route::v3::HeaderMatcher::HeaderMatchSpecifierCase::\\n119:       HEADER_MATCH_SPECIFIER_NOT_SET:\\n120:     FALLTHRU;\\n121:   default:\\n122:     return std::make_unique<HeaderDataPresentMatch>(config, true);\\n123:     break;\\n124:   }\\n125: }\\n126: \\n127: bool HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(const absl::string_view header_value) {\\n128:   return http2::adapter::HeaderValidator::IsValidHeaderValue(header_value,\\n129:                                                              http2::adapter::ObsTextOption::kAllow);\\n130: }\\n131: \\n132: bool HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(absl::string_view header_key) {\\n133:   if (!header_key.empty() && header_key[0] == ':') {\\n134:     header_key.remove_prefix(1);\\n135:     if (header_key.empty()) {\\n136:       return false;\\n137:     }\\n138:   }\\n139:   // For all other header use HTTP/1 semantics. The only difference from HTTP/2 is that\\n140:   // uppercase characters are allowed. This allows HTTP filters to add header with mixed\\n141:   // case names. The HTTP/1 codec will send as is, as uppercase characters are allowed.\\n142:   // However the HTTP/2 codec will NOT convert these to lowercase when serializing the\\n143:   // header map, thus producing an invalid request.\\n144:   // TODO(yanavlasov): make validation in HTTP/2 case stricter.\\n145:   bool is_valid = true;\\n146:   for (auto iter = header_key.begin(); iter != header_key.end() && is_valid; ++iter) {\\n147:     is_valid &= testCharInTable(kGenericHeaderNameCharTable, *iter);\\n148:   }\\n149:   return is_valid;\\n150: }\\n151: \\n152: bool HeaderUtility::headerNameContainsUnderscore(const absl::string_view header_name) {\\n153:   return header_name.find('_') != absl::string_view::npos;\\n154: }\\n155: \\n156: bool HeaderUtility::authorityIsValid(const absl::string_view header_value) {\\n157:   // This function validates the authority header for both HTTP/1 and HTTP/2.\\n158:   // Note the HTTP/1 spec allows \\\"user-info@host:port\\\" for the authority, whereas\\n159:   // the HTTP/2 spec only allows \\\"host:port\\\". Thus, this function permits all the\\n160:   // HTTP/2 valid characters (similar to oghttp2's implementation) and the \\\"@\\\" character.\\n161:   // Once UHV is used, this function should be removed, and the HTTP/1 and HTTP/2\\n162:   // authority validations should be different.\\n163:   static constexpr char ValidAuthorityChars[] = {\\n164:       0 /* NUL  */, 0 /* SOH  */, 0 /* STX  */, 0 /* ETX  */,\\n165:       0 /* EOT  */, 0 /* ENQ  */, 0 /* ACK  */, 0 /* BEL  */,\\n166:       0 /* BS   */, 0 /* HT   */, 0 /* LF   */, 0 /* VT   */,\\n167:       0 /* FF   */, 0 /* CR   */, 0 /* SO   */, 0 /* SI   */,\\n168:       0 /* DLE  */, 0 /* DC1  */, 0 /* DC2  */, 0 /* DC3  */,\\n169:       0 /* DC4  */, 0 /* NAK  */, 0 /* SYN  */, 0 /* ETB  */,\\n170:       0 /* CAN  */, 0 /* EM   */, 0 /* SUB  */, 0 /* ESC  */,\\n171:       0 /* FS   */, 0 /* GS   */, 0 /* RS   */, 0 /* US   */,\\n172:       0 /* SPC  */, 1 /* !    */, 0 /* \\\"    */, 0 /* #    */,\\n173:       1 /* $    */, 1 /* %    */, 1 /* &    */, 1 /* '    */,\\n174:       1 /* (    */, 1 /* )    */, 1 /* *    */, 1 /* +    */,\\n175:       1 /* ,    */, 1 /* -    */, 1 /* . */,    0 /* /    */,\\n176:       1 /* 0 /* <    */, 1 /* =    */, 0 /* >    */, 0 /* ?    */,\\n180:       1 /* @    */, 1 /* A    */, 1 /* B    */, 1 /* C    */,\\n181:       1 /* D    */, 1 /* E    */, 1 /* F    */, 1 /* G    */,\\n182: HeaderUtility::isSpecial1xx(const ResponseHeaderMap& response_headers) {\\n239:   return response_headers.Status()->value() == \\\"100\\\" ||\\n240:          response_headers.Status()->value() == \\\"102\\\" ||\\n241:          response_headers.Status()->value() == \\\"103\\\" || response_headers.Status()->value() == \\\"104\\\";\\n242: }\\n243: \\n244: bool HeaderUtility::isConnect(const RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\\n245:   return headers.Method() && headers.Method()->value() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Connect;\\n246: }\\n247: \\n248: bool HeaderUtility::isConnectUdpRequest(const RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\\n249:   return headers.Upgrade() && absl::EqualsIgnoreCase(headers.getUpgradeValue(),\\n250:                                                      Http::Headers::get().UpgradeValues.ConnectUdp);\\n251: }\\n252: \\n253: bool HeaderUtility::isConnectUdpResponse(const ResponseHeaderMap& headers) {\\n254:   // In connect-udp case, Envoy will transform the H2 headers to H1 upgrade headers.\\n255:   // A valid response should have SwitchingProtocol status and connect-udp upgrade.\\n256:   return headers.Upgrade() && Utility::getResponseStatus(headers) == 101 &&\\n257:          absl::EqualsIgnoreCase(headers.getUpgradeValue(),\\n258:                                 Http::Headers::get().UpgradeValues.ConnectUdp);\\n259: }\\n260: \\n261: bool HeaderUtility::isConnectResponse(const RequestHeaderMap* request_headers,\\n262:                                       const ResponseHeaderMap& response_headers) {\\n263:   return request_headers && isConnect(*request_headers) &&\\n264:          static_cast<Http::Code>(Http::Utility::getResponseStatus(response_headers)) ==\\n265:              Http::Code::OK;\\n266: }\\n267: \\n268: bool HeaderUtility::rewriteAuthorityForConnectUdp(RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\\n269:   // Per RFC 9298, the URI template must only contain ASCII characters in the range 0x21-0x7E.\\n270:   absl::string_view path = headers.getPathValue();\\n271:   for (char c : path) {\\n272:     unsigned char ascii_code = static_cast<unsigned char>(c);\\n273:     if (ascii_code < 0x21 || ascii_code > 0x7e) {\\n274:       ENVOY_LOG_MISC(warn, \\\"CONNECT-UDP request with a bad character in the path {}\\\", path);\\n275:       return false;\\n276:     }\\n277:   }\\n278: \\n279:   // Extract target host and port from path using default template.\\n280:   if (!absl::StartsWith(path, \\\"/.well-known/masque/udp/\\\")) {\\n281:     ENVOY_LOG_MISC(warn, \\\"CONNECT-UDP request path is not a well-known URI: {}\\\", path);\\n282:     return false;\\n283:   }\\n284: \\n285:   std::vector<absl::string_view> path_split = absl::StrSplit(path, '/');\\n286:   if (path_split.size() != 7 || path_split[4].empty() || path_split[5].empty() ||\\n287:       !path_split[6].empty()) {\\n288:     ENVOY_LOG_MISC(warn, \\\"CONNECT-UDP request with a malformed URI template in the path {}\\\", path);\\n289:     return false;\\n290:   }\\n291: \\n292:   // Utility::PercentEncoding::decode never returns an empty string if the input argument is not\\n293:   // empty.\\n294:   std::string target_host = Utility::PercentEncoding::decode(path_split[4]);\\n295:   // Per RFC 9298, IPv6 Zone ID is not supported.\\n296:   if (target_host.find('%') != std::string::npos) {\\n297:     ENVOY_LOG_MISC(warn, \\\"CONNECT-UDP request with a non-escpaed char (%) in the path {}\\\", path);\\n298:     return false;\\n299:   }\\n300:   std::string target_port = Utility::PercentEncoding::decode(path_split[5]);\\n301: \\n302:   // If the host is an IPv6 address, surround the address with square brackets.\\n303:   in6_addr sin6_addr;\\n304:   bool is_ipv6 = (inet_pton(AF_INET6, target_host.c_str(), &sin6_addr) == 1);\\n305:   std::string new_host =\\n306:       absl::StrCat((is_ipv6 ? absl::StrCat(\\\"[\\\", target_host, \\\"]\\\") : target_host), \\\":\\\", target_port);\\n307:   headers.setHost(new_host);\\n308: \\n309:   return true;\\n310: }\\n311: \\n312: #ifdef ENVOY_ENABLE_HTTP_DATAGRAMS\\n313: bool HeaderUtility::isCapsuleProtocol(const RequestOrResponseHeaderMap& headers) {\\n314:   Http::HeaderMap::GetResult capsule_protocol =\\n315:       headers.get(Envoy::Http::LowerCaseString(\\\"Capsule-Protocol\\\"));\\n316:   // When there are multiple Capsule-Protocol header entries, it returns false. RFC 9297 specifies\\n317:   // that non-boolean value types must be ignored. If there are multiple header entries, the value\\n318:   // type becomes a List so the header field must be ignored.\\n319:   if (capsule_protocol.size() != 1) {\\n320:     return false;\\n321:   }\\n322:   // Parses the header value and extracts the boolean value ignoring parameters.\\n323:   absl::optional<quiche::structured_headers::ParameterizedItem> header_item =\\n324:       quiche::structured_headers::ParseItem(capsule_protocol[0]->value().getStringView());\\n325:   return header_item && header_item->item.is_boolean() && header_item->item.GetBoolean();\\n326: }\\n327: #endif\\n328: \\n329: bool HeaderUtility::requestShouldHaveNoBody(const RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\\n330:   return (headers.Method() &&\\n331:           (headers.Method()->value() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Get ||\\n332:            headers.Method()->value() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Head ||\\n333:            headers.Method()->value() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Delete ||\\n334:            headers.Method()->value() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Trace ||\\n335:            headers.Method()->value() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Connect));\\n336: }\\n337: \\n338: bool HeaderUtility::isEnvoyInternalRequest(const RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\\n339:   const HeaderEntry* internal_request_header = headers.EnvoyInternalRequest();\\n340:   return internal_request_header != nullptr &&\\n341:          internal_request_header->value() == Headers::get().EnvoyInternalRequestValues.True;\\n342: }\\n343: \\n344: void HeaderUtility::stripTrailingHostDot(RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\\n345:   auto host = headers.getHostValue();\\n346:   // If the host ends in a period, remove it.\\n347:   auto dot_index = host.rfind('.');\\n348:   if (dot_index == std::string::npos) {\\n349:     return;\\n350:   } else if (dot_index == (host.size() - 1)) {\\n351:     host.remove_suffix(1);\\n352:     headers.setHost(host);\\n353:     return;\\n354:   }\\n355:   // If the dot is just before a colon, it must be preceding the port number.\\n356:   // IPv6 addresses may contain colons or dots, but the dot will never directly\\n357:   // precede the colon, so this check should be sufficient to detect a trailing port number.\\n358:   if (host[dot_index + 1] == ':') {\\n359:     headers.setHost(absl::StrCat(host.substr(0, dot_index), host.substr(dot_index + 1)));\\n360:   }\\n361: }\\n362: \\n363: bool HeaderUtility::hostHasPort(absl::string_view original_host) {\\n364:   const absl::string_view::size_type port_start = getPortStart(original_host);\\n365:   const absl::string_view port_str = original_host.substr(port_start + 1);\\n366:   if (port_start == absl::string_view::npos) {\\n367:     return false;\\n368:   }\\n369:   uint32_t port = 0;\\n370:   if (!absl::SimpleAtoi(port_str, &port)) {\\n371:     return false;\\n372:   }\\n373:   return true;\\n374: }\\n375: \\n376: absl::optional<uint32_t> HeaderUtility::stripPortFromHost(RequestHeaderMap& headers,\\n377:                                                           absl::optional<uint32_t> listener_port) {\\n378:   const absl::string_view original_host = headers.getHostValue();\\n379:   const absl::string_view::size_type port_start = getPortStart(original_host);\\n380:   if (port_start == absl::string_view::npos) {\\n381:     return absl::nullopt;\\n382:   }\\n383:   const absl::string_view port_str = original_host.substr(port_start + 1);\\n384:   uint32_t port = 0;\\n385:   if (!absl::SimpleAtoi(port_str, &port)) {\\n386:     return absl::nullopt;\\n387:   }\\n388:   if (listener_port.has_value() && port != listener_port) {\\n389:     // We would strip ports only if it is specified and they are the same, as local port of the\\n390:     // listener.\\n391:     return absl::nullopt;\\n392:   }\\n393:   const absl::string_view host = original_host.substr(0, port_start);\\n394:   headers.setHost(host);\\n395:   return port;\\n396: }\\n397: \\n398: void HeaderUtility::stripPortFromHost(std::string& host) {\\n399:   const absl::string_view::size_type port_start = getPortStart(host);\\n400:   if (port_start == absl::string_view::npos) {\\n401:     return;\\n402:   }\\n403:   host = host.substr(0, port_start);\\n404: }\\n405: \\n406: absl::string_view::size_type HeaderUtility::getPortStart(absl::string_view host) {\\n407:   const absl::string_view::size_type port_start = host.rfind(':');\\n408:   if (port_start == absl::string_view::npos) {\\n409:     return absl::string_view::npos;\\n410:   }\\n411:   // According to RFC3986 v6 address is always enclosed in \\\"[]\\\". section 3.2.2.\\n412:   const auto v6_end_index = host.rfind(']');\\n413:   if (v6_end_index == absl::string_view::npos || v6_end_index < port_start) {\\n414:     if ((port_start + 1) > host.size()) {\\n415:       return absl::string_view::npos;\\n416:     }\\n417:     return port_start;\\n418:   }\\n419:   return absl::string_view::npos;\\n420: }\\n421: \\n422: constexpr bool isInvalidToken(unsigned char c) {\\n423:   if (c == '!' || c == '|' || c == '~' || c == '*' || c == '+' || c == '-' || c == '.' ||\\n424:       // #, $, %, &, '\\n425:       (c >= '#' && c <= '\\\\'') ||\\n426:       // [0-9]\\n427:       (c >= '0' && c <= '9') ||\\n428:       // [A-Z]\\n429:       (c >= 'A' && c <= 'Z') ||\\n430:       // ^, _, `, [a-z]\\n431:       (c >= '^' && c <= 'z')) {\\n432:     return false;\\n433:   }\\n434:   return true;\\n435: }\\n436: \\n437: absl::optional<std::reference_wrapper<const absl::string_view>>\\n438: HeaderUtility::requestHeadersValid(const RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\\n439:   // Make sure the host is valid.\\n440:   if (headers.Host() && !HeaderUtility::authorityIsValid(headers.Host()->value().getStringView())) {\\n441:     return SharedResponseCodeDetails::get().InvalidAuthority;\\n442:   }\\n443:   if (headers.Method()) {\\n444:     absl::string_view method = headers.Method()->value().getStringView();\\n445:     if (method.empty() || std::any_of(method.begin(), method.end(), isInvalidToken)) {\\n446:       return SharedResponseCodeDetails::get().InvalidMethod;\\n447:     }\\n448:   }\\n449:   if (headers.Scheme() && absl::StrContains(headers.Scheme()->value().getStringView(), \\\",\\\")) {\\n450:     return SharedResponseCodeDetails::get().InvalidScheme;\\n451:   }\\n452:   return absl::nullopt;\\n453: }\\n454: \\n455: bool HeaderUtility::shouldCloseConnection(Http::Protocol protocol,\\n456:                                           const RequestOrResponseHeaderMap& headers) {\\n457:   // HTTP/1.0 defaults to single-use connections. Make sure the connection will be closed unless\\n458:   // Keep-Alive is present.\\n459:   if (protocol == Protocol::Http10 &&\\n460:       (!headers.Connection() ||\\n461:        !Envoy::StringUtil::caseFindToken(headers.Connection()->value().getStringView(), \\\",\\\",\\n462:                                          Http::Headers::get().ConnectionValues.KeepAlive))) {\\n463:     return true;\\n464:   }\\n465: \\n466:   if (protocol == Protocol::Http11 && headers.Connection() &&\\n467:       Envoy::StringUtil::caseFindToken(headers.Connection()->value().getStringView(), \\\",\\\",\\n468:                                        Http::Headers::get().ConnectionValues.Close)) {\\n469:     return true;\\n470:   }\\n471: \\n472:   // Note: Proxy-Connection is not a standard header, but is supported here\\n473:   // since it is supported by http-parser the underlying parser for http\\n474:   // requests.\\n475:   if (protocol < Protocol::Http2 && headers.ProxyConnection() &&\\n476:       Envoy::StringUtil::caseFindToken(headers.ProxyConnection()->value().getStringView(), \\\",\\\",\\n477:                                        Http::Headers::get().ConnectionValues.Close)) {\\n478:     return true;\\n479:   }\\n480:   return false;\\n481: }\\n482: \\n483: Http::Status HeaderUtility::checkRequiredRequestHeaders(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\\n484:   if (!headers.Method()) {\\n485:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\\n486:         absl::StrCat(\\\"missing required header: \\\", Envoy::Http::Headers::get().Method.get()));\\n487:   }\\n488:   bool is_connect = Http::HeaderUtility::isConnect(headers);\\n489:   if (is_connect) {\\n490:     if (!headers.Host()) {\\n491:       // Host header must be present for CONNECT request.\\n492:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\\n493:           absl::StrCat(\\\"missing required header: \\\", Envoy::Http::Headers::get().Host.get()));\\n494:     }\\n495:     if (headers.Path() && !headers.Protocol()) {\\n496:       // Path and Protocol header should only be present for CONNECT for upgrade style CONNECT.\\n497:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\\n498:           absl::StrCat(\\\"missing required header: \\\", Envoy::Http::Headers::get().Protocol.get()));\\n499:     }\\n500:     if (!headers.Path() && headers.Protocol()) {\\n501:       // Path and Protocol header should only be present for CONNECT for upgrade style CONNECT.\\n502:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\\n503:           absl::StrCat(\\\"missing required header: \\\", Envoy::Http::Headers::get().Path.get()));\\n504:     }\\n505:   } else {\\n506:     if (!headers.Path()) {\\n507:       // :path header must be present for non-CONNECT requests.\\n508:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\\n509:           absl::StrCat(\\\"missing required header: \\\", Envoy::Http::Headers::get().Path.get()));\\n510:     }\\n511:   }\\n512:   return Http::okStatus();\\n513: }\\n514: \\n515: Http::Status HeaderUtility::checkValidRequestHeaders(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\\n516:   if (!Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\\\"envoy.reloadable_features.validate_upstream_headers\\\")) {\\n517:     return Http::okStatus();\\n518:   }\\n519: \\n520:   const HeaderEntry* invalid_entry = nullptr;\\n521:   bool invalid_key = false;\\n522:   headers.iterate([&invalid_entry, &invalid_key](const HeaderEntry& header) -> HeaderMap::Iterate {\\n523:     if (!HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(header.key().getStringView())) {\\n524:       invalid_entry = &header;\\n525:       invalid_key = true;\\n526:       return HeaderMap::Iterate::Break;\\n527: HeaderMap::Iterate::Break;\\n533:     }\\n534: \\n535:     return HeaderMap::Iterate::Continue;\\n536:   });\\n537: \\n538:   if (invalid_entry) {\\n539:     // The header key may contain non-printable characters. Escape the key so that the error\\n540:     // details can be safely presented.\\n541:     const absl::string_view key = invalid_entry->key().getStringView();\\n542:     uint64_t extra_length = JsonEscaper::extraSpace(key);\\n543:     const std::string escaped_key = JsonEscaper::escapeString(key, extra_length);\\n544: \\n545:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\\n546:         absl::StrCat(\\\"invalid header \\\", invalid_key ? \\\"name: \\\" : \\\"value for: \\\", escaped_key));\\n547:   }\\n548:   return Http::okStatus();\\n549: }\\n550: \\n551: Http::Status HeaderUtility::checkRequiredResponseHeaders(const Http::ResponseHeaderMap& headers) {\\n552:   const absl::optional<uint64_t> status = Utility::getResponseStatusOrNullopt(headers);\\n553:   if (!status.has_value()) {\\n554:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\\n555:         absl::StrCat(\\\"missing required header: \\\", Envoy::Http::Headers::get().Status.get()));\\n556:   }\\n557: header[0] != ':') &&\\n567:          !absl::EqualsIgnoreCase(header, Headers::get().HostLegacy.get());\\n568: }\\n569: \\n570: HeaderUtility::HeaderValidationResult HeaderUtility::checkHeaderNameForUnderscores(\\n571:     absl::string_view header_name,\\n572:     envoy::config::core::v3::HttpProtocolOptions::HeadersWithUnderscoresAction\\n573:         headers_with_underscores_action,\\n574:     HeaderValidatorStats& stats) {\\n575:   if (headers_with_underscores_action == envoy::config::core::v3::HttpProtocolOptions::ALLOW ||\\n576: stats.incDroppedHeadersWithUnderscores();\\n583:     return HeaderValidationResult::DROP;\\n584:   }\\n585:   ENVOY_LOG_MISC(debug, \\\"Rejecting request due to header name with underscores: {}\\\", header_name);\\n586:   stats.incRequestsRejectedWithUnderscoresInHeaders();\\n587:   return HeaderUtility::HeaderValidationResult::REJECT;\\n588: }\\n589: \\n590: HeaderUtility::HeaderValidationResult\\n591: HeaderUtility::validateContentLength(absl::string_view header_value,\\n592:                                      bool override_stream_error_on_invalid_http_message,\\n593:                                      bool& should_close_connection, size_t& content_length_output) {\\n594:   should_close_connection = false;\\n595:   std::vector<absl::string_view> values = absl::StrSplit(header_value, ',');\\n596:   absl::optional<uint64_t> content_length;\\n597:   for (const absl::string_view& value : values) {\\n598:     uint64_t new_value;\\n599:     if (!absl::SimpleAtoi(value, &new_value) ||\\n600:         !std::all_of(value.begin(), value.end(), absl::ascii_isdigit)) {\\n601:       ENVOY_LOG_MISC(debug, \\\"Content length was either unparseable or negative\\\");\\n602:       should_close_connection = !override_stream_error_on_invalid_http_message;\\n603:       return HeaderValidationResult::REJECT;\\n604:     }\\n605:     if (!content_length.has_value()) {\\n606:       content_length = new_value;\\n607:       continue;\\n608:     }\\n609:     if (new_value != content_length.value()) {\\n610:       ENVOY_LOG_MISC(\\n611:           debug,\\n612:           \\\"Parsed content length {} is inconsistent with previously detected content length {}\\\",\\n613:           new_value, content_length.value());\\n614:       should_close_connection = !override_stream_error_on_invalid_http_message;\\n615:       return HeaderValidationResult::REJECT;\\n616:     }\\n617:   }\\n618:   content_length_output = content_length.value();\\n619:   return HeaderValidationResult::ACCEPT;\\n620: }\\n621: \\n622: std::vector<absl::string_view>\\n623: HeaderUtility::parseCommaDelimitedHeader(absl::string_view header_value) {\\n624:   std::vector<absl::string_view> values;\\n625:   for (absl::string_view s : absl::StrSplit(header_value, ',')) {\\n626:     absl::string_view token = absl::StripAsciiWhitespace(s);\\n627:     if (token.empty()) {\\n628:       continue;\\n629:     }\\n630:     values.emplace_back(token);\\n631:   }\\n632:   return values;\\n633: }\\n634: \\n635: absl::string_view HeaderUtility::getSemicolonDelimitedAttribute(absl::string_view value) {\\n636:   return absl::StripAsciiWhitespace(StringUtil::cropRight(value, \\\";\\\"));\\n637: }\\n638: \\n639: std::string HeaderUtility::addEncodingToAcceptEncoding(absl::string_view accept_encoding_header,\\n640:                                                        absl::string_view encoding) {\\n641:   // Append the content encoding only if it isn't already present in the\\n642:   // accept_encoding header. If it is present with a q-value (\\\"gzip;q=0.3\\\"),\\n643:   // remove the q-value to indicate that the content encoding setting that we\\n644:   // add has max priority (i.e. q-value 1.0).\\n645:   std::vector<absl::string_view> newContentEncodings;\\n646:   std::vector<absl::string_view> contentEncodings =\\n647:       Http::HeaderUtility::parseCommaDelimitedHeader(accept_encoding_header);\\n648:   for (absl::string_view contentEncoding : contentEncodings) {\\n649:     absl::string_view strippedEncoding =\\n650:         Http::HeaderUtility::getSemicolonDelimitedAttribute(contentEncoding);\\n651:     if (strippedEncoding != encoding) {\\n652:       // Add back all content encodings back except for the content encoding that we want to\\n653:       // add. For example, if content encoding is \\\"gzip\\\", this filters out encodings \\\"gzip\\\" and\\n654:       // \\\"gzip;q=0.6\\\".\\n655:       newContentEncodings.push_back(contentEncoding);\\n656:     }\\n657:   }\\n658:   // Finally add a single instance of our content encoding.\\n659:   newContentEncodings.push_back(encoding);\\n660:   return absl::StrJoin(newContentEncodings, \\\",\\\");\\n661: }\\n662: \\n663: bool HeaderUtility::isStandardConnectRequest(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\\n664:   return headers.getMethodValue() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Connect &&\\n665:          headers.getProtocolValue().empty();\\n666: }\\n667: \\n668: bool HeaderUtility::isExtendedH2ConnectRequest(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\\n669:   return headers.getMethodValue() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Connect &&\\n670:          !headers.getProtocolValue().empty();\\n671: }\\n672: \\n673: bool HeaderUtility::isPseudoHeader(absl::string_view header_name) {\\n674:   return !header_name.empty() && header_name[0] == ':';\\n675: }\\n676: \\n677: } // namespace Http\\n678: } // namespace Envoy\\n679: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/header_utility.cc\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1: #include \\\"source/common/http/filter_manager.h\\\"\\n2: \\n3: #include <functional>\\n4: \\n5: #include \\\"envoy/http/header_map.h\\\"\\n6: #include \\\"envoy/matcher/matcher.h\\\"\\n7: \\n8: #include \\\"source/common/common/enum_to_int.h\\\"\\n9: #include \\\"source/common/common/scope_tracked_object_stack.h\\\"\\n10: #include \\\"source/common/common/scope_tracker.h\\\"\\n11: #include \\\"source/common/http/codes.h\\\"\\n12: #include \\\"source/common/http/header_map_impl.h\\\"\\n13: #include \\\"source/common/http/header_utility.h\\\"\\n14: #include \\\"source/common/http/utility.h\\\"\\n15: #include \\\"source/common/runtime/runtime_features.h\\\"\\n16: \\n17: #include \\\"matching/data_impl.h\\\"\\n18: \\n19: namespace Envoy {\\n20: namespace Http {\\n21: \\n22: namespace {\\n23: \\n24: // Shared helper for recording the latest filter used.\\n25: template <class Filters>\\n26: void recordLatestDataFilter(typename Filters::Iterator current_filter,\\n27:                             typename Filters::Element*& latest_filter, Filters& filters) {\\n28:   // If this is the first time we're calling onData, just record the current filter.\\n29:   if (latest_filter == nullptr) {\\n30:     latest_filter = current_filter->get();\\n31:     return;\\n32:   }\\n33: \\n34:   // We want to keep this pointing at the latest filter in the filter list that has received the\\n35:   // onData callback. To do so, we compare the current latest with the *previous* filter. If they\\n36:   // match, then we must be processing a new filter for the first time. We omit this check if we're\\n37:   // the first filter, since the above check handles that case.\\n38:   //\\n39:   // We compare against the previous filter to avoid multiple filter iterations from resetting the\\n40:   // pointer: If we just set latest to current, then the first onData filter iteration would\\n41:   // correctly iterate over the filters and set latest, but on subsequent onData iterations\\n42:   // we'd start from the beginning again, potentially allowing filter N to modify the buffer even\\n43:   // though filter M > N was the filter that inserted data into the buffer.\\n44:   if (current_filter != filters.begin() && latest_filter == std::prev(current_filter)->get()) {\\n45:     latest_filter = current_filter->get();\\n46:   }\\n47: }\\n48: \\n49: void finalizeHeaders(FilterManagerCallbacks& callbacks, StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info,\\n50:                      ResponseHeaderMap& headers) {\\n51:   const Router::RouteConstSharedPtr& route = stream_info.route();\\n52:   if (route != nullptr && route->routeEntry() != nullptr) {\\n53:     const Formatter::Context formatter_context{\\n54:         callbacks.requestHeaders().ptr(), &headers, {}, {}, {}, &callbacks.activeSpan()};\\n55:     route->routeEntry()->finalizeResponseHeaders(headers, formatter_context, stream_info);\\n56:   }\\n57: }\\n58: \\n59: } // namespace\\n60: \\n61: void ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonContinue() {\\n62:   if (!canContinue()) {\\n63:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"cannot continue filter chain: filter={}\\\", *this,\\n64:                      static_cast<const void*>(this));\\n65:     return;\\n66:   }\\n67: \\n68:   // Set ScopeTrackerScopeState if there's no existing crash context.\\n69:   ScopeTrackedObjectStack encapsulated_object;\\n70:   absl::optional<ScopeTrackerScopeState> state;\\n71:   if (parent_.dispatcher_.trackedObjectStackIsEmpty()) {\\n72:     restoreContextOnContinue(encapsulated_object);\\n73:     state.emplace(&encapsulated_object, parent_.dispatcher_);\\n74:   }\\n75: \\n76:   ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"continuing filter chain: filter={}\\\", *this,\\n77:                    static_cast<const void*>(this));\\n78:   ASSERT(!canIterate(),\\n79:          \\\"Attempting to continue iteration while the IterationState is already Continue\\\");\\n80:   // If iteration has stopped for all frame types, set iterate_from_current_filter_ to true so the\\n81:   // filter iteration starts with the current filter instead of the next one.\\n82:   if (stoppedAll()) {\\n83:     iterate_from_current_filter_ = true;\\n84:   }\\n85:   allowIteration();\\n86: \\n87:   // Only resume with do1xxHeaders() if we've actually seen 1xx headers.\\n88:   if (has1xxHeaders()) {\\n89:     continued_1xx_headers_ = true;\\n90:     do1xxHeaders();\\n91:     // If the response headers have not yet come in, don't continue on with\\n92:     // headers and body. doHeaders expects request headers to exist.\\n93:     if (!parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders()) {\\n94:       return;\\n95:     }\\n96:   }\\n97: \\n98:   if (!canContinue()) {\\n99:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"cannot continue filter chain: filter={}\\\", *this,\\n100:                      static_cast<const void*>(this));\\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc%3FL1-100\"}"

Output
"{\"content\":\"937: StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator\\n938: FilterManager::commonEncodePrefix(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter, bool end_stream,\\n939:                                   FilterIterationStartState filter_iteration_start_state) {\\n940:   // Only do base state setting on the initial call. Subsequent calls for filtering do not touch\\n941:   // the base state.\\n942:   ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"commonEncodePrefix end_stream: {}, isHalfCloseEnabled: {}\\\", *this,\\n943:                    end_stream, filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled());\\n944:   if (filter == nullptr) {\\n945:     if (end_stream) {\\n946:       ASSERT(!state_.observed_encode_end_stream_);\\n947:       state_.observed_encode_end_stream_ = true;\\n948: \\n949:       // When half close semantics are disabled, receiving end stream from the upstream causes\\n950:       // decoder filter to stop, as neither filters nor upstream is interested in downstream data.\\n951:       // half close is enabled in case tcp proxying is done with http1 encoder. In this case, we\\n952:       // should not stop decoder filter chain when end_stream is true, as it will cause any data\\n953:       // sent in the upstream direction to be\\n954:       // dropped.\\n955:       if (!filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled()) {\\n956:         state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\\n957:       }\\n958:     }\\n959:     return encoder_filters_.begin();\\n960:   }\\n961: \\n962:   if (filter_iteration_start_state == FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent &&\\n963:       (*(filter->entry()))->iterate_from_current_filter_) {\\n964:     // The filter iteration has been stopped for all frame types, and now the iteration continues.\\n965:     // The current filter's encoding callback has not be called. Call it now.\\n966:     return filter->entry();\\n967:   }\\n968:   return std::next(filter->entry());\\n969: }\\n970: \\n971: StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator\\n972: FilterManager::commonDecodePrefix(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* filter,\\n973:                                   FilterIterationStartState filter_iteration_start_state) {\\n974:   if (!filter) {\\n975:     return decoder_filters_.begin();\\n976:   }\\n977:   if (filter_iteration_start_state == FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent &&\\n978:       (*(filter->entry()))->iterate_from_current_filter_) {\\n979:     // The filter iteration has been stopped for all frame types, and now the iteration continues.\\n980:     // The current filter's callback function has not been called. Call it now.\\n981:     return filter->entry();\\n982:   }\\n983:   return std::next(filter->entry());\\n984: }\\n985: \\n986: void DownstreamFilterManager::onLocalReply(StreamFilterBase::LocalReplyData& data) {\\n987:   state_.under_on_local_reply_ = true;\\n988:   filter_manager_callbacks_.onLocalReply(data.code_);\\n989: \\n990:   for (auto entry : filters_) {\\n991:     if (entry->onLocalReply(data) == LocalErrorStatus::ContinueAndResetStream) {\\n992:       data.reset_imminent_ = true;\\n993:     }\\n994:   }\\n995:   state_.under_on_local_reply_ = false;\\n996: }\\n997: \\n998: void DownstreamFilterManager::sendLocalReply(\\n999:     Code code, absl::string_view body,\\n1000:     const std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)>& modify_headers,\\n1001: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc%3FL937-1000\"}"

Output
"{\"content\":\"1559: \\n1560: void FilterManager::maybeEndDecode(bool terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream) {\\n1561:   if (terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream) {\\n1562:     ASSERT(!state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_);\\n1563:     state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_ = true;\\n1564:     // If the decoder filter chain was aborted (i.e. due to local reply)\\n1565:     // we rely on the encoding of end_stream to close the stream.\\n1566:     if (filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled() && !stopDecoderFilterChain()) {\\n1567:       checkAndCloseStreamIfFullyClosed();\\n1568:     }\\n1569:   }\\n1570: }\\n1571: \\n1572: void FilterManager::checkAndCloseStreamIfFullyClosed() {\\n1573:   ASSERT(filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled());\\n1574:   // When the independent half close is enabled the stream is always closed on error responses\\n1575:   // from the server.\\n1576:   if (filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders().has_value()) {\\n1577:     const uint64_t response_status =\\n1578:         Http::Utility::getResponseStatus(filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders().ref());\\n1579:     const bool error_response =\\n1580:         !(Http::CodeUtility::is2xx(response_status) || Http::CodeUtility::is1xx(response_status));\\n1581:     // Abort the decoder filter if it has not yet been completed.\\n1582:     if (error_response && !state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_) {\\n1583:       state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\\n1584:     }\\n1585:   }\\n1586: \\n1587:   // Handle the case where the end_stream was received from both downstream client and upstream\\n1588:   // server but a decoder filter added either request body or trailers AND paused decoder filter\\n1589:   // chain iteration. This case is currently handled the same way as if independent half-close is\\n1590:   // disabled, i.e. proxying is stopped as soon as encoding has finished.\\n1591:   // TODO(yanavlasov): to support this case the codec needs to notify HCM that it has closed its low\\n1592:   // level stream\\n1593:   //                   to avoid use-after-free when HCM cleans-up its ActiveStream\\n1594:   const bool downstream_client_sent_end_stream = decoderObservedEndStream();\\n1595:   const bool decoder_filter_chain_paused =\\n1596:       !state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_ && !state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_;\\n1597:   if (state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_ && downstream_client_sent_end_stream &&\\n1598:       decoder_filter_chain_paused) {\\n1599:     state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\\n1600:   }\\n1601: \\n1602:   // If independent half close is enabled then close the stream when\\n1603:   // 1. Both encoder and decoder filter chains has completed.\\n1604:   // 2. Encoder filter chain has completed and decoder filter chain was aborted (i.e. local reply).\\n1605:   // There is no need to check for aborted encoder filter chain as the filter will either be\\n1606:   // completed or stream is reset.\\n1607:   if (state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_ &&\\n1608:       (state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_ || state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_)) {\\n1609:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"closing stream\\\", *this);\\n1610:     filter_manager_callbacks_.endStream();\\n1611:   }\\n1612: }\\n1613: \\n1614: bool FilterManager::processNewlyAddedMetadata() {\\n1615:   if (request_metadata_map_vector_ == nullptr) {\\n1616:     return false;\\n1617:   }\\n1618:   for (const auto& metadata_map : *getRequestMetadataMapVector()) {\\n1619:     decodeMetadata(nullptr, *metadata_map);\\n1620:   }\\n1621: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc%3FL1559-1620\"}"

{

  "stdout": "  EXPECT_FALSE(HeaderUtility::matchHeaders(headers, header_data));\n\n  headers.addCopy(\"match-header\", \"a\");\n  // With a single \"match-header\" this regex will match.\n  EXPECT_TRUE(HeaderUtility::matchHeaders(headers, header_data));\n\n  headers.addCopy(\"match-header\", \"b\");\n  // With two \"match-header\" we now logically have \"a,b\" as the value, so the regex will not match.\n  EXPECT_FALSE(HeaderUtility::matchHeaders(headers, header_data));\n\n  header_data[0] = HeaderUtility::createHeaderData(parseHeaderMatcherFromYaml(R\"EOF(\nname: match-header\nstring_match:\n  exact: a,b\n  )EOF\"),\n                                                   context_);\n  // Make sure that an exact match on \"a,b\" does in fact work.\n  EXPECT_TRUE(HeaderUtility::matchHeaders(headers, header_data));\n}\n\nTEST_F(MatchHeadersTest, MustMatchAllHeaderData) {\n  TestRequestHeaderMapImpl matching_headers_1{{\"match-header-A\", \"1\"}, {\"match-header-B\", \"2\"}};\n  TestRequestHeaderMapImpl matching_headers_2{\n      {\"match-header-A\", \"3\"}, {\"match-header-B\", \"4\"}, {\"match-header-C\", \"5\"}};\n  TestRequestHeaderMapImpl unmatching_headers_1{{\"match-header-A\", \"6\"}};\n  TestRequestHeaderMapImpl unmatching_headers_2{{\"match-header-B\", \"7\"}};\n  TestRequestHeaderMapImpl unmatching_headers_3{{\"match-header-A\", \"8\"}, {\"match-header-C\", \"9\"}};\n  TestRequestHeaderMapImpl unmatching_headers_4{{\"match-header-C\", \"10\"}, {\"match-header-D\", \"11\"}};\n\n  const std::string yamlA = R\"EOF(\nname: match-header-A\n  )EOF\";\n\n  const std::string yamlB = R\"EOF(\nname: match-header-B\n  )EOF\";\n\n  std::vector<HeaderUtility::HeaderDataPtr> header_data;\n--\n  EXPECT_TRUE(HeaderUtility::matchHeaders(matching_headers_1, header_data));\n  EXPECT_TRUE(HeaderUtility::matchHeaders(matching_headers_2, header_data));\n  EXPECT_FALSE(HeaderUtility::matchHeaders(unmatching_headers_1, header_data));\n  EXPECT_FALSE(HeaderUtility::matchHeaders(unmatching_headers_2, header_data));\n  EXPECT_FALSE(HeaderUtility::matchHeaders(unmatching_headers_3, header_data));\n  EXPECT_FALSE(HeaderUtility::matchHeaders(unmatching_headers_4, header_data));\n}\n\nTEST_F(MatchHeadersTest, HeaderPresence) {\n  TestRequestHeaderMapImpl matching_headers{{\"match-header\", \"value\"}};\n  TestRequestHeaderMapImpl unmatching_headers{{\"other-header\", \"value\"}};\n  const std::string yaml = R\"EOF(\nname: match-header\n  )EOF\";\n\n  std::vector<HeaderUtility::HeaderDataPtr> header_data;\n  header_data.push_back(\n      HeaderUtility::createHeaderData(parseHeaderMatcherFromYaml(yaml), context_));\n  EXPECT_TRUE(HeaderUtility::matchHeaders(matching_headers, header_data));\n  EXPECT_FALSE(HeaderUtility::matchHeaders(unmatching_headers, header_data));\n}\n\nTEST_F(MatchHeadersTest, HeaderPresenceTreatMissingAsEmpty) {\n  TestRequestHeaderMapImpl matching_headers{{\"match-header\", \"value\"}};\n  TestRequestHeaderMapImpl unmatching_headers{{\"other-header\", \"value\"}};\n  TestRequestHeaderMapImpl empty_headers{{}};\n  const std::string yaml = R\"EOF(\nname: match-header\ntreat_missing_header_as_empty: true\n  )EOF\";\n\n  std::vector<HeaderUtility::HeaderDataPtr> header_data;\n  header_data.push_back(\n      HeaderUtility::createHeaderData(parseHeaderMatcherFromYaml(yaml), context_));\n  EXPECT_TRUE(HeaderUtility::matchHeaders(matching_headers, header_data));\n  EXPECT_TRUE(HeaderUtility::matchHeaders(unmatching_headers, header_data));\n  EXPECT_TRUE(HeaderUtility::matchHeaders(empty_headers, header_data));\n}\n\nTEST_F(MatchHeadersTest, DEPRECATED_FEATURE_TEST(HeaderExactMatch)) {\n  TestRequestHeaderMapImpl matching_headers{{\"match-header\", \"match-value\"}};"
}

setResponseFlag(StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::DelayInjected))\n      .Times(0);\n\n  // Abort related calls\n  EXPECT_CALL(\n      runtime_.snapshot_,\n      featureEnabled(\"fault.http.abort.abort_percent\",\n                     testing::Matcher<const envoy::type::v3::FractionalPercent&>(Percent(100))))\n      .WillOnce(Return(true));\n\n  EXPECT_CALL(runtime_.snapshot_, getInteger(\"fault.http.abort.http_status\", 429))\n      .WillOnce(Return(429));\n\n  Http::TestResponseHeaderMapImpl response_headers{\n      {\":status\", \"429\"}, {\"content-length\", \"18\"}, {\"content-type\", \"text/plain\"}};\n  EXPECT_CALL(decoder_filter_callbacks_,\n--\n  EXPECT_EQ(1UL, config_->stats().active_faults_.value());\n filter_->onDestroy();\n\n  EXPECT_EQ(0UL, config_->stats().delays_injected_.value());\n  EXPECT_EQ(1UL, config_->stats().aborts_injected_.value());\n  EXPECT_EQ(0UL, config_->stats().active_faults_.value());\n  EXPECT_EQ(\"fault_filter_abort\", decoder_filter_callbacks_.details());\n}\n\nTEST_F(FaultFilterTest, HeaderAbortWithHttpStatus) {\n  setUpTest(header_abort_only_yaml);\n\n  request_headers_.addCopy(\"x-envoy-fault-abort-request\", \"429\");\n\n  envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata dynamic_metadata;\n  envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata expected_metadata;\n  auto& filter_metadata = *expected_metadata.mutable_filter_metadata();\n  (*filter_metadata[\"envoy.filters.http.fault\"].mutable_fields())[\"hello\"].set_string_value(\n      \"world\");\n\n  EXPECT_CALL(runtime_.snapshot_,\n              getInteger(\"fault.http.max_active_faults\", std::numeric_limits<uint64_t>::max()))\n      .WillOnce(Return(std::numeric_limits<uint64_t>::max()));\n\n  EXPECT_CALL(decoder_filter_callbacks_, continueDecoding()).Times(0);\n  EXPECT_CALL(decoder_filter_callbacks_.stream_info_,\n              setResponseFlag(StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::DelayInjected))\n      .Times(0);\n\n  // Abort related calls\n  EXPECT_CALL(\n      runtime_.snapshot_,\n      featureEnabled(\"fault.http.abort.abort_percent\",\n                     testing::Matcher<const envoy::type::v3::FractionalPercent&>(Percent(100))))\n      .WillOnce(Return(true));\n\n  EXPECT_CALL(runtime_.snapshot_, getInteger(\"fault.http.abort.http_status\", 429))\n      .WillOnce(Return(429));\n  EXPECT_CALL(decoder_filter_callbacks_.stream_info_, dynamicMetadata())\n      .WillOnce(ReturnRef(dynamic_metadata));\n\n  Http::TestResponseHeaderMapImpl response_headers{\n--\n  EXPECT_EQ(1UL, config_->stats().active_faults_.value());\n  EXPECT_EQ(Http::FilterDataStatus::Continue, filter_->decodeData(data_, false));\n  EXPECT_EQ(Http::FilterTrailersStatus::Continue, filter_->decodeTrailers(request_trailers_));\n  filter_->onDestroy();\n\n  EXPECT_EQ(0UL, config_->stats().delays_injected_.value());\n  EXPECT_EQ(1UL, config_->stats().aborts_injected_.value());\n  EXPECT_EQ(0UL, config_->stats().active_faults_.value());\n  EXPECT_EQ(\"fault_filter_abort\", decoder_filter_callbacks_.details());\n  EXPECT_THAT(dynamic_metadata, ProtoEq(expected_metadata));\n}\n\nTEST_F(FaultFilterTest, AbortWithGrpcStatus) {"

  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "  response.headers_to_add = {{invalid_key_, \"bar\"}};\n  testResponse(response);\n}\n\n// headers_to_set is also used when the authz response has status denied.\nTEST_F(InvalidMutationTest, HeadersToSetKeyDenied) {\n  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::Response response;\n  response.status = Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus::Denied;\n  response.headers_to_set = {{invalid_key_, \"bar\"}};\n  testResponse(response);\n}\n\nTEST_F(InvalidMutationTest, HeadersToAppendKey) {\n  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::Response response;\n  response.status = Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus::OK;\n  response.headers_to_append = {{invalid_key_, \"bar\"}};\n  testResponse(response);\n}\n\nTEST_F(InvalidMutationTest, ResponseHeadersToAddKey) {\n  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::Response response;\n--\n  response.response_headers_to_add_if_absent = {{invalid_key_, \"bar\"}};\n  testResponse(response);\n}\n\nTEST_F(InvalidMutationTest, ResponseHeadersToOverwriteIfExistsKey) {\n  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::Response response;\n  response.status = Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus::OK;\n  response.response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists = {{invalid_key_, \"bar\"}};\n  testResponse(response);\n}\n\nTEST_F(InvalidMutationTest, QueryParametersToSetKey) {\n  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::Response response;\n  response.status = Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus::OK;\n  response.query_parameters_to_set = {{\"f o o\", \"bar\"}};\n  testResponse(response);\n}\n\n// Test that the filter rejects mutations with an invalid value\nTEST_F(InvalidMutationTest, HeadersToSetValueOk) {\n  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::Response response;\n--\n  response.headers_to_add = {{\"foo\", invalid_value_}};\n  testResponse(response);\n}\n\n// headers_to_set is also used when the authz response has status denied.\nTEST_F(InvalidMutationTest, HeadersToSetValueDenied) {\n  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::Response response;\n  response.status = Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus::Denied;\n  response.headers_to_set = {{\"foo\", invalid_value_}};\n  testResponse(response);\n}\n\nTEST_F(InvalidMutationTest, HeadersToAppendValue) {\n  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::Response response;\n  response.status = Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus::OK;\n  response.headers_to_append = {{\"foo\", invalid_value_}};\n  testResponse(response);\n}\n\nTEST_F(InvalidMutationTest, ResponseHeadersToAddValue) {\n  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::Response response;\n--\n  response.response_headers_to_add_if_absent = {{\"foo\", invalid_value_}};\n  testResponse(response);\n}\n\nTEST_F(InvalidMutationTest, ResponseHeadersToOverwriteIfExistsValue) {\n  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::Response response;\n  response.status = Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus::OK;\n  // Add a valid header to see if it gets added to the downstream response.\n  response.response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists = {{\"foo\", invalid_value_}};\n  testResponse(response);\n}\n\nTEST_F(InvalidMutationTest, QueryParametersToSetValue) {\n  Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::Response response;"
}

{\n596:     logging_info_->setBytesSent(bytes_meter->wireBytesSent());\n597:     logging_info_->setBytesReceived(bytes_meter->wireBytesReceived());\n598:   }\n599:   if (stream_info->upstreamInfo().has_value()) {\n600:     logging_info_->setUpstreamHost(stream_info->upstreamInfo()->upstreamHost());\n601:   }\n602:   absl::optional<Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr> cluster_info =\n603:       stream_info->upstreamClusterInfo();\n604:   if (cluster_info) {\n605:     logging_info_->setClusterInfo(std::move(*cluster_info));\n606:   }\n607: }\n608: \n609: void Filter::setEncoderFilterCallbacks(Http::StreamEncoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) {\n610:   encoder_callbacks_ = &callbacks;\n611: }\n612: \n613: void Filter::onDestroy() {\n614:   if (state_ == State::Calling) {\n615:     state_ = State::Complete;\n616:     client_->cancel();\n617:   }\n618: }\n619: \n620: CheckResult Filter::validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\n621:     Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation operation, absl::string_view key,\n622:     absl::string_view value) const {\n623:   if (config_->validateMutations() && // Check header mutation is valid according to configured decoder mutation rules.\n628:   return config_->checkDecoderHeaderMutation(operation, Http::LowerCaseString(key), value);\n629: }\n630: \n631: void Filter::onComplete(Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponsePtr&& response) {\n632:   state_ = State::Complete;\n633:   using Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus;\n634:   Stats::StatName empty_stat_name;\n635: \n636:   updateLoggingInfo(response->grpc_status);\n637: \n638:   if (response->saw_invalid_append_actions) {\n639:     if (config_->validateMutations()) {\n640:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejecting response with invalid header append action.\",\n641:                        *decoder_callbacks_);\n642:       rejectResponse();\n643:       return;\n644:     }\n645:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring response headers with invalid header append action.\",\n646:                      *decoder_callbacks_);\n647:   }\n648: \n649:   if (!response->dynamic_metadata.fields().empty()) {\n650:     if (!config_->enableDynamicMetadataIngestion()) {\n651:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n652:                        \"Response is trying to inject dynamic metadata, but dynamic metadata \"\n653:                        \"ingestion is disabled. Ignoring...\",\n654:                        *decoder_callbacks_);\n655:       stats_.ignored_dynamic_metadata_.inc();\n656:     } else {\n657:       // Add duration of call to dynamic metadata if applicable\n658:       if (start_time_.has_value() && response->status == CheckStatus::OK) {\n659:         Protobuf::Value ext_authz_duration_value;\n660:         auto duration =\n661:             decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime() - start_time_.value();\n662:         ext_authz_duration_value.set_number_value(\n663:             std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(duration).count());\n664:         (*response->dynamic_metadata.mutable_fields())[\"ext_authz_duration\"] =\n665:             ext_authz_duration_value;\n666:       }\n667:       decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setDynamicMetadata(\"envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\",\n668:                                                           response->dynamic_metadata);\n669:     }\n670:   }\n671: \n672:   switch (response->status) {\n673:   case CheckStatus::OK: {\n674:     // Any changes to request headers or query parameters can affect how the request is going to be\n675:     // routed. If we are changing the headers we also need to clear the route\n676:     // cache.\n677:     if (config_->clearRouteCache() &&\n678:         (!response->headers_to_set.empty() || !response->headers_to_append.empty() ||\n679:          !response->headers_to_remove.empty() || !response->query_parameters_to_set.empty() ||\n680:          !response->query_parameters_to_remove.empty())) {\n681:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"ext_authz is clearing route cache\", *decoder_callbacks_);\n682:       decoder_callbacks_->downstreamCallbacks()->clearRouteCache();\n683:     }\n684: \n685:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n686:                      \"ext_authz filter added header(s) to the request:\", 71. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git -C /workspace show HEAD:source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc | sed -n '505,630p'",
  "description": "Read onComplete section from HEAD"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "    if (config_->clearRouteCache() &&\n        (!response->headers_to_set.empty() || !response->headers_to_append.empty() ||\n         !response->headers_to_remove.empty() || !response->query_parameters_to_set.empty() ||\n         !response->query_parameters_to_remove.empty())) {\n      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"ext_authz is clearing route cache\", *decoder_callbacks_);\n      decoder_callbacks_->downstreamCallbacks()->clearRouteCache();\n    }\n\n    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n                     \"ext_authz filter added header(s) to the request:\", *decoder_callbacks_);\n    for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_set) {\n      CheckResult check_result = validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\n          Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation::SET, key, value);\n      switch (check_result) {\n      case CheckResult::OK:\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\n        request_headers_->setCopy(Http::LowerCaseString(key), value);\n        break;\n      case CheckResult::IGNORE:\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring invalid header to set '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n                         key, value);\n        break;\n      case CheckResult::FAIL:\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejecting invalid header to set '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n                         key, value);\n        rejectResponse();\n        return;\n      }\n    }\n    for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_add) {\n      CheckResult check_result = validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\n          Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation::SET, key, value);\n      switch (check_result) {\n      case CheckResult::OK:\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\n        request_headers_->addCopy(Http::LowerCaseString(key), value);\n        break;\n      case CheckResult::IGNORE:\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring invalid header to add '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n                         key, value);\n        break;\n      case CheckResult::FAIL:\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejecting invalid header to add '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n                         key, value);\n        rejectResponse();\n        return;\n      }\n    }\n    for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_append) {\n      CheckResult check_result = validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\n          Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation::APPEND, key, value);\n      switch (check_result) {\n      case CheckResult::OK: {\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\n        Http::LowerCaseString lowercase_key(key);\n        const auto header_to_modify = request_headers_->get(lowercase_key);\n        // TODO(dio): Add a flag to allow appending non-existent headers, without setting it\n        // first (via `headers_to_add`). For example, given:\n        // 1. Original headers {\"original\": \"true\"}\n        // 2. Response headers from the authorization servers {{\"append\": \"1\"}, {\"append\":\n        // \"2\"}}\n        //\n        // Currently it is not possible to add {{\"append\": \"1\"}, {\"append\": \"2\"}} (the\n        // intended combined headers: {{\"original\": \"true\"}, {\"append\": \"1\"}, {\"append\":\n        // \"2\"}}) to the request to upstream server by only sets `headers_to_append`.\n        if (!header_to_modify.empty()) {\n          ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\n          // The current behavior of appending is by combining entries with the same key,\n          // into one entry. The value of that combined entry is separated by \",\".\n          // TODO(dio): Consider to use addCopy instead.\n          request_headers_->appendCopy(lowercase_key, value);\n        }\n        break;\n      }\n      case CheckResult::IGNORE:\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring invalid header to append '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n                         key, value);\n        break;\n      case CheckResult::FAIL:\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejecting invalid header to append '{}':'{}'.\",\n                         *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\n        rejectResponse();\n        return;\n      }\n    }\n\n    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n                     \"ext_authz filter removed header(s) from the request:\", *decoder_callbacks_);\n    for (const auto& key : response->headers_to_remove) {\n      // If the response contains an invalid header to remove, it's the same as trying to remove a\n      // header that doesn't exist, so just ignore it.\n      if (config_->validateMutations() && !Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key)) {\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring invalid header removal '{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_, key);\n        continue;\n      }\n      // We don't allow removing any :-prefixed headers, nor Host, as removing them would make the\n      // request malformed. checkDecoderHeaderMutation also performs this check, however, so only\n      // perform this check explicitly if decoder header mutation rules is empty.\n      if (!config_->hasDecoderHeaderMutationRules() &&\n          !Http::HeaderUtility::isRemovableHeader(key)) {\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring invalid header removal '{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_, key);\n        continue;\n      }\n      // Check header mutation is valid according to configured decoder header mutation rules.\n      Http::LowerCaseString lowercase_key(key);\n      switch (config_->checkDecoderHeaderMutation(CheckOperation::REMOVE, lowercase_key,\n                                                  EMPTY_STRING)) {\n      case CheckResult::OK:\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key);\n        request_headers_->remove(lowercase_key);\n        break;\n      case CheckResult::IGNORE:\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring disallowed header removal '{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n                         key);\n        break;\n      case CheckResult::FAIL:\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejecting disallowed header removal '{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n                         key);\n        rejectResponse();\n        return;\n      }\n    }\n\n    if (!response->response_headers_to_add.empty()) {\n      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter saving {} header(s) to add to the response:\",\n                       *decoder_callbacks_, response->response_headers_to_add.size());"
}

  if (!headersWithinLimits(*request_headers_)) {\n759:             stats_.request_header_limits_reached_.inc();\n760:             rejectResponse();\n761:             return;\n762:           }\n763:         }\n764:         break;\n765:       }\n766:       case CheckResult::IGNORE:\n767:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring invalid header to append '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n768:                          key, value);\n769:         break;\n770:       case CheckResult::FAIL:\n771:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejecting invalid header to append '{}':'{}'.\",\n772:                          *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\n773:         rejectResponse();\n774:         return;\n775:       }\n776:     }\n777: \n778:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n779:                      \"ext_authz filter removed header(s) from the request:\", *decoder_callbacks_);\n780:     for (const auto& key : response->headers_to_remove) {\n781:       // If the response contains an invalid header to remove, it's the same as trying to remove a\n782:       // header that doesn't exist, so just ignore it.\n783:       if (config_->validateMutations() && !Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key)) {\n784:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring invalid header removal '{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_, key);\n785:         continue;\n786:       }\n787:       // We don't allow removing any :-prefixed headers, nor Host, as removing them would make the\n788:       // request malformed. checkDecoderHeaderMutation also performs this check, however, so only\n789:       // perform this check explicitly if decoder header mutation rules is empty.\n790:       if (!config_->hasDecoderHeaderMutationRules() &&\n791:           !Http::HeaderUtility::isRemovableHeader(key)) {\n792:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring invalid header removal '{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_, key);\n793:         continue;\n794:       }\n795:       // Check header mutation is valid according to configured decoder header mutation rules.\n796:       Http::LowerCaseString lowercase_key(key);\n797:       switch (config_->checkDecoderHeaderMutation(CheckOperation::REMOVE, lowercase_key,\n798:                                                   EMPTY_STRING)) {\n799:       case CheckResult::OK:\n800:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key);\n801:         request_headers_->remove(lowercase_key);\n802:         if (!headersWithinLimits(*request_headers_)) {\n803:           stats_.request_header_limits_reached_.inc();\n804:           rejectResponse();\n805:           return;\n806:         }\n807:         break;\n808:       case CheckResult::IGNORE:\n809:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring disallowed header removal '{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n810:                          key);\n811:         break;\n812:       case CheckResult::FAIL:\n813:         ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejecting disallowed header removal '{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n814:                          key);\n815:         rejectResponse();\n816:         return;\n817:       }\n818:     }\n819: \n820:     if (!response->response_headers_to_add.empty()) {\n821:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter saving {} header(s) to add to the response:\",\n822:                        *decoder_callbacks_, response->response_headers_to_add.size());\n823:       for (const auto& [key, value] : response->response_headers_to_add) {\n824:         if (config_->validateMutations() && (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\n825:                                              !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value))) {\n826:           ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejected invalid header '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_, key,\n827:                            value);\n828:           rejectResponse();\n829:           return;\n830:         }\n831:       }\n832:       response_headers_to_add_ = Http::HeaderVector{response->response_headers_to_add.begin(),\n833:                                                     response->response_headers_to_add.end()};\n834:     }\n835: \n836:     if (!response->response_headers_to_set.empty()) {\n837:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter saving {} header(s) to set to the response:\",\n838:                        *decoder_callbacks_, response->response_headers_to_set.size());\n839:       for (const auto& [key, value] : response->response_headers_to_set) {\n840:         if (config_->validateMutations() && (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\n841:                                              !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value))) {\n842:           ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejected invalid header '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_, key,\n843:                            value);\n844:           rejectResponse();\n845:           return;\n846:         }\n847:       }\n848:       response_headers_to_set_ = Http::HeaderVector{response->response_headers_to_set.begin(),\n849:                                                     response->response_headers_to_set.end()};\n850:     }\n851: ",

Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "#include \"source/extensions/filters/http/fault/fault_filter.h\"\n\n#include <chrono>\n#include <cstdint>\n#include <string>\n#include <vector>\n\n#include \"envoy/event/timer.h\"\n#include \"envoy/extensions/filters/http/fault/v3/fault.pb.h\"\n#include \"envoy/http/codes.h\"\n#include \"envoy/http/filter.h\"\n#include \"envoy/http/header_map.h\"\n#include \"envoy/stats/scope.h\"\n\n#include \"source/common/common/assert.h\"\n#include \"source/common/common/empty_string.h\"\n#include \"source/common/common/fmt.h\"\n#include \"source/common/http/codes.h\"\n#include \"source/common/http/header_map_impl.h\"\n#include \"source/common/http/headers.h\"\n#include \"source/common/http/utility.h\"\n#include \"source/common/protobuf/utility.h\"\n#include \"source/common/stats/utility.h\"\n\nnamespace Envoy {\nnamespace Extensions {\nnamespace HttpFilters {\nnamespace Fault {\n\nstruct RcDetailsValues {\n  // The fault filter injected an abort for this request.\n  const std::string FaultAbort = \"fault_filter_abort\";\n};\nusing RcDetails = ConstSingleton<RcDetailsValues>;\n\nFaultSettings::FaultSettings(const envoy::extensions::filters::http::fault::v3::HTTPFault& fault,\n                             Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context)\n    : fault_filter_headers_(Http::HeaderUtility::buildHeaderDataVector(fault.headers(), context)),\n      delay_percent_runtime_(PROTOBUF_GET_STRING_OR_DEFAULT(fault, delay_percent_runtime,\n                                                            RuntimeKeys::get().DelayPercentKey)),\n      abort_percent_runtime_(PROTOBUF_GET_STRING_OR_DEFAULT(fault, abort_percent_runtime,\n                                                            RuntimeKeys::get().AbortPercentKey)),\n      delay_duration_runtime_(PROTOBUF_GET_STRING_OR_DEFAULT(fault, delay_duration_runtime,\n                                                             RuntimeKeys::get().DelayDurationKey)),\n      abort_http_status_runtime_(PROTOBUF_GET_STRING_OR_DEFAULT(\n          fault, abort_http_status_runtime, RuntimeKeys::get().AbortHttpStatusKey)),\n      abort_grpc_status_runtime_(PROTOBUF_GET_STRING_OR_DEFAULT(\n          fault, abort_grpc_status_runtime, RuntimeKeys::get().AbortGrpcStatusKey)),\n      max_active_faults_runtime_(PROTOBUF_GET_STRING_OR_DEFAULT(\n          fault, max_active_faults_runtime, RuntimeKeys::get().MaxActiveFaultsKey)),\n      response_rate_limit_percent_runtime_(\n          PROTOBUF_GET_STRING_OR_DEFAULT(fault, response_rate_limit_percent_runtime,\n                                         RuntimeKeys::get().ResponseRateLimitPercentKey)),\n      disable_downstream_cluster_stats_(fault.disable_downstream_cluster_stats()),\n      filter_metadata_(fault.filter_metadata()) {\n  if (fault.has_abort()) {\n    request_abort_config_ =\n        std::make_unique<Filters::Common::Fault::FaultAbortConfig>(fault.abort());\n  }\n\n  if (fault.has_delay()) {\n    request_delay_config_ =\n        std::make_unique<Filters::Common::Fault::FaultDelayConfig>(fault.delay());\n  }\n\n  upstream_cluster_ = fault.upstream_cluster();\n\n  for (const auto& node : fault.downstream_nodes()) {\n    downstream_nodes_.insert(node);\n  }\n\n  if (fault.has_max_active_faults()) {\n    max_active_faults_ = fault.max_active_faults().value();\n  }\n\n  if (fault.has_response_rate_limit()) {\n    response_rate_limit_ =\n        std::make_unique<Filters::Common::Fault::FaultRateLimitConfig>(fault.response_rate_limit());\n  }\n}\n\nFaultFilterConfig::FaultFilterConfig(\n    const envoy::extensions::filters::http::fault::v3::HTTPFault& fault,\n    const std::string& stats_prefix, Stats::Scope& scope,\n    Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& context)\n    : settings_(fault, context), runtime_(context.runtime()),\n      stats_(generateStats(stats_prefix, scope)), scope_(scope), time_source_(context.timeSource()),\n      stat_name_set_(scope.symbolTable().makeSet(\"Fault\")),\n      aborts_injected_(stat_name_set_->add(\"aborts_injected\")),\n      delays_injected_(stat_name_set_->add(\"delays_injected\")),\n      stats_prefix_(stat_name_set_->add(absl::StrCat(stats_prefix, \"fault\"))) {}\n\nvoid FaultFilterConfig::incCounter(Stats::StatName downstream_cluster, Stats::StatName stat_name) {\n  if (!settings_.disableDownstreamClusterStats()) {\n    Stats::Utility::counterFromStatNames(scope_, {stats_prefix_, downstream_cluster, stat_name})\n        .inc();\n  }\n}\n\nFaultFilter::FaultFilter(FaultFilterConfigSharedPtr config) : config_(config) {}\n\nFaultFilter::~FaultFilter() {\n  ASSERT(delay_timer_ == nullptr);\n  ASSERT(response_limiter_ == nullptr || response_limiter_->destroyed());\n}\n\n// Delays and aborts are independent events. One can inject a delay\n// followed by an abort or inject just a delay or abort. In this callback,\n// if we inject a delay, then we will inject the abort in the delay timer\n// callback.\nHttp::FilterHeadersStatus FaultFilter::decodeHeaders(Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers, bool) {\n  // Route-level configuration overrides filter-level configuration\n  // NOTE: We should not use runtime when reading from route-level\n  // faults. In other words, runtime is supported only when faults are\n  // configured at the filter level.\n  fault_settings_ = config_->settings();\n  const auto* per_route_settings =\n      Http::Utility::resolveMostSpecificPerFilterConfig<FaultSettings>(decoder_callbacks_);\n  fault_settings_ = per_route_settings ? per_route_settings : fault_settings_;\n\n  if (!matchesTargetUpstreamCluster()) {\n    return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\n  }\n\n  if (!matchesDownstreamNodes(headers)) {\n    return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\n  }\n\n  // Check for header matches\n  if (!Http::HeaderUtility::matchHeaders(headers, fault_settings_->filterHeaders())) {\n    return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\n  }\n\n  if (headers.EnvoyDownstreamServiceCluster()) {\n    downstream_cluster_ = std::string(headers.getEnvoyDownstreamServiceClusterValue());\n    if (!downstream_cluster_.empty() && !fault_settings_->disableDownstreamClusterStats()) {\n      downstream_cluster_storage_ = std::make_unique<Stats::StatNameDynamicStorage>(\n          downstream_cluster_, config_->scope().symbolTable());\n    }\n\n    downstream_cluster_delay_percent_key_ =\n        fmt::format(\"fault.http.{}.delay.fixed_delay_percent\", downstream_cluster_);\n    downstream_cluster_abort_percent_key_ =\n        fmt::format(\"fault.http.{}.abort.abort_percent\", downstream_cluster_);\n    downstream_cluster_delay_duration_key_ =\n        fmt::format(\"fault.http.{}.delay.fixed_duration_ms\", downstream_cluster_);\n    downstream_cluster_abort_http_status_key_ =\n        fmt::format(\"fault.http.{}.abort.http_status\", downstream_cluster_);\n    downstream_cluster_abort_grpc_status_key_ =\n        fmt::format(\"fault.http.{}.abort.grpc_status\", downstream_cluster_);\n  }\n\n  maybeSetupResponseRateLimit(headers);\n\n  if (maybeSetupDelay(headers)) {\n    return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\n  }\n\n  if (maybeDoAbort(headers)) {\n    return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\n  }\n\n  return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\n}\n\nbool FaultFilter::maybeSetupDelay(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\n  absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds> duration = delayDuration(request_headers);\n  if (duration.has_value() && tryIncActiveFaults()) {\n    delay_timer_ = decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher().createTimer(\n        [this, &request_headers]() -> void { postDelayInjection(request_headers); });\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"fault: delaying request {}ms\", duration.value().count());\n    delay_timer_->enableTimer(duration.value(), &decoder_callbacks_->scope());\n    recordDelaysInjectedStats();\n    decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::DelayInjected);\n    auto& dynamic_metadata = decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().dynamicMetadata();\n    (*dynamic_metadata.mutable_filter_metadata())[decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName()] =\n        fault_settings_->filterMetadata();\n    return true;\n  }\n  return false;\n}\n\nbool FaultFilter::maybeDoAbort(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\n  absl::optional<Http::Code> http_status;\n  absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status;\n  std::tie(http_status, grpc_status) = abortStatus(request_headers);\n\n  if (http_status.has_value() && tryIncActiveFaults()) {\n    abortWithStatus(http_status.value(), grpc_status);\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  return false;\n}\n\nvoid FaultFilter::maybeSetupResponseRateLimit(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\n  if (!isResponseRateLimitEnabled(request_headers)) {\n    return;\n  }"
}
