Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"400:                                  : Http::FilterTrailersStatus::StopIteration;\\n401: }\\n402: \\n403: FaultFilterStats FaultFilterConfig::generateStats(const std::string& prefix, Stats::Scope& scope) {\\n404:   const std::string final_prefix = prefix + \\\"fault.\\\";\\n405:   return {ALL_FAULT_FILTER_STATS(POOL_COUNTER_PREFIX(scope, final_prefix),\\n406:                                  POOL_GAUGE_PREFIX(scope, final_prefix))};\\n407: }\\n408: \\n409: bool FaultFilter::tryIncActiveFaults() {\\n410:   // Only charge 1 active fault per filter in case we are injecting multiple faults.\\n411:   // Since we count at most one active fault per filter, we also allow multiple faults\\n412:   // per filter without checking for overflow.\\n413:   if (fault_active_) {\\n414:     return true;\\n415:   }\\n416: \\n417:   // We only check for overflow when attempting to perform a fault. Note that this means that a\\n418:   // single request might increment the counter more than once if it tries to apply multiple faults,\\n419:   // and it is also possible for it to fail the first check then succeed on the second (should\\n420:   // another thread decrement the active fault gauge).\\n421:   if (faultOverflow()) {\\n422:     return false;\\n423:   }\\n424: \\n425:   // TODO(mattklein123): Consider per-fault type active fault gauges.\\n426:   config_->stats().active_faults_.inc();\\n427:   fault_active_ = true;\\n428: \\n429:   return true;\\n430: }\\n431: \\n432: void FaultFilter::onDestroy() {\\n433:   resetTimerState();\\n434:   if (response_limiter_ != nullptr) {\\n435:     response_limiter_->destroy();\\n436:   }\\n437:   if (fault_active_) {\\n438:     config_->stats().active_faults_.dec();\\n439:   }\\n440: }\\n441: \\n442: bool FaultFilter::isResponseRateLimitConfigured() {\\n443:   return fault_settings_->responseRateLimit() != nullptr;\\n444: }\\n445: \\n446: void FaultFilter::postDelayInjection(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\\n447:   resetTimerState();\\n448: \\n449:   // Delays can be followed by aborts\\n450:   absl::optional<Http::Code> http_status;\\n451:   absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status;\\n452:   std::tie(http_status, grpc_status) = abortStatus(request_headers);\\n453: \\n454:   if (http_status.has_value()) {\\n455:     abortWithStatus(http_status.value(), grpc_status);\\n456:   } else {\\n457:     // Should not continue to count as an active fault after the delay has elapsed if no other type\\n458:     // of fault is active. As the delay timer is always done at this point and followed abort faults\\n459:     // have been checked earlier, here we just check if there's a response rate limit configured.\\n460:     ASSERT(fault_active_);\\n461:     ASSERT(delay_timer_ == nullptr);\\n462:     if (!isResponseRateLimitConfigured()) {\\n463:       config_->stats().active_faults_.dec();\\n464:       fault_active_ = false;\\n465:     }\\n466: \\n467:     // Continue request processing.\\n468:     decoder_callbacks_->continueDecoding();\\n469:   }\\n470: }\\n471: \\n472: void FaultFilter::abortWithStatus(Http::Code http_status_code,\\n473:                                   absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status) {\\n474:   recordAbortsInjectedStats();\\n475:   decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::FaultInjected);\\n476:   auto& dynamic_metadata = decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().dynamicMetadata();\\n477:   (*dynamic_metadata.mutable_filter_metadata())[decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName()] =\\n478:       fault_settings_->filterMetadata();\\n479:   decoder_callbacks_->sendLocalReply(http_status_code, \\\"fault filter abort\\\", nullptr, grpc_status,\\n480:                                      RcDetails::get().FaultAbort);\\n481: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/extensions/filters/http/fault/fault_filter.cc%3FL400-480\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "400:                                  : Http::FilterTrailersStatus::StopIteration;\n401: }\n402: \n403: FaultFilterStats FaultFilterConfig::generateStats(const std::string& prefix, Stats::Scope& scope) {\n404:   const std::string final_prefix = prefix + \"fault.\";\n405:   return {ALL_FAULT_FILTER_STATS(POOL_COUNTER_PREFIX(scope, final_prefix),\n406:                                  POOL_GAUGE_PREFIX(scope, final_prefix))};\n407: }\n408: \n409: bool FaultFilter::tryIncActiveFaults() {\n410:   // Only charge 1 active fault per filter in case we are injecting multiple faults.\n411:   // Since we count at most one active fault per filter, we also allow multiple faults\n412:   // per filter without checking for overflow.\n413:   if (fault_active_) {\n414:     return true;\n415:   }\n416: \n417:   // We only check for overflow when attempting to perform a fault. Note that this means that a\n418:   // single request might increment the counter more than once if it tries to apply multiple faults,\n419:   // and it is also possible for it to fail the first check then succeed on the second (should\n420:   // another thread decrement the active fault gauge).\n421:   if (faultOverflow()) {\n422:     return false;\n423:   }\n424: \n425:   // TODO(mattklein123): Consider per-fault type active fault gauges.\n426:   config_->stats().active_faults_.inc();\n427:   fault_active_ = true;\n428: \n429:   return true;\n430: }\n431: \n432: void FaultFilter::onDestroy() {\n433:   resetTimerState();\n434:   if (response_limiter_ != nullptr) {\n435:     response_limiter_->destroy();\n436:   }\n437:   if (fault_active_) {\n438:     config_->stats().active_faults_.dec();\n439:   }\n440: }\n441: \n442: bool FaultFilter::isResponseRateLimitConfigured() {\n443:   return fault_settings_->responseRateLimit() != nullptr;\n444: }\n445: \n446: void FaultFilter::postDelayInjection(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\n447:   resetTimerState();\n448: \n449:   // Delays can be followed by aborts\n450:   absl::optional<Http::Code> http_status;\n451:   absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status;\n452:   std::tie(http_status, grpc_status) = abortStatus(request_headers);\n453: \n454:   if (http_status.has_value()) {\n455:     abortWithStatus(http_status.value(), grpc_status);\n456:   } else {\n457:     // Should not continue to count as an active fault after the delay has elapsed if no other type\n458:     // of fault is active. As the delay timer is always done at this point and followed abort faults\n459:     // have been checked earlier, here we just check if there's a response rate limit configured.\n460:     ASSERT(fault_active_);\n461:     ASSERT(delay_timer_ == nullptr);\n462:     if (!isResponseRateLimitConfigured()) {\n463:       config_->stats().active_faults_.dec();\n464:       fault_active_ = false;\n465:     }\n466: \n467:     // Continue request processing.\n468:     decoder_callbacks_->continueDecoding();\n469:   }\n470: }\n471: \n472: void FaultFilter::abortWithStatus(Http::Code http_status_code,\n473:                                   absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status) {\n474:   recordAbortsInjectedStats();\n475:   decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::FaultInjected);\n476:   auto& dynamic_metadata = decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().dynamicMetadata();\n477:   (*dynamic_metadata.mutable_filter_metadata())[decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName()] =\n478:       fault_settings_->filterMetadata();\n479:   decoder_callbacks_->sendLocalReply(http_status_code, \"fault filter abort\", nullptr, grpc_status,\n480:                                      RcDetails::get().FaultAbort);\n481: ",

  "content": "{\"content\":\"560: bool HeaderUtility::isRemovableHeader(absl::string_view header) {\\n561:   return (header.empty() || header[0] != ':') &&\\n562:          !absl::EqualsIgnoreCase(header, Headers::get().HostLegacy.get());\\n563: }\\n564: \\n565: bool HeaderUtility::isModifiableHeader(absl::string_view header) {\\n566:   return (header.empty() || header[0] != ':') &&\\n567:          !absl::EqualsIgnoreCase(header, Headers::get().HostLegacy.get());\\n568: }\\n569: \\n570: HeaderUtility::HeaderValidationResult HeaderUtility::checkHeaderNameForUnderscores(\\n571:     absl::string_view header_name,\\n572:     envoy::config::core::v3::HttpProtocolOptions::HeadersWithUnderscoresAction\\n573:         headers_with_underscores_action,\\n574:     HeaderValidatorStats& stats) {\\n575:   if (headers_with_underscores_action == envoy::config::core::v3::HttpProtocolOptions::ALLOW ||\\n576:       !HeaderUtility::headerNameContainsUnderscore(header_name)) {\\n577:     return HeaderValidationResult::ACCEPT;\\n578:   }\\n579:   if (headers_with_underscores_action ==\\n580:       envoy::config::core::v3::HttpProtocolOptions::DROP_HEADER) {\\n581:     ENVOY_LOG_MISC(debug, \\\"Dropping header with invalid characters in its name: {}\\\", header_name);\\n582:     stats.incDroppedHeadersWithUnderscores();\\n583:     return HeaderValidationResult::DROP;\\n584:   }\\n585:   ENVOY_LOG_MISC(debug, \\\"Rejecting request due to header name with underscores: {}\\\", header_name);\\n586:   stats.incRequestsRejectedWithUnderscoresInHeaders();\\n587:   return HeaderUtility::HeaderValidationResult::REJECT;\\n588: }\\n589: \\n590: HeaderUtility::HeaderValidationResult\\n591: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/header_utility.cc%3FL560-590\"}",

streamInfo(), code, body, content_type);\\n1184:           },\\n1185:           [&](ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& response_headers, bool end_stream) -> void {\\n1186:             // Move the response headers into the FilterManager to make sure they're visible to\\n1187:             // access logs.\\n1188:             filter_manager_callbacks_.setResponseHeaders(std::move(response_headers));\\n1189: \\n1190:             state_.non_100_response_headers_encoded_ = true;\\n1191:             filter_manager_callbacks_.encodeHeaders(*filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders(),\\n1192:                                                     end_stream);\\n1193:             if (state_.saw_downstream_reset_) {\\n1194:               return;\\n1195:             }\\n1196:             maybeEndEncode(end_stream);\\n1197:           },\\n1198:           [&](Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) -> void {\\n1199:             filter_manager_callbacks_.encodeData(data, end_stream);\\n1200:             if (state_.saw_downstream_reset_) {\\n1201:               return;\\n1202:             }\\n1203:             maybeEndEncode(end_stream);\\n1204:           }},\\n1205:       Utility::LocalReplyData{state_.is_grpc_request_, code, body, grpc_status, is_head_request});\\n1206: }\\n1207: \\n1208: void FilterManager::encode1xxHeaders(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter,\\n1209:                                      ResponseHeaderMap& headers) {\\n1210:   filter_manager_callbacks_.resetIdleTimer();\\n1211:   ASSERT(proxy_100_continue_);\\n1212:   // The caller must guarantee that encode1xxHeaders() is invoked at most once.\\n1213:   ASSERT(!state_.has_1xx_headers_ || filter != nullptr);\\n1214:   // Make sure commonContinue continues encode1xxHeaders.\\n1215:   state_.has_1xx_headers_ = true;\\n1216: \\n1217:   // Similar to the block in encodeHeaders, run encode1xxHeaders on each\\n1218:   // filter. This is simpler than that case because 100 continue implies no\\n1219:   // end-stream, and because there are normal headers coming there's no need for\\n1220:   // complex continuation logic.\\n1221:   // 100-continue filter iteration should always start with the next filter if available.\\n1222:   StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator entry =\\n1223:       commonEncodePrefix(filter, false, FilterIterationStartState::AlwaysStartFromNext);\\n1224:   for (; entry != encoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\\n1225:     ENVOY_EXECUTION_SCOPE(trackedStream(), &(*entry)->filter_context_);\\n1226:     ASSERT(!(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::Encode1xxHeaders));\\n1227:     state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::Encode1xxHeaders;\\n1228:     const Filter1xxHeadersStatus status = (*entry)->handle_->encode1xxHeaders(headers);\\n1229:     state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::Encode1xxHeaders;\\n1230: \\n1231:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"encode 1xx continue headers called: filter={} status={}\\\", *this,\\n1232:                      (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name, static_cast<uint64_t>(status));\\n1233:     if (state_.encoder_filter_chain_aborted_) {\\n1234:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\\n1235:                        \\\"encode1xxHeaders filter iteration aborted due to local reply: filter={}\\\",\\n1236:                        *this, (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name);\\n1237:       return;\\n1238:     }\\n1239: \\n1240:     if (!(*entry)->commonHandleAfter1xxHeadersCallback(status)) {\\n1241:       return;\\n1242:     }\\n1243:   }\\n1244: \\n1245:   filter_manager_callbacks_.encode1xxHeaders(headers);\\n1246: }\\n1247: \\n1248: void FilterManager::maybeContinueEncoding(StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator continue_data_entry) {\\n1249:   if (continue_data_entry != encoder_filters_.end()) {\\n1250:     // We use the continueEncoding() code since it will correctly handle not calling\\n1251:     // encodeHeaders() again. Fake setting StopSingleIteration since the continueEncoding() code\\n1252:     // expects it.\\n1253:     ASSERT(buffered_response_data_);\\n1254:     (*continue_data_entry)->iteration_state_ =\\n1255:         ActiveStreamFilterBase::IterationState::StopSingleIteration;\\n1256:     (*continue_data_entry)->continueEncoding();\\n1257:   }\\n1258: }\\n1259: \\n1260: void FilterManager::encodeHeaders(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter, ResponseHeaderMap& headers,\\n1261: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc%3FL1170-1260\"}",

            end_stream);\n1193:             if (state_.saw_downstream_reset_) {\n1194:               return;\n1195:             }\n1196:             maybeEndEncode(end_stream);\n1197:           },\n1198:           [&](Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) -> void {\n1199:             filter_manager_callbacks_.encodeData(data, end_stream);\n1200:             if (state_.saw_downstream_reset_) {\n1201:               return;\n1202:             }\n1203:             maybeEndEncode(end_stream);\n1204:           }},\n1205:       Utility::LocalReplyData{state_.is_grpc_request_, code, body, grpc_status, is_head_request});\n1206: }\n1207: \n1208: void FilterManager::encode1xxHeaders(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter,\n1209:                                      ResponseHeaderMap& headers) {\n1210:   filter_manager_callbacks_.resetIdleTimer();\n1211:   ASSERT(proxy_100_continue_);\n1212:   // The caller must guarantee that encode1xxHeaders() is invoked at most once.\n1213:   ASSERT(!state_.has_1xx_headers_ || filter != nullptr);\n1214:   // Make sure commonContinue continues encode1xxHeaders.\n1215:   state_.has_1xx_headers_ = true;\n1216: \n1217:   // Similar to the block in encodeHeaders, run encode1xxHeaders on each\n1218:   // filter. This is simpler than that case because 100 continue implies no\n1219:   // end-stream, and because there are normal headers coming there's no need for\n1220:   // complex continuation logic.\n1221:   // 100-continue filter iteration should always start with the next filter if available.\n1222:   StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator entry =\n1223:       commonEncodePrefix(filter, false, FilterIterationStartState::AlwaysStartFromNext);\n1224:   for (; entry != encoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\n1225:     ENVOY_EXECUTION_SCOPE(trackedStream(), &(*entry)->filter_context_);\n1226:     ASSERT(!(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::Encode1xxHeaders));\n1227:     state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::Encode1xxHeaders;\n1228:     const Filter1xxHeadersStatus status = (*entry)->handle_->encode1xxHeaders(headers);\n1229:     state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::Encode1xxHeaders;\n1230: \n1231:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"encode 1xx continue headers called: filter={} status={}\", *this,\n1232:                      (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name, static_cast<uint64_t>(status));\n1233:     if (state_.encoder_filter_chain_aborted_) {\n1234:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n1235:                        \"encode1xxHeaders filter iteration aborted due to local reply: filter={}\",\n1236:                        *this, (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name);\n1237:       return;\n1238:     }\n1239: \n1240:     if (!(*entry)->commonHandleAfter1xxHeadersCallback(status)) {\n1241:       return;\n1242:     }\n1243:   }\n1244: \n1245:   filter_manager_callbacks_.encode1xxHeaders(headers);\n1246: }\n1247: \n1248: void FilterManager::maybeContinueEncoding(StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator continue_data_entry) {\n1249:   if (continue_data_entry != encoder_filters_.end()) {\n1250:     // We use the continueEncoding() code since it will correctly handle not calling\n1251:     // encodeHeaders() again. Fake setting StopSingleIteration since the continueEncoding() code\n1252:     // expects it.\n1253:     ASSERT(buffered_response_data_);\n1254:     (*continue_data_entry)->iteration_state_ =\n1255:         ActiveStreamFilterBase::IterationState::StopSingleIteration;\n1256:     (*continue_data_entry)->continueEncoding();\n1257:   }\n1258: }\n1259: \n1260: void FilterManager::encodeHeaders(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter, ResponseHeaderMap& headers,\n1261: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc%3FL1170-1260"
  }
}

82. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 410,
  "path": "source/common/http/filter_manager.cc",
  "repo": "github.com/envoyproxy/envoy",
  "startLine": 377
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"377: \\n378: bool ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::canContinue() {\\n379:   // It is possible for the connection manager to respond directly to a request even while\\n380:   // a filter is trying to continue. If a response has already happened, we should not\\n381:   // continue to further filters. A concrete example of this is a filter buffering data, the\\n382:   // last data frame comes in and the filter continues, but the final buffering takes the stream\\n383:   // over the high watermark such that a 413 is returned.\\n384:   return !parent_.stopDecoderFilterChain();\\n385: }\\n386: \\n387: bool ActiveStreamEncoderFilter::canContinue() {\\n388:   // As with ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::canContinue() make sure we do not\\n389:   // continue if a local reply has been sent or ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::recreateStream() is\\n390:   // called, etc.\\n391:   return !parent_.state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_ && !parent_.stopEncoderFilterChain();\\n392: }\\n393: \\n394: Buffer::InstancePtr ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::createBuffer() {\\n395:   auto buffer = dispatcher().getWatermarkFactory().createBuffer(\\n396:       [this]() -> void { this->requestDataDrained(); },\\n397:       [this]() -> void { this->requestDataTooLarge(); },\\n398:       []() -> void { /* TODO(adisuissa): Handle overflow watermark */ });\\n399:   buffer->setWatermarks(parent_.buffer_limit_);\\n400:   return buffer;\\n401: }\\n402: \\n403: Buffer::InstancePtr& ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::bufferedData() {\\n404:   return parent_.buffered_request_data_;\\n405: }\\n406: \\n407: bool ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::observedEndStream() { return parent_.decoderObservedEndStream(); }\\n408: \\n409: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::doHeaders(bool end_stream) {\\n410:   parent_.decodeHeaders(this, *parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.requestHeaders(), end_stream);\\n411: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc%3FL377-410\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "377: \n378: bool ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::canContinue() {\n379:   // It is possible for the connection manager to respond directly to a request even while\n380:   // a filter is trying to continue. If a response has already happened, we should not\n381:   // continue to further filters. A concrete example of this is a filter buffering data, the\n382:   // last data frame comes in and the filter continues, but the final buffering takes the stream\n383:   // over the high watermark such that a 413 is returned.\n384:   return !parent_.stopDecoderFilterChain();\n385: }\n386: \n387: bool ActiveStreamEncoderFilter::canContinue() {\n388:   // As with ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::canContinue() make sure we do not\n389:   // continue if a local reply has been sent or ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::recreateStream() is\n390:   // called, etc.\n391:   return !parent_.state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_ && !parent_.stopEncoderFilterChain();\n392: }\n393: \n394: Buffer::InstancePtr ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::createBuffer() {\n395:   auto buffer = dispatcher().getWatermarkFactory().createBuffer(\n396:       [this]() -> void { this->requestDataDrained(); },\n397:       [this]() -> void { this->requestDataTooLarge(); },\n398:       []() -> void { /* TODO(adisuissa): Handle overflow watermark */ });\n399:   buffer->setWatermarks(parent_.buffer_limit_);\n400:   return buffer;\n401: }\n402: \n403: Buffer::InstancePtr& ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::bufferedData() {\n404:   return parent_.buffered_request_data_;\n405: }\n406: \n407: bool ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::observedEndStream() { return parent_.decoderObservedEndStream(); }\n408: \n409: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::doHeaders(bool end_stream) {\n410:   parent_.decodeHeaders(this, *parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.requestHeaders(), end_stream);\n411: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc%3FL377-410"

  "content": "{\"content\":\"38: bool HeaderUtility::matchHeaders(const HeaderMap& request_headers,\\n39:                                  const std::vector<HeaderDataPtr>& config_headers) {\\n40:   // No headers to match is considered a match.\\n41:   if (!config_headers.empty()) {\\n42:     for (const HeaderDataPtr& cfg_header_data : config_headers) {\\n43:       if (!cfg_header_data->matchesHeaders(request_headers)) {\\n44:         return false;\\n45:       }\\n46:     }\\n47:   }\\n48: \\n49:   return true;\\n50: }\\n51: \\n52: HeaderUtility::GetAllOfHeaderAsStringResult\\n53: HeaderUtility::getAllOfHeaderAsString(const HeaderMap::GetResult& header_value,\\n54:                                       absl::string_view separator) {\\n55:   GetAllOfHeaderAsStringResult result;\\n56: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/header_utility.cc%3FL38-55\"}",
  "structuredContent": {

  "content": "{\"content\":\"405:   return {ALL_FAULT_FILTER_STATS(POOL_COUNTER_PREFIX(scope, final_prefix),\\n406:                                  POOL_GAUGE_PREFIX(scope, final_prefix))};\\n407: }\\n408: \\n409: bool FaultFilter::tryIncActiveFaults() {\\n410:   // Only charge 1 active fault per filter in case we are injecting multiple faults.\\n411:   // Since we count at most one active fault per filter, we also allow multiple faults\\n412:   // per filter without checking for overflow.\\n413:   if (fault_active_) {\\n414:     return true;\\n415:   }\\n416: \\n417:   // We only check for overflow when attempting to perform a fault. Note that this means that a\\n418:   // single request might increment the counter more than once if it tries to apply multiple faults,\\n419:   // and it is also possible for it to fail the first check then succeed on the second (should\\n420:   // another thread decrement the active fault gauge).\\n421:   if (faultOverflow()) {\\n422:     return false;\\n423:   }\\n424: \\n425:   // TODO(mattklein123): Consider per-fault type active fault gauges.\\n426:   config_->stats().active_faults_.inc();\\n427:   fault_active_ = true;\\n428: \\n429:   return true;\\n430: }\\n431: \\n432: void FaultFilter::onDestroy() {\\n433:   resetTimerState();\\n434:   if (response_limiter_ != nullptr) {\\n435:     response_limiter_->destroy();\\n436:   }\\n437:   if (fault_active_) {\\n438:     config_->stats().active_faults_.dec();\\n439:   }\\n440: }\\n441: \\n442: bool FaultFilter::isResponseRateLimitConfigured() {\\n443:   return fault_settings_->responseRateLimit() != nullptr;\\n444: }\\n445: \\n446: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/extensions/filters/http/fault/fault_filter.cc%3FL405-445\"}",

Note that this means that a\n418:   // single request might increment the counter more than once if it tries to apply multiple faults,\n419:   // and it is also possible for it to fail the first check then succeed on the second (should\n420:   // another thread decrement the active fault gauge).\n421:   if (faultOverflow()) {\n422:     return false;\n423:   }\n424: \n425:   // TODO(mattklein123): Consider per-fault type active fault gauges.\n426:   config_->stats().active_faults_.inc();\n427:   fault_active_ = true;\n428: \n429:   return true;\n430: }\n431: \n432: void FaultFilter::onDestroy() {\n433:   resetTimerState();\n434:   if (response_limiter_ != nullptr) {\n435:     response_limiter_->destroy();\n436:   }\n437:   if (fault_active_) {\n438:     config_->stats().active_faults_.dec();\n439:   }\n440: }\n441: \n442: bool FaultFilter::isResponseRateLimitConfigured() {\n443:   return fault_settings_->responseRateLimit() != nullptr;\n444: }\n445: \n446: ",

85. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git -C /workspace show HEAD:source/extensions/filters/http/fault/fault_filter.cc | sed -n '380,540p'",
  "description": "Read fault_filter.cc stats area"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "void FaultFilter::recordAbortsInjectedStats() {\n  // Downstream specific stats.\n  if (!downstream_cluster_.empty() && !fault_settings_->disableDownstreamClusterStats()) {\n    config_->incAborts(downstream_cluster_storage_->statName());\n  }\n\n  config_->stats().aborts_injected_.inc();\n}\n\nHttp::FilterDataStatus FaultFilter::decodeData(Buffer::Instance&, bool) {\n  if (delay_timer_ == nullptr) {\n    return Http::FilterDataStatus::Continue;\n  }\n  // If the request is too large, stop reading new data until the buffer drains.\n  return Http::FilterDataStatus::StopIterationAndWatermark;\n}\n\nHttp::FilterTrailersStatus FaultFilter::decodeTrailers(Http::RequestTrailerMap&) {\n  return delay_timer_ == nullptr ? Http::FilterTrailersStatus::Continue\n                                 : Http::FilterTrailersStatus::StopIteration;\n}\n\nFaultFilterStats FaultFilterConfig::generateStats(const std::string& prefix, Stats::Scope& scope) {\n  const std::string final_prefix = prefix + \"fault.\";\n  return {ALL_FAULT_FILTER_STATS(POOL_COUNTER_PREFIX(scope, final_prefix),\n                                 POOL_GAUGE_PREFIX(scope, final_prefix))};\n}\n\nbool FaultFilter::tryIncActiveFaults() {\n  // Only charge 1 active fault per filter in case we are injecting multiple faults.\n  // Since we count at most one active fault per filter, we also allow multiple faults\n  // per filter without checking for overflow.\n  if (fault_active_) {\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  // We only check for overflow when attempting to perform a fault. Note that this means that a\n  // single request might increment the counter more than once if it tries to apply multiple faults,\n  // and it is also possible for it to fail the first check then succeed on the second (should\n  // another thread decrement the active fault gauge).\n  if (faultOverflow()) {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  // TODO(mattklein123): Consider per-fault type active fault gauges.\n  config_->stats().active_faults_.inc();\n  fault_active_ = true;\n\n  return true;\n}\n\nvoid FaultFilter::onDestroy() {\n  resetTimerState();\n  if (response_limiter_ != nullptr) {\n    response_limiter_->destroy();\n  }\n  if (fault_active_) {\n    config_->stats().active_faults_.dec();\n  }\n}\n\nbool FaultFilter::isResponseRateLimitConfigured() {\n  return fault_settings_->responseRateLimit() != nullptr;\n}\n\nvoid FaultFilter::postDelayInjection(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\n  resetTimerState();\n\n  // Delays can be followed by aborts\n  absl::optional<Http::Code> http_status;\n  absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status;\n  std::tie(http_status, grpc_status) = abortStatus(request_headers);\n\n  if (http_status.has_value()) {\n    abortWithStatus(http_status.value(), grpc_status);\n  } else {\n    // Should not continue to count as an active fault after the delay has elapsed if no other type\n    // of fault is active. As the delay timer is always done at this point and followed abort faults\n    // have been checked earlier, here we just check if there's a response rate limit configured.\n    ASSERT(fault_active_);\n    ASSERT(delay_timer_ == nullptr);\n    if (!isResponseRateLimitConfigured()) {\n      config_->stats().active_faults_.dec();\n      fault_active_ = false;\n    }\n\n    // Continue request processing.\n    decoder_callbacks_->continueDecoding();\n  }\n}\n\nvoid FaultFilter::abortWithStatus(Http::Code http_status_code,\n                                  absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status) {\n  recordAbortsInjectedStats();\n  decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::FaultInjected);\n  auto& dynamic_metadata = decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().dynamicMetadata();\n  (*dynamic_metadata.mutable_filter_metadata())[decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName()] =\n      fault_settings_->filterMetadata();\n  decoder_callbacks_->sendLocalReply(http_status_code, \"fault filter abort\", nullptr, grpc_status,\n                                     RcDetails::get().FaultAbort);\n}\n\nbool FaultFilter::matchesTargetUpstreamCluster() {\n  bool matches = true;\n\n  if (!fault_settings_->upstreamCluster().empty()) {\n    Router::RouteConstSharedPtr route = decoder_callbacks_->route();\n    matches = route && route->routeEntry() &&\n              (route->routeEntry()->clusterName() == fault_settings_->upstreamCluster());\n  }\n\n  return matches;\n}\n\nbool FaultFilter::matchesDownstreamNodes(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\n  if (fault_settings_->downstreamNodes().empty()) {\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  if (!headers.EnvoyDownstreamServiceNode()) {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  const absl::string_view downstream_node = headers.getEnvoyDownstreamServiceNodeValue();\n  return fault_settings_->downstreamNodes().find(downstream_node) !=\n         fault_settings_->downstreamNodes().end();\n}\n\nvoid FaultFilter::resetTimerState() {\n  if (delay_timer_) {\n    delay_timer_->disableTimer();\n    delay_timer_.reset();\n  }\n}\n\nHttp::FilterDataStatus FaultFilter::encodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) {\n  if (response_limiter_ != nullptr) {\n    response_limiter_->writeData(data, end_stream);\n    return Http::FilterDataStatus::StopIterationNoBuffer;\n  }\n\n  return Http::FilterDataStatus::Continue;\n}\n\nHttp::FilterTrailersStatus FaultFilter::encodeTrailers(Http::ResponseTrailerMap&) {\n  if (response_limiter_ != nullptr) {\n    return response_limiter_->onTrailers() ? Http::FilterTrailersStatus::StopIteration\n                                           : Http::FilterTrailersStatus::Continue;\n  }\n\n  return Http::FilterTrailersStatus::Continue;\n}\n\n} // namespace Fault\n} // namespace HttpFilters\n} // namespace Extensions\n} // namespace Envoy"
}

== decoder_filters_.end() ||\\n582:          (*entry)->end_stream_);\\n583: \\n584:   for (; entry != decoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\\n585:     ENVOY_EXECUTION_SCOPE(trackedStream(), &(*entry)->filter_context_);\\n586:     ASSERT(!(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::DecodeHeaders));\\n587:     state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::DecodeHeaders;\\n588: ~FilterCallState::DecodeHeaders;\\n594:     if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\\n595:       state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::EndOfStream;\\n596:     }\\n597:     if (state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_) {\\n598:       executeLocalReplyIfPrepared();\\n599:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\\n600:                        \\\"decodeHeaders filter iteration aborted due to local reply: filter={}\\\",\\n601:                        *this, (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name);\\n602:       status = FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\\n603:     }\\n604: \\n605:     ASSERT(!(status == FilterHeadersStatus::ContinueAndDontEndStream && !(*entry)->end_stream_),\\n606:            \\\"Filters should not return FilterHeadersStatus::ContinueAndDontEndStream from \\\"\\n607:            \\\"decodeHeaders when end_stream is already false\\\");\\n608: \\n609:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"decode headers called: filter={} status={}\\\", *this,\\n610: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc%3FL555-620\"}",

    "content": "555:   } else {\n556:     parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.onRequestDataTooLarge();\n557:     sendLocalReply(Code::PayloadTooLarge, CodeUtility::toString(Code::PayloadTooLarge), nullptr,\n558:                    absl::nullopt, StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().RequestPayloadTooLarge);\n559:   }\n560: }\n561: \n562: void FilterManager::maybeContinueDecoding(StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator continue_data_entry) {\n563:   if (continue_data_entry != decoder_filters_.end()) {\n564:     // We use the continueDecoding() code since it will correctly handle not calling\n565:     // decodeHeaders() again. Fake setting StopSingleIteration since the continueDecoding() code\n566:     // expects it.\n567:     ASSERT(buffered_request_data_);\n568:     (*continue_data_entry)->iteration_state_ =\n569:         ActiveStreamFilterBase::IterationState::StopSingleIteration;\n570:     (*continue_data_entry)->continueDecoding();\n571:   }\n572: }\n573: \n574: void FilterManager::decodeHeaders(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* filter, RequestHeaderMap& headers,\n575:                                   bool end_stream) {\n576:   // Headers filter iteration should always start with the next filter if available.\n577:   StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator entry =\n578:       commonDecodePrefix(filter, FilterIterationStartState::AlwaysStartFromNext);\n579:   StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator continue_data_entry = decoder_filters_.end();\n580:   bool terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream = false;\n581:   ASSERT(!state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_ || entry == decoder_filters_.end() ||\n582:          (*entry)->end_stream_);\n583: \n584:   for (; entry != decoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\n585:     ENVOY_EXECUTION_SCOPE(trackedStream(), &(*entry)->filter_context_);\n586:     ASSERT(!(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::DecodeHeaders));\n587:     state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::DecodeHeaders;\n588:     (*entry)->end_stream_ = (end_stream && continue_data_entry == decoder_filters_.end());\n589:     if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\n590:       state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::EndOfStream;\n591:     }\n592:     FilterHeadersStatus status = (*entry)->decodeHeaders(headers, (*entry)->end_stream_);\n593:     state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::DecodeHeaders;\n594:     if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\n595:       state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::EndOfStream;\n596:     }\n597:     if (state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_) {\n598:       executeLocalReplyIfPrepared();\n599:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n600:                        \"decodeHeaders filter iteration aborted due to local reply: filter={}\",\n601:                        *this, (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name);\n602:       status = FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\n603:     }\n604: \n605:     ASSERT(!(status == FilterHeadersStatus::ContinueAndDontEndStream && !(*entry)->end_stream_),\n606:            \"Filters should not return FilterHeadersStatus::ContinueAndDontEndStream from \"\n607: but a filter adds a body\\n1317:     // to it.   continue_data_entry = entry;\\n1320:     }\\n1321:   }\\n1322: \\n1323:   // Check if the filter chain above did not remove critical headers or set malformed header values.\\n1324:   // We could do this at the codec in order to prevent other places than the filter chain from\\n1325:   // removing critical headers, but it will come with the implementation complexity.\\n1326:   // See the previous encoder_filters_.end());\\n1339:   state_.non_100_response_headers_encoded_ = true;\\n1340:   filter_manager_callbacks_.encodeHeaders(headers, modified_end_stream);\\n1341:   if (state_.saw_downstream_reset_) {\\n1342:     return;\\n1343:   }\\n1344:   maybeEndEncode(modified_end_stream);\\n1345: \\n1346:   if (!modified_end_stream) {\\n1347:   "content": "{\"content\":\"1540:   }\\n1541:   maybeEndEncode(true);\\n1542: }\\n1543: \\n1544: void FilterManager::maybeEndEncode(bool end_stream) {\\n1545:   if (end_stream) {\\n1546:     ASSERT(!state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_);\\n1547:     state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_ = true;\\n1548:     if (filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled()) {\\n1549:       // If independent half close is enabled the stream is closed when both decoder and encoder\\n1550:       // filter chains has completed or were aborted.\\n1551:       checkAndCloseStreamIfFullyClosed();\\n1552:     } else {\\n1553:       // Otherwise encoding end_stream always closes the stream (and resets it if request was not\\n1554:       // complete yet).\\n1555:       filter_manager_callbacks_.endStream();\\n1556:     }\\n1557:   }\\n1558: }\\n1559: \\n1560: void FilterManager::maybeEndDecode(bool terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream) {\\n1561:   if (terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream) {\\n1562:     ASSERT(!state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_);\\n1563:     state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_ = true;\\n1564:     // If the decoder filter chain was aborted (i.e. due to local reply)\\n1565:     // we rely on the encoding of end_stream to close the stream.\\n1566:     if (filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled() && !stopDecoderFilterChain()) {\\n1567:       checkAndCloseStreamIfFullyClosed();\\n1568:     }\\n1569:   }\\n1570: }\\n1571: \\n1572: void FilterManager::checkAndCloseStreamIfFullyClosed() {\\n1573:   ASSERT(filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled());\\n1574:   // When the independent half close is enabled the stream is always closed on error responses\\n1575:   // from the server.\\n1576:   if (filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders().has_value()) {\\n1577:     const uint64_t response_status =\\n1578:         Http::Utility::getResponseStatus(filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders().ref());\\n1579:     const bool error_response =\\n1580:         !(Http::CodeUtility::is2xx(response_status) || Http::CodeUtility::is1xx(response_status));\\n1581:     // Abort the decoder filter if it has not yet been completed.\\n1582:     if (error_response && !state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_) {\\n1583:       state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\\n1584:     }\\n1585:   }\\n1586: \\n1587:   // Handle the case where the end_stream was received from both downstream client and upstream\\n1588:   // server but a decoder filter added either request body or trailers AND paused decoder filter\\n1589:   // chain iteration. This case is currently handled the same way as if independent half-close is\\n1590:   // disabled, i.e. proxying is stopped as soon as encoding has finished.\\n1591:   // TODO(yanavlasov): to support this case the codec needs to notify HCM that it has closed its low\\n1592:   // level stream\\n1593:   //                   to avoid use-after-free when HCM cleans-up its ActiveStream\\n1594:   const bool downstream_client_sent_end_stream = decoderObservedEndStream();\\n1595:   const bool decoder_filter_chain_paused =\\n1596:       !state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_ && !state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_;\\n1597:   if (state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_ && downstream_client_sent_end_stream &&\\n1598:       decoder_filter_chain_paused) {\\n1599:     state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\\n1600:   }\\n1601: \\n1602:   // If independent half close is enabled then close the stream when\\n1603:   // 1. Both encoder and decoder filter chains has completed.\\n1604:   // 2. Encoder filter chain has completed and decoder filter chain was aborted (i.e. local reply).\\n1605:   // There is no need to check for aborted encoder filter chain as the filter will either be\\n1606:   // completed or stream is reset.\\n1607:   if (state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_ &&\\n1608:       (state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_ || state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_)) {\\n1609:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \\\"closing stream\\\", *this);\\n1610:     filter_manager_callbacks_.endStream();\\n1611:   }\\n1612: }\\n1613: \\n1614: bool FilterManager::processNewlyAddedMetadata() {\\n1615:   if (request_metadata_map_vector_ == nullptr) {\\n1616:     return false;\\n1617:   }\\n1618:   for (const auto& metadata_map : *getRequestMetadataMapVector()) {\\n1619:     decodeMetadata(nullptr, *metadata_map);\\n1620:   }\\n1621: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc%3FL1540-1620\"}",
  "structuredContent": {

half close is enabled the stream is closed when both decoder and encoder\n1550:       // filter chains has completed or were aborted.\n1551:       checkAndCloseStreamIfFullyClosed();\n1552:     } else Http::CodeUtility::is1xx(response_status));\n1581:     // Abort the decoder filter if it has not yet been completed.\n1582:     if (error_response && !state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_) {\n1583:       state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n1584:     }\n1585:   }\n1586: \n1587:   // Handle the case where the end_stream was received from both downstream client and upstream\n1588:   // server but a decoder filter added either request body or trailers AND paused decoder filter\n1589:   // chain iteration. This case is currently handled the same way as if independent half-close is\n1590:   // disabled, i.e. proxying is stopped as soon as encoding has finished.\n1591:   // TODO(yanavlasov): to support this case the codec needs to notify HCM that it has closed its low\n1592:   // level stream\n1593:   //                   to avoid use-after-free when HCM cleans-up its ActiveStream\n1594:   const bool downstream_client_sent_end_stream = decoderObservedEndStream();\n1595:   const bool decoder_filter_chain_paused =\n1596:       !state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_ && !state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_;\n1597:   if (state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_ && downstream_client_sent_end_stream &&\n1598:       decoder_filter_chain_paused) {\n1599:     state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n1600:   }\n1601: \n1602:   // If independent half close is enabled then close the stream when\n1603:   // 1. Both encoder and decoder filter chains has completed.\n1604:   // 2. Encoder filter chain has completed and decoder filter chain was aborted (i.e. local reply).\n1605:   // There is no need to check for aborted encoder filter chain as the filter will either be\n1606:   // completed or stream is reset.\n1607:   if (state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_ &&\n1608:       (state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_ || state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_)) {\n1609:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"closing stream\", *this);\n1610:     filter_manager_callbacks_.endStream();\n1611:   }\n1612: }\n1613: \n1614: bool FilterManager::processNewlyAddedMetadata() {\n1615:   if (request_metadata_map_vector_ == nullptr) {\n1616:     return false;\n1617:   }\n1618:   for (const auto& metadata_map : *getRequestMetadataMapVector()) {\n1619:     decodeMetadata(nullptr, *metadata_map);\n1620:   }\n1621: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc%3FL1540-1620"
  }
}

91. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/common/http/filter_manager.cc | sed -n '339,370p'",
  "description": "Read canContinue functions from filter_manager.cc"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "bool ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::canContinue() {\n  // It is possible for the connection manager to respond directly to a request even while\n  // a filter is trying to continue. If a response has already happened, we should not\n  // continue to further filters. A concrete example of this is a filter buffering data, the\n  // last data frame comes in and the filter continues, but the final buffering takes the stream\n  // over the high watermark such that a 413 is returned.\n  return !parent_.stopDecoderFilterChain();\n}\n\nbool ActiveStreamEncoderFilter::canContinue() {\n  // As with ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::canContinue() make sure we do not\n  // continue if a local reply has been sent or ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::recreateStream() is\n  // called, etc.\n  return !parent_.state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_ &&\n         (!Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n              \"envoy.reloadable_features.filter_chain_aborted_can_not_continue\") ||\n          !parent_.stopEncoderFilterChain());\n}\n\nBuffer::InstancePtr ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::createBuffer() {\n  auto buffer = dispatcher().getWatermarkFactory().createBuffer(\n      [this]() -> void { this->requestDataDrained(); },\n      [this]() -> void { this->requestDataTooLarge(); },\n      []() -> void { /* TODO(adisuissa): Handle overflow watermark */ });\n  buffer->setWatermarks(parent_.buffer_limit_);\n  return buffer;\n}\n\nBuffer::InstancePtr& ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::bufferedData() {\n  return parent_.buffered_request_data_;\n}"
}

}

97. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/common/http/filter_manager.cc | sed -n '985,1005p'",
  "description": "Check line 993 context in filter_manager.cc HEAD"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Resetting stream due to {}. onLocalReply requested reset.\", *this,\n                     details);\n    filter_manager_callbacks_.resetStream(Http::StreamResetReason::LocalReset, \"\");\n    return;\n  }\n\n  if (!filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders().has_value() &&\n      (!filter_manager_callbacks_.informationalHeaders().has_value() ||\n       (Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n            \"envoy.reloadable_features.local_reply_traverses_filter_chain_after_1xx\") &&\n        !(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsEncodingMask)))) {\n    // If the response has not started at all, or if the only response so far is an informational\n    // 1xx that has already been fully processed, send the response through the filter chain.\n\n    if (auto cb = filter_manager_callbacks_.downstreamCallbacks(); cb.has_value()) {\n      // The initial route maybe never be set or the cached route maybe cleared by the filters.\n      // This will force route refreshment if there is not a cached route to avoid potential\n      // route refreshment in the response filter chain.\n      cb->route(nullptr);\n    }"
}

102. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/common/http/filter_manager.cc | sed -n '980,1010p'",
  "description": "Look at the sendLocalReply function in HEAD filter_manager.cc"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "  streamInfo().setResponseCodeDetails(details);\n  StreamFilterBase::LocalReplyData data{code, grpc_status, details, false};\n  onLocalReply(data);\n  if (data.reset_imminent_) {\n    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Resetting stream due to {}. onLocalReply requested reset.\", *this,\n                     details);\n    filter_manager_callbacks_.resetStream(Http::StreamResetReason::LocalReset, \"\");\n    return;\n  }\n\n  if (!filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders().has_value() &&\n      (!filter_manager_callbacks_.informationalHeaders().has_value() ||\n       (Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n            \"envoy.reloadable_features.local_reply_traverses_filter_chain_after_1xx\") &&\n        !(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsEncodingMask)))) {\n    // If the response has not started at all, or if the only response so far is an informational\n    // 1xx that has already been fully processed, send the response through the filter chain.\n\n    if (auto cb = filter_manager_callbacks_.downstreamCallbacks(); cb.has_value()) {\n      // The initial route maybe never be set or the cached route maybe cleared by the filters.\n      // This will force route refreshment if there is not a cached route to avoid potential\n      // route refreshment in the response filter chain.\n      cb->route(nullptr);\n    }\n\n    // We only prepare a local reply to execute later if we're actively\n    // invoking filters to avoid re-entrant in filters.\n    if (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsDecodingMask) {\n      prepareLocalReplyViaFilterChain(is_grpc_request, code, body, modify_headers, is_head_request,\n                                      grpc_status, details);"
}

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"385: }\\n386: \\n387: bool ActiveStreamEncoderFilter::canContinue() {\\n388:   // As with ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::canContinue() make sure we do not\\n389:   // continue if a local reply has been sent or ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::recreateStream() is\\n390:   // called, etc.\\n391:   return !parent_.state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_ && !parent_.stopEncoderFilterChain();\\n392: }\\n393: \\n394: Buffer::InstancePtr ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::createBuffer() {\\n395:   auto buffer = dispatcher().getWatermarkFactory().createBuffer(\\n396:       [this]() -> void { this->requestDataDrained(); },\\n397:       [this]() -> void { this->requestDataTooLarge(); },\\n398:       []() -> void { /* TODO(adisuissa): Handle overflow watermark */ });\\n399:   buffer->setWatermarks(parent_.buffer_limit_);\\n400:   return buffer;\\n401: }\\n402: \\n403: Buffer::InstancePtr& ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::bufferedData() {\\n404:   return parent_.buffered_request_data_;\\n405: }\\n406: \\n407: bool ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::observedEndStream() { return parent_.decoderObservedEndStream(); }\\n408: \\n409: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::doHeaders(bool end_stream) {\\n410:   parent_.decodeHeaders(this, *parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.requestHeaders(), end_stream);\\n411: }\\n412: \\n413: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::doData(bool end_stream) {\\n414:   parent_.decodeData(this, *parent_.buffered_request_data_, end_stream,\\n415:                      FilterManager::FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent);\\n416: }\\n417: \\n418: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::doTrailers() {\\n419:   parent_.decodeTrailers(this, *parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.requestTrailers());\\n420: }\\n421: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc%3FL385-420\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "385: }\n386: \n387: bool ActiveStreamEncoderFilter::canContinue() {\n388:   // As with ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::canContinue() make sure we do not\n389:   // continue if a local reply has been sent or ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::recreateStream() is\n390:   // called, etc.\n391:   return !parent_.state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_ && !parent_.stopEncoderFilterChain();\n392: }\n393: \n394: Buffer::InstancePtr ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::createBuffer() {\n395:   auto buffer = dispatcher().getWatermarkFactory().createBuffer(\n396:       [this]() -> void { this->requestDataDrained(); },\n397:       [this]() -> void { this->requestDataTooLarge(); },\n398:       []() -> void { /* TODO(adisuissa): Handle overflow watermark */ });\n399:   buffer->setWatermarks(parent_.buffer_limit_);\n400:   return buffer;\n401: }\n402: \n403: Buffer::InstancePtr& ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::bufferedData() {\n404:   return parent_.buffered_request_data_;\n405: }\n406: \n407: bool ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::observedEndStream() { return parent_.decoderObservedEndStream(); }\n408: \n409: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::doHeaders(bool end_stream) {\n410:   parent_.decodeHeaders(this, *parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.requestHeaders(), end_stream);\n411: }\n412: \n413: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::doData(bool end_stream) {\n414:   parent_.decodeData(this, *parent_.buffered_request_data_, end_stream,\n415:                      FilterManager::FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent);\n416: }\n417: \n418: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::doTrailers() {\n419:   parent_.decodeTrailers(this, *parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.requestTrailers());\n420: }\n421: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc%3FL385-420"

107. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 50,
  "path": "changelogs/1.33.0.yaml",
  "repo": "github.com/envoyproxy/envoy",
  "startLine": 30
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"30:     rather than waiting for the original request to complete. This allows shadowing requests larger than the buffer limit,\\n31:     but also means shadowing may take place for requests which are cancelled mid-stream. This behavior change can be\\n32:     temporarily reverted by flipping ``envoy.reloadable_features.streaming_shadow`` to ``false``.\\n33: - area: http\\n34:   change: |\\n35:     If the ``encoder_filter_chain_aborted_`` status bit of the HTTP filter manager is set to true, the encode filter chain\\n36:     cannot be continued, thus preventing unexpected logic from being triggered in scenarios such as\\n37:     ``ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::recreateStream()`` is called. This behavior change can be temporarily reverted by flipping\\n38:     ``envoy.reloadable_features.filter_chain_aborted_can_not_continue`` to ``false``.\\n39: \\n40: minor_behavior_changes:\\n41: - area: access_log\\n42:   change: |\\n43:     New implementation of the JSON formatter will be enabled by default.\\n44:     The :ref:`sort_properties <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.JsonFormatOptions.sort_properties>` field will\\n45:     be ignored in the new implementation because the new implementation always sorts properties, and the new implementation\\n46:     will always keep the value type in the JSON output. For example, the ``duration`` field will always be rendered as a number\\n47:     instead of a string.\\n48:     This behavior change could be disabled temporarily by setting the runtime\\n49:     ``envoy.reloadable_features.logging_with_fast_json_formatter`` to false.\\n50: - area: xds\\n51: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/changelogs/1.33.0.yaml%3FL30-50\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "30:     rather than waiting for the original request to complete. This allows shadowing requests larger than the buffer limit,\n31:     but also means shadowing may take place for requests which are cancelled mid-stream. This behavior change can be\n32:     temporarily reverted by flipping ``envoy.reloadable_features.streaming_shadow`` to ``false``.\n33: - area: http\n34:   change: |\n35:     If the ``encoder_filter_chain_aborted_`` status bit of the HTTP filter manager is set to true, the encode filter chain\n36:     cannot be continued, thus preventing unexpected logic from being triggered in scenarios such as\n37:     ``ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::recreateStream()`` is called. This behavior change can be temporarily reverted by flipping\n38:     ``envoy.reloadable_features.filter_chain_aborted_can_not_continue`` to ``false``.\n39: \n40: minor_behavior_changes:\n41: - area: access_log\n42:   change: |\n43:     New implementation of the JSON formatter will be enabled by default.\n44:     The :ref:`sort_properties <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.JsonFormatOptions.sort_properties>` field will\n45:     be ignored in the new implementation because the new implementation always sorts properties, and the new implementation\n46:     will always keep the value type in the JSON output. For example, the ``duration`` field will always be rendered as a number\n47:     instead of a string.\n48:     This behavior change could be disabled temporarily by setting the runtime\n49:     ``envoy.reloadable_features.logging_with_fast_json_formatter`` to false.\n50: - area: xds\n51: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/changelogs/1.33.0.yaml%3FL30-50"
  }
}

  "stdout": "#pragma once\n\nnamespace Envoy {\n\n/**\n * @return the size of a C array.\n */\n#define ARRAY_SIZE(X) (sizeof(X) / sizeof(X[0]))\n\n/**\n * @return the length of a static string literal, e.g. STATIC_STRLEN(\"foo\") == 3.\n */\n#define STATIC_STRLEN(X) (sizeof(X) - 1)\n\n/**\n * Helper macros from enum to string macros.\n */\n#define GENERATE_ENUM(X) X,\n#define GENERATE_STRING(X) #X,\n\n/**\n * Stop the compiler from complaining about an unreferenced parameter.\n */\n#ifndef WIN32\n#define UNREFERENCED_PARAMETER(X) ((void)(X))\n#endif\n\n/**\n * Construct On First Use idiom.\n * See https://isocpp.org/wiki/faq/ctors#static-init-order-on-first-use.\n */\n#define CONSTRUCT_ON_FIRST_USE(type, ...)                                                          \\\n  do {                                                                                             \\\n    static const type* objectptr = new type{__VA_ARGS__};                                          \\\n    return *objectptr;                                                                             \\\n  } while (0)\n\n#define MUTABLE_CONSTRUCT_ON_FIRST_USE(type, ...)                                                  \\\n  do {                                                                                             \\\n    static type* objectptr = new type{__VA_ARGS__};                                                \\"
}

133. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc | sed -n '550,650p'",
  "description": "Read same section of ext_authz.cc in HEAD"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "        return;\n      }\n    }\n    for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_append) {\n      CheckResult check_result = validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\n          Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation::APPEND, key, value);\n      switch (check_result) {\n      case CheckResult::OK: {\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\n        Http::LowerCaseString lowercase_key(key);\n        const auto header_to_modify = request_headers_->get(lowercase_key);\n        // TODO(dio): Add a flag to allow appending non-existent headers, without setting it\n        // first (via `headers_to_add`). For example, given:\n        // 1. Original headers {\"original\": \"true\"}\n        // 2. Response headers from the authorization servers {{\"append\": \"1\"}, {\"append\":\n        // \"2\"}}\n        //\n        // Currently it is not possible to add {{\"append\": \"1\"}, {\"append\": \"2\"}} (the\n        // intended combined headers: {{\"original\": \"true\"}, {\"append\": \"1\"}, {\"append\":\n        // \"2\"}}) to the request to upstream server by only sets `headers_to_append`.\n        if (!header_to_modify.empty()) {\n          ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\n          // The current behavior of appending is by combining entries with the same key,\n          // into one entry. The value of that combined entry is separated by \",\".\n          // TODO(dio): Consider to use addCopy instead.\n          request_headers_->appendCopy(lowercase_key, value);\n        }\n        break;\n      }\n      case CheckResult::IGNORE:\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring invalid header to append '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n                         key, value);\n        break;\n      case CheckResult::FAIL:\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejecting invalid header to append '{}':'{}'.\",\n                         *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\n        rejectResponse();\n        return;\n      }\n    }\n\n    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n                     \"ext_authz filter removed header(s) from the request:\", *decoder_callbacks_);\n    for (const auto& key : response->headers_to_remove) {\n      // If the response contains an invalid header to remove, it's the same as trying to remove a\n      // header that doesn't exist, so just ignore it.\n      if (config_->validateMutations() && !Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key)) {\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring invalid header removal '{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_, key);\n        continue;\n      }\n      // We don't allow removing any :-prefixed headers, nor Host, as removing them would make the\n      // request malformed. checkDecoderHeaderMutation also performs this check, however, so only\n      // perform this check explicitly if decoder header mutation rules is empty.\n      if (!config_->hasDecoderHeaderMutationRules() &&\n          !Http::HeaderUtility::isRemovableHeader(key)) {\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring invalid header removal '{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_, key);\n        continue;\n      }\n      // Check header mutation is valid according to configured decoder header mutation rules.\n      Http::LowerCaseString lowercase_key(key);\n      switch (config_->checkDecoderHeaderMutation(CheckOperation::REMOVE, lowercase_key,\n                                                  EMPTY_STRING)) {\n      case CheckResult::OK:\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key);\n        request_headers_->remove(lowercase_key);\n        break;\n      case CheckResult::IGNORE:\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring disallowed header removal '{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n                         key);\n        break;\n      case CheckResult::FAIL:\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejecting disallowed header removal '{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n                         key);\n        rejectResponse();\n        return;\n      }\n    }\n\n    if response->response_headers_to_add.size());\n      for (const auto& [key, value] : response->response_headers_to_add) {\n        if (config_->validateMutations() && (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\n                                             !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value))) {\n          ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejected invalid header '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_, key,\n                           value);\n          rejectResponse();\n          return;\n        }\n      }\n      response_headers_to_add_ = Http::HeaderVector{response->response_headers_to_add.begin(),\n                                                    response->response_headers_to_add.end()};\n    }\n\n    if (!response->response_headers_to_set.empty()) {\n      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"ext_authz filter saving {} header(s) to set to the response:\",\n                       *decoder_callbacks_, response->response_headers_to_set.size());\n      for (const auto& [key, value] : response->response_headers_to_set) {\n        if (config_->validateMutations() && 136. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc | sed -n '470,540p'",
  "description": "Read the onComplete function from ext_authz.cc HEAD"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "void Filter::onComplete(Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponsePtr&& response) {\n  state_ = State::Complete;\n  using Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus;\n  Stats::StatName empty_stat_name;\n\n  updateLoggingInfo();\n\n  if (!response->dynamic_metadata.fields().empty()) {\n    if (!config_->enableDynamicMetadataIngestion()) {\n      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n                       \"Response is trying to inject dynamic metadata, but dynamic metadata \"\n                       \"ingestion is disabled. Ignoring...\",\n                       *decoder_callbacks_);\n      stats_.ignored_dynamic_metadata_.inc();\n    } else {\n      // Add duration of call to dynamic metadata if applicable\n      if (start_time_.has_value() && response->status == CheckStatus::OK) {\n        ProtobufWkt::Value ext_authz_duration_value;\n        auto duration =\n            decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime() - start_time_.value();\n        ext_authz_duration_value.set_number_value(\n            std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(duration).count());\n        (*response->dynamic_metadata.mutable_fields())[\"ext_authz_duration\"] =\n            ext_authz_duration_value;\n      }\n      decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setDynamicMetadata(\"envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\",\n                                                          response->dynamic_metadata);\n    }\n  }\n\n  switch (response->status) {\n  case CheckStatus::OK: {\n    // Any changes to request headers or query parameters can affect how the request is going to be\n    // routed. If we are changing the headers we also need to clear the route\n    // cache.\n    if (config_->clearRouteCache() &&\n        (!response->headers_to_set.empty() || !response->headers_to_append.empty() ||\n         !response->headers_to_remove.empty() || !response->query_parameters_to_set.empty() ||\n         !response->query_parameters_to_remove.empty())) {\n      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"ext_authz is clearing route cache\", *decoder_callbacks_);\n      decoder_callbacks_->downstreamCallbacks()->clearRouteCache();\n    }\n\n    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n                     \"ext_authz filter added header(s) to the request:\", *decoder_callbacks_);\n    for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_set) {\n      CheckResult check_result = validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\n          Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation::SET, key, value);\n      switch (check_result) {\n      case CheckResult::OK:\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\n        request_headers_->setCopy(Http::LowerCaseString(key), value);\n        break;\n      case CheckResult::IGNORE:\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring invalid header to set '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n                         key, value);\n        break;\n      case CheckResult::FAIL:\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejecting invalid header to set '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n                         key, value);\n        rejectResponse();\n        return;\n      }\n    }\n    for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_add) {\n      CheckResult check_result = validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\n          Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation::SET, key, value);\n      switch (check_result) {\n      case CheckResult::OK:\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\n        request_headers_->addCopy(Http::LowerCaseString(key), value);"
}

  "command": "git show HEAD:source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc | sed -n '400,435p'",
  "description": "Read updateLoggingInfo function in HEAD ext_authz.cc"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "Http::FilterTrailersStatus Filter::encodeTrailers(Http::ResponseTrailerMap&) {\n  return Http::FilterTrailersStatus::Continue;\n}\n\nHttp::FilterMetadataStatus Filter::encodeMetadata(Http::MetadataMap&) {\n  return Http::FilterMetadataStatus::Continue;\n}\n\nvoid Filter::setDecoderFilterCallbacks(Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) {\n  decoder_callbacks_ = &callbacks;\n}\n\nvoid Filter::updateLoggingInfo() {\n  if (!config_->emitFilterStateStats()) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  if (logging_info_ == nullptr) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Latency is the only stat available if we aren't using envoy grpc.\n  if (start_time_.has_value()) {\n    logging_info_->setLatency(std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::microseconds>(\n        decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime() - start_time_.value()));\n  }\n\n  auto const* stream_info = client_->streamInfo();\n  if (stream_info == nullptr) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const auto& bytes_meter = stream_info->getUpstreamBytesMeter();\n  if (bytes_meter != nullptr) {\n    logging_info_->setBytesSent(bytes_meter->wireBytesSent());"
}

\\n589:   auto const* stream_info = client_->streamInfo();\\n590:   if (stream_info == nullptr) {\\n591:     return;\\n592:   }\\n593: \\n594:   const auto& bytes_meter = stream_info->getUpstreamBytesMeter();\\n595:   if (bytes_meter != nullptr) {\\n596:     logging_info_->setBytesSent(bytes_meter->wireBytesSent());\\n597: 148. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz.h | sed -n '86,135p'",
  "description": "Read the Response struct definition from ext_authz.h"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "struct Response {\n  // Call status.\n  CheckStatus status;\n  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, that will be optionally appended\n  // to the request to the upstream server.\n  UnsafeHeaderVector headers_to_append;\n  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally set\n  // (using \"setCopy\") to the request to the upstream server.\n  UnsafeHeaderVector headers_to_set;\n  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally added\n  // (using \"addCopy\") to the request to the upstream server.\n  UnsafeHeaderVector headers_to_add;\n  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally added\n  // (using \"addCopy\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth\n  // responses.\n  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_add;\n  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally set (using\n  // \"setCopy\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth responses.\n  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_set;\n  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally added\n  // (using \"addCopy\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth\n  // responses only if the headers were not returned from the authz server.\n  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_add_if_absent;\n  // A set of HTTP headers returned by the authorization server, will be optionally set (using\n  // \"setCopy\") to the response sent back to the downstream client on OK auth responses\n  // only if the headers were returned from the authz server.\n  UnsafeHeaderVector response_headers_to_overwrite_if_exists;\n  // A set of HTTP headers consumed by the authorization server, will be removed\n  // from the request to the upstream server.\n  std::vector<std::string> headers_to_remove;\n  // A set of query string parameters to be set (possibly overwritten) on the\n  // request to the upstream server.\n  Http::Utility::QueryParamsVector query_parameters_to_set;\n  // A set of query string parameters to remove from the request to the upstream server.\n  std::vector<std::string> query_parameters_to_remove;\n  // Optional http body used only on denied response.\n  std::string body;\n  // Optional http status used only on denied response.\n  Http::Code status_code{};\n\n  // A set of metadata returned by the authorization server, that will be emitted as filter's\n  // dynamic metadata that other filters can leverage.\n  ProtobufWkt::Struct dynamic_metadata;\n};\n\nusing ResponsePtr = std::unique_ptr<Response>;\n\n/**\n * Async callbacks used during check() calls.\n */"
}

Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/extensions/filters/http/fault/fault_filter.cc | sed -n '120,200p'",
  "description": "Read decodeHeaders and related functions from HEAD fault_filter.cc"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "  if (!matchesTargetUpstreamCluster()) {\n    return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\n  }\n\n  if (!matchesDownstreamNodes(headers)) {\n    return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\n  }\n\n  // Check for header matches\n  if (!Http::HeaderUtility::matchHeaders(headers, fault_settings_->filterHeaders())) {\n    return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\n  }\n\n  if (headers.EnvoyDownstreamServiceCluster()) {\n    downstream_cluster_ = std::string(headers.getEnvoyDownstreamServiceClusterValue());\n    if (!downstream_cluster_.empty() && !fault_settings_->disableDownstreamClusterStats()) {\n      downstream_cluster_storage_ = std::make_unique<Stats::StatNameDynamicStorage>(\n          downstream_cluster_, config_->scope().symbolTable());\n    }\n\n    downstream_cluster_delay_percent_key_ =\n        fmt::format(\"fault.http.{}.delay.fixed_delay_percent\", downstream_cluster_);\n    downstream_cluster_abort_percent_key_ =\n        fmt::format(\"fault.http.{}.abort.abort_percent\", downstream_cluster_);\n    downstream_cluster_delay_duration_key_ =\n        fmt::format(\"fault.http.{}.delay.fixed_duration_ms\", downstream_cluster_);\n    downstream_cluster_abort_http_status_key_ =\n        fmt::format(\"fault.http.{}.abort.http_status\", downstream_cluster_);\n    downstream_cluster_abort_grpc_status_key_ =\n        fmt::format(\"fault.http.{}.abort.grpc_status\", downstream_cluster_);\n  }\n\n  maybeSetupResponseRateLimit(headers);\n\n  if (maybeSetupDelay(headers)) {\n    return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\n  }\n\n  if (maybeDoAbort(headers)) {\n    return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\n  }\n\n  return Http::FilterHeadersStatus::Continue;\n}\n\nbool FaultFilter::maybeSetupDelay(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\n  absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds> duration = delayDuration(request_headers);\n  if (duration.has_value() && tryIncActiveFaults()) {\n    delay_timer_ = decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher().createTimer(\n        [this, &request_headers]() -> void { postDelayInjection(request_headers); });\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"fault: delaying request {}ms\", duration.value().count());\n    delay_timer_->enableTimer(duration.value(), &decoder_callbacks_->scope());\n    recordDelaysInjectedStats();\n    decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::DelayInjected);\n    auto& dynamic_metadata = decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().dynamicMetadata();\n    (*dynamic_metadata.mutable_filter_metadata())[decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName()] =\n        fault_settings_->filterMetadata();\n    return true;\n  }\n  return false;\n}\n\nbool FaultFilter::maybeDoAbort(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\n  absl::optional<Http::Code> http_status;\n  absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status;\n  std::tie(http_status, grpc_status) = abortStatus(request_headers);\n\n  if (http_status.has_value() && tryIncActiveFaults()) {\n    abortWithStatus(http_status.value(), grpc_status);\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  return false;\n}\n\nvoid FaultFilter::maybeSetupResponseRateLimit(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\n  if (!isResponseRateLimitEnabled(request_headers)) {\n    return;\n  }"
}

duration = delayDuration(request_headers);\\n168:   if (duration.has_value() && tryIncActiveFaults()) {\\n169:     delay_timer_ = decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher().createTimer(\\n170:         [this, 155. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/extensions/filters/http/fault/fault_filter.cc | sed -n '409,480p'",
  "description": "Read tryIncActiveFaults, onDestroy, postDelayInjection from HEAD"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "bool FaultFilter::tryIncActiveFaults() {\n  // Only charge 1 active fault per filter in case we are injecting multiple faults.\n  // Since we count at most one active fault per filter, we also allow multiple faults\n  // per filter without checking for overflow.\n  if (fault_active_) {\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  // We only check for overflow when attempting to perform a fault. Note that this means that a\n  // single request might increment the counter more than once if it tries to apply multiple faults,\n  // and it is also possible for it to fail the first check then succeed on the second (should\n  // another thread decrement the active fault gauge).\n  if (faultOverflow()) {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  // TODO(mattklein123): Consider per-fault type active fault gauges.\n  config_->stats().active_faults_.inc();\n  fault_active_ = true;\n\n  return true;\n}\n\nvoid FaultFilter::onDestroy() {\n  resetTimerState();\n  if (response_limiter_ != nullptr) {\n    response_limiter_->destroy();\n  }\n  if (fault_active_) {\n    config_->stats().active_faults_.dec();\n  }\n}\n\nbool FaultFilter::isResponseRateLimitConfigured() {\n  return fault_settings_->responseRateLimit() != nullptr;\n}\n\nvoid FaultFilter::postDelayInjection(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\n  resetTimerState();\n\n  // Delays can be followed by aborts\n  absl::optional<Http::Code> http_status;\n  absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status;\n  std::tie(http_status, grpc_status) = abortStatus(request_headers);\n\n  if (http_status.has_value()) {\n    abortWithStatus(http_status.value(), grpc_status);\n  } else {\n    // Should not continue to count as an active fault after the delay has elapsed if no other type\n    // of fault is active. As the delay timer is always done at this point and followed abort faults\n    // have been checked earlier, here we just check if there's a response rate limit configured.\n    ASSERT(fault_active_);\n    ASSERT(delay_timer_ == nullptr);\n    if (!isResponseRateLimitConfigured()) {\n      config_->stats().active_faults_.dec();\n      fault_active_ = false;\n    }\n\n    // Continue request processing.\n    decoder_callbacks_->continueDecoding();\n  }\n}\n\nvoid FaultFilter::abortWithStatus(Http::Code http_status_code,\n                                  absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status) {\n  recordAbortsInjectedStats();\n  decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::FaultInjected);\n  auto& dynamic_metadata = decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().dynamicMetadata();\n  (*dynamic_metadata.mutable_filter_metadata())[decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName()] =\n      fault_settings_->filterMetadata();\n  decoder_callbacks_->sendLocalReply(http_status_code, \"fault filter abort\", nullptr, grpc_status,\n                                     RcDetails::get().FaultAbort);"
}
