As the delay timer is always done at this point and followed abort faults\\n459:     // have been checked earlier, here we just check if there's a response rate limit configured.\\n460:     ASSERT(fault_active_);\\n461:     ASSERT(delay_timer_ == nullptr);\\n462:     if (!isResponseRateLimitConfigured()) {\\n463:       config_->stats().active_faults_.dec();\\n464:       fault_active_ = false;\\n465:     }\\n466: \\n467:     // Continue request processing.\\n468:     decoder_callbacks_->continueDecoding();\\n469:   }\\n470: }\\n471: \\n472: void FaultFilter::abortWithStatus(Http::Code http_status_code,\\n473:                                   absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status) {\\n474:   recordAbortsInjectedStats();\\n475:   decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::FaultInjected);\\n476: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/extensions/filters/http/fault/fault_filter.cc%3FL408-475\"}",

As the delay timer is always done at this point and followed abort faults\n459:     // have been checked earlier, here we just check if there's a response rate limit configured.\n460:     ASSERT(fault_active_);\n461:     ASSERT(delay_timer_ == nullptr);\n462:     if (!isResponseRateLimitConfigured()) {\n463:       config_->stats().active_faults_.dec();\n464:       fault_active_ = false;\n465:     }\n466: \n467:     // Continue request processing.\n468:     decoder_callbacks_->continueDecoding();\n469:   }\n470: }\n471: \n472: void FaultFilter::abortWithStatus(Http::Code http_status_code,\n473:                                   absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status) {\n474:   recordAbortsInjectedStats();\n475:   decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::FaultInjected);\n476: ",

Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "bool HeaderUtility::matchHeaders(const HeaderMap& request_headers,\n                                 const std::vector<HeaderDataPtr>& config_headers) {\n  // No headers to match is considered a match.\n  if (!config_headers.empty()) {\n    for (const HeaderDataPtr& cfg_header_data : config_headers) {\n      if (!cfg_header_data->matchesHeaders(request_headers)) {\n        return false;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  return true;\n}\n\nHeaderUtility::GetAllOfHeaderAsStringResult\nHeaderUtility::getAllOfHeaderAsString(const HeaderMap::GetResult& header_value,\n                                      absl::string_view separator) {\n  GetAllOfHeaderAsStringResult result;\n  // In this case we concatenate all found headers using a delimiter before performing the\n  // final match. We use an InlinedVector of absl::string_view to invoke the optimized join\n  // algorithm. This requires a copying phase before we invoke join. The 3 used as the inline\n  // size has been arbitrarily chosen.\n  // TODO(mattklein123): Do we need to normalize any whitespace here?\n  absl::InlinedVector<absl::string_view, 3> string_view_vector;\n  string_view_vector.reserve(header_value.size());\n  for (size_t i = 0; i < header_value.size(); i++) {\n    string_view_vector.push_back(header_value[i]->value().getStringView());\n  }\n  result.result_backing_string_ = absl::StrJoin(string_view_vector, separator);\n\n  return result;\n}\n\nHeaderUtility::GetAllOfHeaderAsStringResult\nHeaderUtility::getAllOfHeaderAsString(const HeaderMap& headers, const Http::LowerCaseString& key,\n                                      absl::string_view separator) {\n  GetAllOfHeaderAsStringResult result;\n  const auto header_value = headers.get(key);\n\n  if (header_value.empty()) {\n    // Empty for clarity. Avoid handling the empty case in the block below if the runtime feature\n    // is disabled.\n  } else if (header_value.size() == 1) {\n    result.result_ = header_value[0]->value().getStringView();\n  } else {\n    return getAllOfHeaderAsString(header_value, separator);\n  }\n\n  return result;\n}\n\nHeaderUtility::HeaderDataPtr\nHeaderUtility::createHeaderData(const envoy::config::route::v3::HeaderMatcher& config,\n                                Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& factory_context) {\n  switch (config.header_match_specifier_case()) {\n  case envoy::config::route::v3::HeaderMatcher::HeaderMatchSpecifierCase::kExactMatch:\n    return std::make_unique<HeaderDataExactMatch>(config);\n    break;"
}

  "stdout": "Http::Status HeaderUtility::checkRequiredResponseHeaders(const Http::ResponseHeaderMap& headers) {\n  const absl::optional<uint64_t> status = Utility::getResponseStatusOrNullopt(headers);\n  if (!status.has_value()) {\n    return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n        absl::StrCat(\"missing required header: \", Envoy::Http::Headers::get().Status.get()));\n  }\n  return Http::okStatus();\n}\n\nbool HeaderUtility::isRemovableHeader(absl::string_view header) {\n  return (header.empty() || header[0] != ':') &&\n         !absl::EqualsIgnoreCase(header, Headers::get().HostLegacy.get());\n}\n\nbool HeaderUtility::isModifiableHeader(absl::string_view header) {\n  return (header.empty() || header[0] != ':') &&\n         !absl::EqualsIgnoreCase(header, Headers::get().HostLegacy.get());\n}\n\nHeaderUtility::HeaderValidationResult HeaderUtility::checkHeaderNameForUnderscores(\n    absl::string_view header_name,\n    envoy::config::core::v3::HttpProtocolOptions::HeadersWithUnderscoresAction\n        headers_with_underscores_action,\n    HeaderValidatorStats& stats) {\n  if (headers_with_underscores_action == envoy::config::core::v3::HttpProtocolOptions::ALLOW ||\n      !HeaderUtility::headerNameContainsUnderscore(header_name)) {\n    return HeaderValidationResult::ACCEPT;\n  }\n  if (headers_with_underscores_action =="
}

165. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/common/http/filter_manager.cc | sed -n '339,360p'",
  "description": "Read both canContinue functions from filter_manager.cc HEAD"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "bool ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::canContinue() {\n  // It is possible for the connection manager to respond directly to a request even while\n  // a filter is trying to continue. If a response has already happened, we should not\n  // continue to further filters. A concrete example of this is a filter buffering data, the\n  // last data frame comes in and the filter continues, but the final buffering takes the stream\n  // over the high watermark such that a 413 is returned.\n  return !parent_.stopDecoderFilterChain();\n}\n\nbool ActiveStreamEncoderFilter::canContinue() {\n  // As with ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::canContinue() make sure we do not\n  // continue if a local reply has been sent or ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::recreateStream() is\n  // called, etc.\n  return !parent_.state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_ &&\n         (!Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n              \"envoy.reloadable_features.filter_chain_aborted_can_not_continue\") ||\n          !parent_.stopEncoderFilterChain());\n}\n\nBuffer::InstancePtr ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::createBuffer() {\n  auto buffer = dispatcher().getWatermarkFactory().createBuffer(\n      [this]() -> void { this->requestDataDrained(); },"
}

174. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD~1:source/common/http/filter_manager.cc | sed -n '339,360p' 2>/dev/null | head -25",
  "description": "Check canContinue in the commit BEFORE v1.33.0"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "bool ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::canContinue() {\n  // It is possible for the connection manager to respond directly to a request even while\n  // a filter is trying to continue. If a response has already happened, we should not\n  // continue to further filters. A concrete example of this is a filter buffering data, the\n  // last data frame comes in and the filter continues, but the final buffering takes the stream\n  // over the high watermark such that a 413 is returned.\n  return !parent_.stopDecoderFilterChain();\n}\n\nbool ActiveStreamEncoderFilter::canContinue() {\n  // As with ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::canContinue() make sure we do not\n  // continue if a local reply has been sent or ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::recreateStream() is\n  // called, etc.\n  return !parent_.state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_ &&\n         (!Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n              \"envoy.reloadable_features.filter_chain_aborted_can_not_continue\") ||\n          !parent_.stopEncoderFilterChain());\n}\n\nBuffer::InstancePtr ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::createBuffer() {\n  auto buffer = dispatcher().getWatermarkFactory().createBuffer(\n      [this]() -> void { this->requestDataDrained(); },"
}

Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.h | sed -n '55,120p'",
  "description": "Read ExtAuthzLoggingInfo class from ext_authz.h"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "class ExtAuthzLoggingInfo : public Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterState::Object {\npublic:\n  explicit ExtAuthzLoggingInfo(const absl::optional<Envoy::ProtobufWkt::Struct> filter_metadata)\n      : filter_metadata_(filter_metadata) {}\n\n  const absl::optional<ProtobufWkt::Struct>& filterMetadata() const { return filter_metadata_; }\n  absl::optional<std::chrono::microseconds> latency() const { return latency_; };\n  absl::optional<uint64_t> bytesSent() const { return bytes_sent_; }\n  absl::optional<uint64_t> bytesReceived() const { return bytes_received_; }\n  Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr clusterInfo() const { return cluster_info_; }\n  Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr upstreamHost() const { return upstream_host_; }\n\n  void setLatency(std::chrono::microseconds ms) { latency_ = ms; };\n  void setBytesSent(uint64_t bytes_sent) { bytes_sent_ = bytes_sent; }\n  void setBytesReceived(uint64_t bytes_received) { bytes_received_ = bytes_received; }\n  void setClusterInfo(Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr cluster_info) {\n    cluster_info_ = std::move(cluster_info);\n  }\n  void setUpstreamHost(Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr upstream_host) {\n    upstream_host_ = std::move(upstream_host);\n  }\n\n  bool hasFieldSupport() const override { return true; }\n  Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterState::Object::FieldType\n  getField(absl::string_view field_name) const override {\n    if (field_name == \"latency_us\" && latency_.has_value()) {\n      return int64_t(latency_.value().count());\n    } else if (field_name == \"bytesSent\" && bytes_sent_.has_value()) {\n      return int64_t(bytes_sent_.value());\n    } else if (field_name == \"bytesReceived\" && bytes_received_.has_value()) {\n      return int64_t(bytes_received_.value());\n    }\n    return {};\n  }\n\n  // For convenience in testing.\n  void clearLatency() { latency_ = absl::nullopt; };\n  void clearBytesSent() { bytes_sent_ = absl::nullopt; }\n  void clearBytesReceived() { bytes_received_ = absl::nullopt; }\n  void clearClusterInfo() { cluster_info_ = nullptr; }\n  void clearUpstreamHost() { upstream_host_ = nullptr; }\n\nprivate:\n  const absl::optional<Envoy::ProtobufWkt::Struct> filter_metadata_;\n  absl::optional<std::chrono::microseconds> latency_;\n  // The following stats are populated for ext_authz filters using Envoy gRPC only.\n  absl::optional<uint64_t> bytes_sent_;\n  absl::optional<uint64_t> bytes_received_;\n  Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr cluster_info_;\n  Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr upstream_host_;\n};\n\n/**\n * Configuration for the External Authorization (ext_authz) filter.\n */\nclass FilterConfig {\n  using LabelsMap = Protobuf::Map<std::string, std::string>;\n\npublic:\n  FilterConfig(const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3::ExtAuthz& config,\n               Stats::Scope& scope, const std::string& stats_prefix,\n               Server::Configuration::ServerFactoryContext& factory_context);\n\n  bool allowPartialMessage() const { return allow_partial_message_; }\n\n  bool withRequestBody() const { return max_request_bytes_ > 0; }"
}

{

  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "  ASSERT(saved_request_metadata_ != nullptr);\n  for (auto& metadata_map : *getSavedRequestMetadata()) {\n    parent_.decodeMetadata(this, *metadata_map);\n  }\n  getSavedRequestMetadata()->clear();\n}\n\nvoid ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::handleMetadataAfterHeadersCallback() {\n  if (parent_.state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_) {\n    // The decoder filter chain has been aborted, possibly due to a local reply. In this case,\n    // there's no reason to decode saved metadata.\n    getSavedRequestMetadata()->clear();\n    return;\n  }\n  // If we drain accumulated metadata, the iteration must start with the current filter.\n  const bool saved_state = iterate_from_current_filter_;\n  iterate_from_current_filter_ = true;\n  // If decodeHeaders() returns StopAllIteration, we should skip draining metadata, and wait\n  // for doMetadata() to drain the metadata after iteration continues.\n  if (!stoppedAll() && saved_request_metadata_ != nullptr && !getSavedRequestMetadata()->empty()) {\n    drainSavedRequestMetadata();\n  }\n  // Restores the original value of iterate_from_current_filter_.\n  iterate_from_current_filter_ = saved_state;\n}\n\nRequestTrailerMap& ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::addDecodedTrailers() {\n  return parent_.addDecodedTrailers();\n}\n\nvoid ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::addDecodedData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool streaming) {\n  parent_.addDecodedData(*this, data, streaming);\n}\n\nMetadataMapVector& ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::addDecodedMetadata() {\n  return parent_.addDecodedMetadata();\n}\n\nvoid ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::injectDecodedDataToFilterChain(Buffer::Instance& data,\n                                                               bool end_stream) {\n  if (!headers_continued_) {"
}

  "stdout": "void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::injectDecodedDataToFilterChain(Buffer::Instance& data,\n                                                               bool end_stream) {\n  if (!headers_continued_) {\n    headers_continued_ = true;\n    doHeaders(false);\n  }\n  parent_.decodeData(this, data, end_stream,\n                     FilterManager::FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent);\n}\n\nvoid ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::continueDecoding() { commonContinue(); }\nconst Buffer::Instance* ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::decodingBuffer() {\n  return parent_.buffered_request_data_.get();\n}\n\nbool ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::shouldLoadShed() const { return parent_.shouldLoadShed(); }\n\nvoid ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::modifyDecodingBuffer(\n    std::function<void(Buffer::Instance&)> callback) {\n  ASSERT(parent_.state_.latest_data_decoding_filter_ == this);\n  callback(*parent_.buffered_request_data_.get());\n}\n\nvoid ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::sendLocalReply(\n    Code code, absl::string_view body,\n    std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)> modify_headers,\n    const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status, absl::string_view details) {\n  ActiveStreamFilterBase::sendLocalReply(code, body, modify_headers, grpc_status, details);\n}\n\nvoid ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::sendGoAwayAndClose() { parent_.sendGoAwayAndClose(); }\n\nvoid ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::encode1xxHeaders(ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers) {"
}

194. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/common/http/filter_manager.cc | sed -n '537,640p'",
  "description": "Read decodeHeaders function from HEAD filter_manager.cc"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "void FilterManager::decodeHeaders(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* filter, RequestHeaderMap& headers,\n                                  bool end_stream) {\n  // Headers filter iteration should always start with the next filter if available.\n  StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator entry =\n      commonDecodePrefix(filter, FilterIterationStartState::AlwaysStartFromNext);\n  StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator continue_data_entry = decoder_filters_.end();\n  bool terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream = false;\n  ASSERT(!state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_ || entry == decoder_filters_.end() ||\n         (*entry)->end_stream_);\n\n  for (; entry != decoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\n    ENVOY_EXECUTION_SCOPE(trackedStream(), &(*entry)->filter_context_);\n    ASSERT(!(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::DecodeHeaders));\n    state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::DecodeHeaders;\n    (*entry)->end_stream_ = (end_stream && continue_data_entry == decoder_filters_.end());\n    if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\n      state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::EndOfStream;\n    }\n    FilterHeadersStatus status = (*entry)->decodeHeaders(headers, (*entry)->end_stream_);\n    state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::DecodeHeaders;\n    if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\n      state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::EndOfStream;\n    }\n    if (state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_) {\n      executeLocalReplyIfPrepared();\n      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n                       \"decodeHeaders filter iteration aborted due to local reply: filter={}\",\n                       *this, (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name);\n      status = FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\n    }\n\n    ASSERT(!(status == FilterHeadersStatus::ContinueAndDontEndStream && !(*entry)->end_stream_),\n           \"Filters should not return FilterHeadersStatus::ContinueAndDontEndStream from \"\n           \"decodeHeaders when end_stream is already false\");\n\n    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"decode headers called: filter={} status={}\", *this,\n                     (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name, static_cast<uint64_t>(status));\n\n    (*entry)->processed_headers_ = true;\n\n    const auto continue_iteration = (*entry)->commonHandleAfterHeadersCallback(status, end_stream);\n    ENVOY_BUG(!continue_iteration || !stopDecoderFilterChain(),\n              fmt::format(\n                  \"filter={} did not return StopAll or StopIteration after sending a local reply.\",\n                  (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name));\n\n    // If this filter ended the stream, decodeComplete() should be called for it.\n    if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\n      (*entry)->handle_->decodeComplete();\n    }\n\n    // Skip processing metadata after sending local reply\n    if (stopDecoderFilterChain() && std::next(entry) != decoder_filters_.end()) {\n      maybeContinueDecoding(continue_data_entry);\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const bool new_metadata_added = processNewlyAddedMetadata();\n    // If end_stream is set in headers, and a filter adds new metadata, we need to delay end_stream\n    // in headers by inserting an empty data frame with end_stream set. The empty data frame is sent\n    // after the new metadata.\n    if ((*entry)->end_stream_ && new_metadata_added && !buffered_request_data_) {\n      Buffer::OwnedImpl empty_data(\"\");\n      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\n          trace, \"inserting an empty data frame for end_stream due metadata being added.\", *this);\n      // Metadata frame doesn't carry end of stream bit. We need an empty data frame to end the\n      // stream.\n      addDecodedData(*(*entry), empty_data, true);\n    }\n\n    if (!continue_iteration && std::next(entry) != decoder_filters_.end()) {\n      // Stop iteration IFF this is not the last filter. If it is the last filter, continue with\n      // processing since we need to handle the case where a terminal filter wants to buffer, but\n      // a previous filter has added body.\n      maybeContinueDecoding(continue_data_entry);\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // Here we handle the case where we have a header only request, but a filter adds a body\n    // to it. We need to not raise end_stream = true to further filters during inline iteration.\n    if (end_stream && buffered_request_data_ && continue_data_entry == decoder_filters_.end()) {\n      continue_data_entry = entry;\n    }\n    // The decoder filter chain is finished here if the following is true:\n    // 1. the last filter has observed the end_stream AND\n    // 2. no filter, including the last filter, has injected body during header iteration.\n    // If body was injected the end_stream will be processed in the `decodeData()` method below.\n    const bool no_body_was_injected = continue_data_entry == decoder_filters_.end();\n    terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream =\n        (std::next(entry) == decoder_filters_.end() && (*entry)->end_stream_) &&\n        no_body_was_injected;\n  }\n\n  maybeContinueDecoding(continue_data_entry);\n\n  if (end_stream) {\n    disarmRequestTimeout();\n  }\n  maybeEndDecode(terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream);\n}\n\nvoid FilterManager::decodeData(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* filter, Buffer::Instance& data,\n                               bool end_stream,\n                               FilterIterationStartState filter_iteration_start_state) {"
}

    "content": "540:   parent_.encodeTrailers(nullptr, *parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.responseTrailers());\n541: }\n542: \n543: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::encodeMetadata(MetadataMapPtr&& metadata_map_ptr) {\n544:   parent_.encodeMetadata(nullptr, std::move(metadata_map_ptr));\n545: }\n546: \n547: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::onDecoderFilterAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() {\n548:   parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.onDecoderFilterAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark();\n549: }\n550: \n551: void ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::requestDataTooLarge() {\n552:   ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"request data too large watermark exceeded\", parent_);\n553:   if (parent_.state_.decoder_filters_streaming_) {\n554:     onDecoderFilterAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark();\n555:   } else {\n556:     parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.onRequestDataTooLarge();\n557:     sendLocalReply(Code::PayloadTooLarge, CodeUtility::toString(Code::PayloadTooLarge), nullptr,\n558:                    absl::nullopt, StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().RequestPayloadTooLarge);\n559:   }\n560: }\n561: \n562: void FilterManager::maybeContinueDecoding(StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator continue_data_entry) {\n563:   if (continue_data_entry != decoder_filters_.end()) {\n564:     // We use the continueDecoding() code since it will correctly handle not calling\n565:     // decodeHeaders() again. Fake setting StopSingleIteration since the continueDecoding() code\n566:     // expects it.\n567:     ASSERT(buffered_request_data_);\n568:     (*continue_data_entry)->iteration_state_ =\n569:         ActiveStreamFilterBase::IterationState::StopSingleIteration;\n570:     (*continue_data_entry)->continueDecoding();\n571:   }\n572: }\n573: \n574: void FilterManager::decodeHeaders(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* filter, RequestHeaderMap& headers,\n575:                                   bool end_stream) {\n576:   // Headers filter iteration should always start with the next filter if available.\n577:   StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator entry =\n578:       commonDecodePrefix(filter, FilterIterationStartState::AlwaysStartFromNext);\n579: decoder_filters_.end() ||\n582:          (*entry)->end_stream_);\n583: \n584:   for (; entry != decoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\n585:     ENVOY_EXECUTION_SCOPE(trackedStream(), &(*entry)->filter_context_);\n586:     ASSERT(!(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::DecodeHeaders));\n587:     state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::DecodeHeaders;\n588:     (*entry)->end_stream_ = (end_stream && continue_data_entry == decoder_filters_.end());\n589:     if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\n590:       state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::EndOfStream;\n591:     }\n592:     FilterHeadersStatus status = (*entry)->decodeHeaders(headers, (*entry)->end_stream_);\n593:     state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::DecodeHeaders;\n594:     if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\n595:       state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::EndOfStream;\n596:     }\n597:     if (state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_) {\n598:       executeLocalReplyIfPrepared();\n599:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n600:                        \"decodeHeaders filter iteration aborted due to (*entry)->commonHandleAfterHeadersCallback(status, end_stream);\n615:     ENVOY_BUG(!continue_iteration || !stopDecoderFilterChain(),\n616:               fmt::format(\n617: sending local reply\n626:     if (stopDecoderFilterChain() && std::next(entry) != decoder_filters_.end()) {\n627:       maybeContinueDecoding(continue_data_entry);\n628:       return;\n629: in headers by inserting an empty data frame with end_stream set. 197. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/common/http/filter_manager.cc | sed -n '1225,1310p'",
  "description": "Read encodeHeaders function from HEAD filter_manager.cc"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "void FilterManager::encodeHeaders(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter, ResponseHeaderMap& headers,\n                                  bool end_stream) {\n  // See encodeHeaders() comments in envoy/http/filter.h for why the 1xx precondition holds.\n  ASSERT(!CodeUtility::is1xx(Utility::getResponseStatus(headers)) ||\n         Utility::getResponseStatus(headers) == enumToInt(Http::Code::SwitchingProtocols));\n  filter_manager_callbacks_.resetIdleTimer();\n  disarmRequestTimeout();\n\n  // Headers filter iteration should always start with the next filter if available.\n  StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator entry =\n      commonEncodePrefix(filter, end_stream, FilterIterationStartState::AlwaysStartFromNext);\n  StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator continue_data_entry = encoder_filters_.end();\n\n  for (; entry != encoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\n    ENVOY_EXECUTION_SCOPE(trackedStream(), &(*entry)->filter_context_);\n    ASSERT(!(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::EncodeHeaders));\n    state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::EncodeHeaders;\n    (*entry)->end_stream_ = (end_stream && continue_data_entry == encoder_filters_.end());\n    if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\n      state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::EndOfStream;\n    }\n    FilterHeadersStatus status = (*entry)->handle_->encodeHeaders(headers, (*entry)->end_stream_);\n    if (state_.encoder_filter_chain_aborted_) {\n      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n                       \"encodeHeaders filter iteration aborted due to local reply: filter={}\",\n                       *this, (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name);\n      status = FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\n    }\n\n    ASSERT(!(status == FilterHeadersStatus::ContinueAndDontEndStream && !(*entry)->end_stream_),\n           \"Filters should not return FilterHeadersStatus::ContinueAndDontEndStream from \"\n           \"encodeHeaders when end_stream is already false\");\n\n    state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::EncodeHeaders;\n    if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\n      state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::EndOfStream;\n    }\n    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"encode headers called: filter={} status={}\", *this,\n                     (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name, static_cast<uint64_t>(status));\n\n    (*entry)->processed_headers_ = true;\n\n    const auto continue_iteration = (*entry)->commonHandleAfterHeadersCallback(status, end_stream);\n\n    // If this filter ended the stream, encodeComplete() should be called for it.\n    if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\n      (*entry)->handle_->encodeComplete();\n    }\n\n    if (!continue_iteration) {\n      if (!(*entry)->end_stream_) {\n        maybeContinueEncoding(continue_data_entry);\n      }\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // Here we handle the case where we have a header only response, but a filter adds a body\n    // to it. We need to not raise end_stream = true to further filters during inline iteration.\n    if (end_stream && buffered_response_data_ && continue_data_entry == encoder_filters_.end()) {\n      continue_data_entry = entry;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Check if the filter chain above did not remove critical headers or set malformed header values.\n  // We could do this at the codec in order to prevent other places than the filter chain from\n  // removing critical headers, but it will come with the implementation complexity.\n  // See the previous attempt (#15658) for detail, and for now we choose to protect only against\n  // filter chains.\n  const auto status = HeaderUtility::checkRequiredResponseHeaders(headers);\n  if (!status.ok()) {\n    // If the check failed, then we reply with BadGateway, and stop the further processing.\n    sendLocalReply(\n        Http::Code::BadGateway, status.message(), nullptr, absl::nullopt,\n        absl::StrCat(StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().FilterRemovedRequiredResponseHeaders,\n                     \"{\", StringUtil::replaceAllEmptySpace(status.message()), \"}\"));\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const bool modified_end_stream = (end_stream && continue_data_entry == encoder_filters_.end());\n  state_.non_100_response_headers_encoded_ = true;\n  filter_manager_callbacks_.encodeHeaders(headers, modified_end_stream);\n  if (state_.saw_downstream_reset_) {\n    return;\n  }\n  maybeEndEncode(modified_end_stream);"
}

entry != encoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\\n1274:     ENVOY_EXECUTION_SCOPE(trackedStream(), &(*entry)->filter_context_);\\n1275:     ASSERT(!(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::EncodeHeaders));\\n1276:     state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::EncodeHeaders;\\n1277:     (*entry)->end_stream_ = (end_stream && continue_data_entry == encoder_filters_.end());\\n1278:     if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\\n1279:       state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::EndOfStream;\\n1280:     }\\n1281:     FilterHeadersStatus status = (*entry)->handle_->encodeHeaders(headers, (*entry)->end_stream_);\\n1282:     if (state_.encoder_filter_chain_aborted_) {\\n1283:       ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\\n1284:                        \\\"encodeHeaders filter iteration aborted due to local reply: filter={}\\\",\\n1285:                        *this, (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name);\\n1286:       status = FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\\n1287:     }\\n1288: \\n1289:     ASSERT(!(status == FilterHeadersStatus::ContinueAndDontEndStream && !(*entry)->end_stream_),\\n1290:            \\\"Filters should not return FilterHeadersStatus::ContinueAndDontEndStream from \\\"\\n1291: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc%3FL1200-1290\"}",

199. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/common/http/filter_manager.cc | sed -n '940,1000p'",
  "description": "Read onLocalReply and sendLocalReply from HEAD filter_manager.cc"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "  if (filter_iteration_start_state == FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent &&\n      (*(filter->entry()))->iterate_from_current_filter_) {\n    // The filter iteration has been stopped for all frame types, and now the iteration continues.\n    // The current filter's callback function has not been called. Call it now.\n    return filter->entry();\n  }\n  return std::next(filter->entry());\n}\n\nvoid DownstreamFilterManager::onLocalReply(StreamFilterBase::LocalReplyData& data) {\n  state_.under_on_local_reply_ = true;\n  filter_manager_callbacks_.onLocalReply(data.code_);\n\n  for (auto entry : filters_) {\n    if (entry->onLocalReply(data) == LocalErrorStatus::ContinueAndResetStream) {\n      data.reset_imminent_ = true;\n    }\n  }\n  state_.under_on_local_reply_ = false;\n}\n\nvoid DownstreamFilterManager::sendLocalReply(\n    Code code, absl::string_view body,\n    const std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)>& modify_headers,\n    const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status, absl::string_view details) {\n  ASSERT(!state_.under_on_local_reply_);\n  const bool is_head_request = state_.is_head_request_;\n  const bool is_grpc_request = state_.is_grpc_request_;\n\n  // Stop filter chain iteration if local reply was sent while filter decoding or encoding callbacks\n  // are running.\n  if (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsDecodingMask) {\n    state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n  } else if (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsEncodingMask) {\n    state_.encoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n  }\n  // When independent half-close is enabled local reply always stops decoder filter chain.\n  if (filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled()) {\n    state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n  }\n\n  streamInfo().setResponseCodeDetails(details);\n  StreamFilterBase::LocalReplyData data{code, grpc_status, details, false};\n  onLocalReply(data);\n  if (data.reset_imminent_) {\n    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Resetting stream due to {}. onLocalReply requested reset.\", *this,\n                     details);\n    filter_manager_callbacks_.resetStream(Http::StreamResetReason::LocalReset, \"\");\n    return;\n  }\n\n  if (!filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders().has_value() &&\n      (!filter_manager_callbacks_.informationalHeaders().has_value() ||\n       (Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n            \"envoy.reloadable_features.local_reply_traverses_filter_chain_after_1xx\") &&\n        !(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsEncodingMask)))) {\n    // If the response has not started at all, or if the only response so far is an informational\n    // 1xx that has already been fully processed, send the response through the filter chain.\n\n    if (auto cb = filter_manager_callbacks_.downstreamCallbacks(); cb.has_value()) {\n      // The initial route maybe never be set or the cached route maybe cleared by the filters."
}

data.reset_imminent_ = true;\\n993:     }\\n994:   }\\n995:   state_.under_on_local_reply_ = false;\\n996: }\\n997: \\n998: void DownstreamFilterManager::sendLocalReply(\\n999:     Code code, absl::string_view body,\\n1000:     const std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)>& modify_headers,\\n1001:     const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status, absl::string_view details) {\\n1002:   ASSERT(!state_.under_on_local_reply_);\\n1003:   const bool is_head_request = state_.is_head_request_;\\n1004:   const bool is_grpc_request = state_.is_grpc_request_;\\n1005: \\n1006:   // Stop filter chain iteration if local reply was sent while filter decoding or encoding callbacks\\n1007:   // are running.\\n1008:   if (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsDecodingMask) {\\n1009:     state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\\n1010:   } else if (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsEncodingMask) {\\n1011:     state_.encoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\\n1012:   }\\n1013:   // When independent half-close is enabled local reply always stops decoder filter chain.\\n1014:   if (filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled()) {\\n1015:     state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\\n1016:   }\\n1017: \\n1018:   streamInfo().setResponseCodeDetails(details);\\n1019:   StreamFilterBase::LocalReplyData data{code, grpc_status, details, false};\\n1020:   onLocalReply(data);\\n1021:   if (data.reset_imminent_) {\\n1022:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \\\"Resetting stream due to {}. onLocalReply requested reset.\\\", *this,\\n1023:                      details);\\n1024:     filter_manager_callbacks_.resetStream(Http::StreamResetReason::LocalReset, \\\"\\\");\\n1025:     return;\\n1026:   }\\n1027: \\n1028:   if (!filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders().has_value() &&\\n1029:       (!filter_manager_callbacks_.informationalHeaders().has_value() ||\\n1030:        !(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsEncodingMask))) {\\n1031:     // If the response has not started at all, or if the only response so far is an informational\\n1032:     // 1xx that has already been fully processed, send the response through the filter chain.\\n1033: \\n1034:     if (auto cb = filter_manager_callbacks_.downstreamCallbacks(); cb.has_value()) {\\n1035:       // The initial route maybe never be set or the cached route maybe cleared by the filters.\\n1036:       // This will force route refreshment if there is not a cached route to avoid potential\\n1037:       // route refreshment in the response filter chain.\\n1038:       cb->route(nullptr);\\n1039:     }\\n1040:     // We only prepare a local reply to execute later if we're actively invoking filters to avoid\\n1041:     // re-entrant in filters.\\n1042:     //\\n1043:     // For reverse connections (where upstream initiates the connection to downstream), we need to\\n1044:     // send local replies immediately rather than queuing them. This ensures proper handling of the\\n1045:     // reversed connection flow and prevents potential issues with connection state and filter chain\\n1046:     // processing.\\n1047:     if (!Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\\n1048:             \\\"envoy.reloadable_features.reverse_conn_force_local_reply\\\") &&\\n1049:         (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsDecodingMask)) {\\n1050:       prepareLocalReplyViaFilterChain(is_grpc_request, code, body, modify_headers, is_head_request,\\n1051:                                       grpc_status, details);\\n1052:     } else {\\n1053:       sendLocalReplyViaFilterChain(is_grpc_request, code, body, modify_headers, is_head_request,\\n1054:                                    grpc_status, details);\\n1055:     }\\n1056:   } else if (!state_.non_100_response_headers_encoded_) {\\n1057:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \\\"Sending local reply with details {} directly to the encoder\\\", *this,\\n1058:                      details);\\n1059:     // In this case, at least the header and possibly the body has started\\n1060:     // processing through the filter chain, but no non-informational headers\\n1061: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc%3FL985-1060\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "985: \n986: void DownstreamFilterManager::onLocalReply(StreamFilterBase::LocalReplyData& data) {\n987:   state_.under_on_local_reply_ = true;\n988:   filter_manager_callbacks_.onLocalReply(data.code_);\n989: \n990:   for (auto entry : filters_) {\n991:     if (entry->onLocalReply(data) == LocalErrorStatus::ContinueAndResetStream) {\n992:       data.reset_imminent_ = true;\n993:     }\n994:   }\n995:   state_.under_on_local_reply_ = false;\n996: }\n997: \n998: void DownstreamFilterManager::sendLocalReply(\n999:     Code code, absl::string_view body,\n1000:     const std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)>& modify_headers,\n1001:     const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status, absl::string_view details) {\n1002:   ASSERT(!state_.under_on_local_reply_);\n1003:   const bool is_head_request = state_.is_head_request_;\n1004:   const bool is_grpc_request = state_.is_grpc_request_;\n1005: \n1006:   // Stop filter chain iteration if local reply was sent while filter decoding or encoding callbacks\n1007:   // are running.\n1008:   if (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsDecodingMask) {\n1009:     state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n1010:   } else if (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsEncodingMask) {\n1011:     state_.encoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n1012:   }\n1013:   // When independent half-close is enabled local reply always stops decoder filter chain.\n1014:   if (filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled()) {\n1015:     state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n1016:   }\n1017: \n1018:   streamInfo().setResponseCodeDetails(details);\n1019:   StreamFilterBase::LocalReplyData data{code, grpc_status, details, false};\n1020:   onLocalReply(data);\n1021:   if (data.reset_imminent_) {\n1022:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Resetting stream due to {}. onLocalReply requested reset.\", *this,\n1023:                      details);\n1024:     filter_manager_callbacks_.resetStream(Http::StreamResetReason::LocalReset, \"\");\n1025:     return;\n1026:   }\n1027: \n1028:   if (!filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders().has_value() &&\n1029:       (!filter_manager_callbacks_.informationalHeaders().has_value() ||\n1030:        !(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsEncodingMask))) {\n1031:     // If the response has not started at all, or if the only response so far is an informational\n1032:     // 1xx that has already been fully processed, send the response through the filter chain.\n1033: \n1034:     if (auto cb = filter_manager_callbacks_.downstreamCallbacks(); cb.has_value()) {\n1035:       // The initial route maybe never be set or the cached route maybe cleared by the filters.\n1036:       // This will force route refreshment if there is not a cached route to avoid potential\n1037:       // route refreshment in the response filter chain.\n1038:       cb->route(nullptr);\n1039:     }\n1040:     // We only prepare a local reply to execute later if we're actively invoking filters to avoid\n1041:     // re-entrant in filters.\n1042:     //\n1043:     // For reverse connections (where upstream initiates the connection to downstream), we need to\n1044:     // send local replies immediately rather than queuing them. This ensures proper handling of the\n1045:     // reversed connection flow and prevents potential issues with connection state and filter chain\n1046:     // processing.\n1047:     if (!Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n1048:             \"envoy.reloadable_features.reverse_conn_force_local_reply\") &&\n1049:         (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsDecodingMask)) {\n1050:       prepareLocalReplyViaFilterChain(is_grpc_request, code, body, modify_headers, is_head_request,\n1051:                                       grpc_status, details);\n1052:     } else {\n1053:       sendLocalReplyViaFilterChain(is_grpc_request, code, body, modify_headers, is_head_request,\n1054:                                    grpc_status, details);\n1055:     }\n1056:   } else if (!state_.non_100_response_headers_encoded_) {\n1057:     ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Sending local reply with details {} directly to the encoder\", *this,\n1058:                      details);\n1059:     // In this case, at least the header and possibly the body has started\n1060:     // processing through the filter chain, but no non-informational headers\n1061: ",

201. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/common/http/filter_manager.cc | sed -n '1005,1065p'",
  "description": "Read the local reply routing logic in HEAD filter_manager.cc"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "    // We only prepare a local reply to execute later if we're actively\n    // invoking filters to avoid re-entrant in filters.\n    if (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsDecodingMask) {\n      prepareLocalReplyViaFilterChain(is_grpc_request, code, body, modify_headers, is_head_request,\n                                      grpc_status, details);\n    } else {\n      sendLocalReplyViaFilterChain(is_grpc_request, code, body, modify_headers, is_head_request,\n                                   grpc_status, details);\n    }\n  } else if (!state_.non_100_response_headers_encoded_) {\n    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Sending local reply with details {} directly to the encoder\", *this,\n                     details);\n    // In this case, at least the header and possibly the body has started\n    // processing through the filter chain, but no non-informational headers\n    // have been sent downstream. To ensure that filters don't get their\n    // state machine screwed up, bypass the filter chain and send the local\n    // reply directly to the codec.\n    //\n    sendDirectLocalReply(code, body, modify_headers, state_.is_head_request_, grpc_status);\n  } else {\n    // If we land in this branch, response headers have already been sent to the client.\n    // All we can do at this point is reset the stream.\n    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Resetting stream due to {}. Prior headers have already been sent\",\n                     *this, details);\n    // TODO(snowp): This means we increment the tx_reset stat which we weren't doing previously.\n    // Intended?\n    filter_manager_callbacks_.resetStream();\n  }\n}\n\nvoid DownstreamFilterManager::prepareLocalReplyViaFilterChain(\n    bool is_grpc_request, Code code, absl::string_view body,\n    const std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)>& modify_headers, bool is_head_request,\n    const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status, absl::string_view details) {\n  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Preparing local reply with details {}\", *this, details);\n  ASSERT(!filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders().has_value());\n  // For early error handling, do a best-effort attempt to create a filter chain\n  // to ensure access logging. If the filter chain already exists this will be\n  // a no-op.\n  createDownstreamFilterChain();\n\n  if (prepared_local_reply_) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  prepared_local_reply_ = Utility::prepareLocalReply(\n      Utility::EncodeFunctions{\n          [this, modify_headers](ResponseHeaderMap& headers) -> void {\n            if (streamInfo().route() && streamInfo().route()->routeEntry()) {\n              streamInfo().route()->routeEntry()->finalizeResponseHeaders(headers, streamInfo());\n            }\n            if (modify_headers) {\n              modify_headers(headers);\n            }\n          },\n          [this](ResponseHeaderMap& response_headers, Code& code, std::string& body,\n                 absl::string_view& content_type) -> void {\n            local_reply_.rewrite(filter_manager_callbacks_.requestHeaders().ptr(), response_headers,\n                                 streamInfo(), code, body, content_type);\n          },"
}

  "interrupted": false,

  "noOutputExpected": false,

  "stdout": "867251ab48 Support two ext_proc filters in the chain 2nd attempt (#43175)\n4c79c71d69 Revert \"ext_authz: added support for the append_action in gRPC ExtAuthz (#42878)\" (#43442)\ncbe23a824a http/ratelimit,http/ext_authz: change timeout=0 behavior (#43020)\n50ead8ad00 ext_authz: added support for the append_action in gRPC ExtAuthz (#42878)\nb26968c710 unified the buffer limit method to single one (#42905)\na671800751 http: unify the buffer limit to uint64 (#42608)\n78df6c1c88 filter chain refactoring: add more context for FilterChainFactoryCallbacks (#42844)\n78e77df4ea ext_authz: track active client explicitly for correct cancellation (#42643)\n39f8327556 ext_authz: add error_response for gRPC ExtAuthZ to return custom error response (#42099)\neef470f6f3 ext_authz: send local reply on failed response header mutations (#41844)\n06dba5986b ext_authz: perform request header checks progressively (#41766)\n5abbffd7de http2: send graceful goaways on close (#41284)\na48b7becb4 ext_authz: add knob enforce_response_header_limits (#41742)\nc144e550ba formatter: pure renaming of the formatter method and the context (#41698)\n858a29b64f ext_authz: check header count before adding response headers (#41334)\n180f01d9b3 ext_authz: avoid check-settings copy (#41424)\nee27890793 add max_denied_response_body_bytes to http ext_authz (#41253)\n0b2da108af ext_authz: check request header size limit after mutations (#41274)\nac9d8ba9a8 ext_authz: check request header map count after applying mutations (#41122)\n7ced9f43c0 fix: fix substitution formatter for header mutation (#40905)"
}

206. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show 0038ebbacf -- source/common/http/filter_manager.cc | head -60",
  "description": "Show what 0038ebbacf changed in filter_manager.cc - the most recent commit"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "commit 0038ebbacfe7899ce1c0b76c5136f1f319c3b2b0\nAuthor: \u6f84\u6f6d <zty98751@alibaba-inc.com>\nDate:   Fri Jan 10 00:11:24 2025 +0800\n\n    Fix the crash caused by continuing the filter after recreating stream (#37627)\n    \n    After the recreatStream() call, both the\n    decode and encode filter chains will abort. Here, by checking if the\n    filter chain has aborted, it prevents the subsequent filters in the\n    filter chain from continuing.\n    \n    Risk Level: Low\n    Testing: Add tests to verify that when recreateStream is executed\n    during the decodeHeader/encodeHeader phase and returns StopIteration,\n    executing continueDecoding/continueEncoding does not proceed with the\n    processing of subsequent filters\n    \n    Runtime guard: envoy.reloadable_features.filter_chain_aborted_can_not_continue\n    Fixes #37625\n    \n    ---------\n    \n    Signed-off-by: zty98751 <zty98751@alibaba-inc.com>\n    Signed-off-by: \u6f84\u6f6d <zty98751@alibaba-inc.com>\n\ndiff --git a/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc b/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc\nindex cc4fb77ec5..665c0572a9 100644\n--- a/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc\n+++ b/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc\n@@ -347,8 +347,12 @@ bool ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::canContinue() {\n \n bool ActiveStreamEncoderFilter::canContinue() {\n   // As with ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::canContinue() make sure we do not\n-  // continue if a local reply has been sent.\n-  return !parent_.state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_;\n+  // continue if a local reply has been sent or ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::recreateStream() is\n+  // called, etc.\n+  return !parent_.state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_ &&\n+         (!Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n+              \"envoy.reloadable_features.filter_chain_aborted_can_not_continue\") ||\n+          !parent_.stopEncoderFilterChain());\n }\n \n Buffer::InstancePtr ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::createBuffer() {"
}

}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "commit 0038ebbacfe7899ce1c0b76c5136f1f319c3b2b0\nAuthor: \u6f84\u6f6d <zty98751@alibaba-inc.com>\nDate:   Fri Jan 10 00:11:24 2025 +0800\n\n    Fix the crash caused by continuing the filter after recreating stream (#37627)\n    \n    After the recreatStream() call, both the\n    decode and encode filter chains will abort. Here, by checking if the\n    filter chain has aborted, it prevents the subsequent filters in the\n    filter chain from continuing.\n    \n    Risk Level: Low\n    Testing: Add tests to verify that when recreateStream is executed\n    during the decodeHeader/encodeHeader phase and returns StopIteration,\n    executing continueDecoding/continueEncoding does not proceed with the\n    processing of subsequent filters\n    \n    Runtime guard: envoy.reloadable_features.filter_chain_aborted_can_not_continue\n    Fixes #37625\n    \n    ---------\n    \n    Signed-off-by: zty98751 <zty98751@alibaba-inc.com>\n    Signed-off-by: \u6f84\u6f6d <zty98751@alibaba-inc.com>\n\ndiff --git a/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc b/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc\nindex cc4fb77ec5..665c0572a9 100644\n--- a/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc\n+++ b/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc\n@@ -347,8 +347,12 @@ bool ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::canContinue() {\n \n bool ActiveStreamEncoderFilter::canContinue() {\n   // As with ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::canContinue() make sure we do not\n-  // continue if a local reply has been sent.\n-  return !parent_.state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_;\n+  // continue if a local reply has been sent or ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::recreateStream() is\n+  // called, etc.\n+  return !parent_.state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_ &&\n+         (!Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n+              \"envoy.reloadable_features.filter_chain_aborted_can_not_continue\") ||\n+          !parent_.stopEncoderFilterChain());\n }\n \n Buffer::InstancePtr ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::createBuffer() {"
}
208. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show 9c754412b9 -- source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc | head -100",
  "description": "Show diff for most recent ext_authz.cc change"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "commit 9c754412b91dc966a79fd915ffcf41f20a52a244\nAuthor: Antonio V. Leonti <53806445+antoniovleonti@users.noreply.github.com>\nDate:   Tue Oct 15 10:35:05 2024 -0400\n\n    fix potential null dereference in ext_authz (#36268)\n    \n    Commit Message: fix potential null dereference in ext_authz\n    Additional Description:\n    \n    Previously, if ext_authz had emit filter state stats set to true and\n    another filter added filter state under the ext authz filter's name, it\n    would result in a null dereference. The member logging_info_ would not\n    be set in initiateCall after seeing there was already data there. Later,\n    we would dereference logging_info_ to update the stats as if it were\n    initialized already.\n    \n    I've added a check for a null logging_info_ and added logging & a stat\n    for when there's a filter state naming collision.\n    \n    I also made some readability improvements to the ext_authz test.\n    \n    Risk Level: low\n    Testing: unit tested\n    Docs Changes: none\n    Release Notes: none\n    Platform Specific Features: none\n    \n    ---------\n    \n    Signed-off-by: antoniovleonti <leonti@google.com>\n\ndiff --git a/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc b/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc\nindex 962aede2c2..7874c8a94b 100644\n--- a/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc\n+++ b/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc\n@@ -180,15 +180,25 @@ void Filter::initiateCall(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\n   // Now that we'll definitely be making the request, add filter state stats if configured to do so.\n   const Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterStateSharedPtr& filter_state =\n       decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().filterState();\n-  if ((config_->emitFilterStateStats() || config_->filterMetadata().has_value()) &&\n-      !filter_state->hasData<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName())) {\n-    filter_state->setData(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName(),\n-                          std::make_shared<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(config_->filterMetadata()),\n-                          Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterState::StateType::Mutable,\n-                          Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterState::LifeSpan::Request);\n+  if ((config_->emitFilterStateStats() || config_->filterMetadata().has_value())) {\n+    if (!filter_state->hasData<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName())) {\n+      filter_state->setData(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName(),\n+                            std::make_shared<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(config_->filterMetadata()),\n+                            Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterState::StateType::Mutable,\n+                            Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterState::LifeSpan::Request);\n \n-    logging_info_ =\n-        filter_state->getDataMutable<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName());\n+      // This may return nullptr (if there's a value at this name whose type doesn't match or isn't\n+      // mutable, for example), so we must check logging_info_ is not nullptr later.\n+      logging_info_ =\n+          filter_state->getDataMutable<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName());\n+    }\n+    if (logging_info_ == nullptr) {\n+      stats_.filter_state_name_collision_.inc();\n+      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug,\n+                       \"Could not find logging info at {}! (Did another filter already put data \"\n+                       \"at this name?)\",\n+                       *decoder_callbacks_, decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName());\n+    }\n   }\n \n   absl::optional<FilterConfigPerRoute> maybe_merged_per_route_config;\n@@ -405,6 +415,10 @@ void Filter::updateLoggingInfo() {\n     return;\n   }\n \n+  if (logging_info_ == nullptr) {\n+    return;\n+  }\n+\n   // Latency is the only stat available if we aren't using envoy grpc.\n   if (start_time_.has_value()) {\n     logging_info_->setLatency(std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::microseconds>("
}

{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "9c754412b9 fix potential null dereference in ext_authz (#36268)\na4ad9c2619 route: use reference wrapper for get all filter config (#36079)\n1eedeeed1a ext_authz: add logging options (#35698)\nb916786755 route: get all per filter config directly (#36028)\nff6ced12ef ext_authz: filter state access logging stats PREP (#34945)\n8646385431 add knob disable_dynamic_metadata_ingestion (#34691)\n7d8791802c exceptions: minor cleanup (#34655)\n74f327dc8b Add decoder header mutation rules (#34182)\n85d8cc7455 Add disallowed_headers knob to ext_authz config (#33349)\n1c6eba59db Add explicit header mutation validation ext_authz (#33244)\neb9adc12ad ext_authz: handle append_action from CheckResponse (#33433)\n519c7b2167 Add option to send ext_authz header values as bytes (#32641)\n00536345a1 stream info: rename the extended response flag and response flag (#32105)\n2ad989f811 Fix a MSAN issue in ext_authz due to max_request_bytes_ change (#31897)\n99ebb7c3b8 ext_authz: fix body buffering feature on ExtAuthZ per-route filter (#31437)\n767e32a871 ext_authz: add an option to allow buffering requests on per-route filer (#30571)\n6b78796398 Propagate route metadata in ext_authz (#30477)\n6538c486d4 ext_authz: option to not charge cluster response code (#29841)\n4b0a5d6da8 ext auth support failure mode allow header add config (#30170)\n15f38ada69 Revert \"ext_authz: make the ext_authz filter a dual filter (#29173)\" (#29779)"
}
211. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "for commit in $(git log --oneline HEAD -- source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc | awk '{print $1}'); do\n  first_line=$(git show \"${commit}^\":source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc 2>/dev/null | head -1)\n  if [ \"$first_line\" != '#include \"ext_authz.h\"' ]; then\n    echo \"First commit WITHOUT the wrong include: $commit\"\n    git log --oneline -1 $commit\n    break\n  fi\ndone",
  "description": "Find which commit introduced the wrong relative include in ext_authz.cc"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,

  "stdout": "First commit WITHOUT the wrong include: b916786755\nb916786755 route: get all per filter config directly (#36028)"
}
212. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show a4ad9c2619 -- source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc | head -30",
  "description": "Check what commit a4ad9c2619 changed in ext_authz.cc (first commit with the wrong include)"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "commit a4ad9c26197ee93b20a5df1aba91aa522f6820c8\nAuthor: code <wbphub@gmail.com>\nDate:   Tue Sep 17 20:51:19 2024 +0800\n\n    route: use reference wrapper for get all filter config (#36079)\n    \n    Commit Message: route: use reference wrapper for get all filter config\n    Additional Description:\n    \n    @alyssawilk I think we have a information gap at #36028. The pointer is\n    better for the `perFilterConfigs()` because we always need to do a\n    dynamic_cast. pointer is simpler and won't result in potential exception\n    throwing.\n    \n    But the `Utility::getAllPerFilterConfigs()` could use the reference\n    wrapper because it take a template parameter and will return the typed\n    configs.\n    \n    Risk Level: low.\n    Testing: n/a.\n    Docs Changes: n/a.\n    Release Notes: n/a.\n    Platform Specific Features: n/a.\n    \n    ---------\n    \n    Signed-off-by: wangbaiping <wangbaiping@bytedance.com>\n\ndiff --git a/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc b/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc\nindex b1713614ca..962aede2c2 100644"
}

217. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show b916786755 -- source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc | head -50",
  "description": "Check what b916786755 changed in ext_authz.cc that introduced the wrong include"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "commit b916786755867f01ec36c8165b4d8b0708b6075e\nAuthor: code <wbphub@gmail.com>\nDate:   Tue Sep 10 00:03:21 2024 +0800\n\n    route: get all per filter config directly (#36028)\n    \n    Commit Message: route: get all per filter config directly\n    Additional Description:\n    \n    1. used absl::string_view rather than `const std::string&` as input\n    parameter type. This allow the `mostSpecificPerFilterConfig()` and\n    `perFilterConfigs()` to take a string view as input parameter.\n    2. replace the `traversePerFilterConfig()` with `perFilterConfigs()`.\n    The `perFilterConfigs()` will return a vector (inlined) of configs\n    directly and needn't the caller to provide a callback function to\n    collect them. The new method is more straight forward and easier to use.\n    The new method didn't effect any exist logic, but reduce ~150 lines\n    code.\n    \n    Risk Level: low.\n    Testing: n/a.\n    Docs Changes: n/a.\n    Release Notes: n/a.\n    Platform Specific Features: n/a.\n    \n    ---------\n    \n    Signed-off-by: wangbaiping <wbphub@gmail.com>\n\ndiff --git a/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc b/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc\nindex d301d12689..92bde93d37 100644\n--- a/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc\n+++ b/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc\n@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@\n+#include \"ext_authz.h\"\n #include \"source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\"\n \n #include <chrono>\n@@ -175,11 +176,16 @@ void Filter::initiateCall(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\n     return;\n   }\n \n-  auto&& maybe_merged_per_route_config =\n-      Http::Utility::getMergedPerFilterConfig<FilterConfigPerRoute>(\n-          decoder_callbacks_, [](FilterConfigPerRoute& cfg_base, const FilterConfigPerRoute& cfg) {\n-            cfg_base.merge(cfg);\n-          });\n+  absl::optional<FilterConfigPerRoute> maybe_merged_per_route_config;\n+  for (const auto* cfg :\n+       Http::Utility::getAllPerFilterConfig<FilterConfigPerRoute>(decoder_callbacks_)) {"
}

}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "commit 3402e0c8c3713485c25ef1959efc9e57926a8d12\nAuthor: phlax <phlax@users.noreply.github.com>\nDate:   Wed Dec 4 16:58:13 2024 +0000\n\n    clang-tidy: Assorted source fixes (#37454)\n    \n    Signed-off-by: Ryan Northey <ryan@synca.io>\n\ndiff --git a/source/common/http/header_utility.cc b/source/common/http/header_utility.cc\nindex af69e708d1..e7af846b83 100644\n--- a/source/common/http/header_utility.cc\n+++ b/source/common/http/header_utility.cc\n@@ -168,7 +168,7 @@ namespace {\n // HTTP/2 valid characters (similar to oghttp2's implementation) and the \"@\" character.\n // Once UHV is used, this function should be removed, and the HTTP/1 and HTTP/2\n // authority validations should be different.\n-bool check_authority_h1_h2(const absl::string_view header_value) {\n+bool checkAuthorityH1H2(const absl::string_view header_value) {\n   static constexpr char ValidAuthorityChars[] = {\n       0 /* NUL  */, 0 /* SOH  */, 0 /* STX  */, 0 /* ETX  */,\n       0 /* EOT  */, 0 /* ENQ  */, 0 /* ACK  */, 0 /* BEL  */,\n@@ -248,7 +248,7 @@ bool check_authority_h1_h2(const absl::string_view header_value) {\n bool HeaderUtility::authorityIsValid(const absl::string_view header_value) {\n   if (Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n           \"envoy.reloadable_features.internal_authority_header_validator\")) {\n-    return check_authority_h1_h2(header_value);\n+    return checkAuthorityH1H2(header_value);\n   }\n   return http2::adapter::HeaderValidator::IsValidAuthority(header_value);\n }"
}
222. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show 1eedeeed1a -- source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc | head -100",
  "description": "Check what the logging options commit changed in ext_authz.cc"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "commit 1eedeeed1a421f15577733347e8a32c1ace1f953\nAuthor: Antonio V. Leonti <53806445+antoniovleonti@users.noreply.github.com>\nDate:   Thu Sep 12 11:04:56 2024 -0400\n\n    ext_authz: add logging options (#35698)\n    \n    Commit Message: ext_authz: add logging options / stats\n    Additional Description:\n    \n    logging_options enables ext_authz to emit per-stream stats through\n    filter state for access logging. The stats emitted depend on the client\n    used.\n    \n    If envoy GRPC is used, latency, bytes sent, bytes received, upstream\n    host, and cluster info are emitted. Otherwise, only latency is emitted.\n    \n    If logging_options.filter_metadata is present, ext_authz will also emit\n    the filter metadata in its logging info.\n    \n    ext authz will only emit stats & and filter metadata if a request is\n    made.\n    \n    Testing: unit & integration tests\n    Docs Changes: none\n    Release Notes: changelog updated\n    Platform Specific Features: n/a\n    \n    ---------\n    \n    Signed-off-by: antoniovleonti <leonti@google.com>\n    Signed-off-by: Antonio V. Leonti <53806445+antoniovleonti@users.noreply.github.com>\n\ndiff --git a/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc b/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc\nindex 92bde93d37..b1713614ca 100644\n--- a/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc\n+++ b/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc\n@@ -90,6 +90,7 @@ FilterConfig::FilterConfig(const envoy::extensions::filters::http::ext_authz::v3\n       runtime_(factory_context.runtime()), http_context_(factory_context.httpContext()),\n       filter_metadata_(config.has_filter_metadata() ? absl::optional(config.filter_metadata())\n                                                     : absl::nullopt),\n+      emit_filter_state_stats_(config.emit_filter_state_stats()),\n       filter_enabled_(config.has_filter_enabled()\n                           ? absl::optional<Runtime::FractionalPercent>(\n                                 Runtime::FractionalPercent(config.filter_enabled(), runtime_))\n@@ -176,6 +177,20 @@ void Filter::initiateCall(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\n     return;\n   }\n \n+  // Now that we'll definitely be making the request, add filter state stats if configured to do so.\n+  const Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterStateSharedPtr& filter_state =\n+      decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().filterState();\n+  if ((config_->emitFilterStateStats() || config_->filterMetadata().has_value()) &&\n+      !filter_state->hasData<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName())) {\n+    filter_state->setData(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName(),\n+                          std::make_shared<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(config_->filterMetadata()),\n+                          Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterState::StateType::Mutable,\n+                          Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterState::LifeSpan::Request);\n+\n+    logging_info_ =\n+        filter_state->getDataMutable<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName());\n+  }\n+\n   absl::optional<FilterConfigPerRoute> maybe_merged_per_route_config;\n   for (const auto* cfg :\n        Http::Utility::getAllPerFilterConfig<FilterConfigPerRoute>(decoder_callbacks_)) {\n@@ -384,14 +399,36 @@ Http::FilterMetadataStatus Filter::encodeMetadata(Http::MetadataMap&) {\n \n void Filter::setDecoderFilterCallbacks(Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) {\n   decoder_callbacks_ = &callbacks;\n-  const Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterStateSharedPtr& filter_state =\n-      decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().filterState();\n-  if (config_->filterMetadata().has_value() &&\n-      !filter_state->hasData<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName())) {\n-    filter_state->setData(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName(),\n-                          std::make_shared<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(*config_->filterMetadata()),\n-                          Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterState::StateType::ReadOnly,\n-                          Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterState::LifeSpan::Request);\n+}\n+\n+void Filter::updateLoggingInfo() {\n+  if (!config_->emitFilterStateStats()) {\n+    return;\n+  }\n+\n+  // Latency is the only stat available if we aren't using envoy grpc.\n+  if (start_time_.has_value()) {\n+    logging_info_->setLatency(std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::microseconds>(\n+        decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime() - start_time_.value()));\n+  }\n+\n+  auto const* stream_info = client_->streamInfo();\n+  if (stream_info == nullptr) {\n+    return;\n+  }\n+\n+  const auto& bytes_meter = stream_info->getUpstreamBytesMeter();\n+  if (bytes_meter != nullptr) {\n+    logging_info_->setBytesSent(bytes_meter->wireBytesSent());\n+    logging_info_->setBytesReceived(bytes_meter->wireBytesReceived());\n+  }"
}

226. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show 0038ebbacf --stat | head -20",
  "description": "Check what files the encoder canContinue PR changed"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "commit 0038ebbacfe7899ce1c0b76c5136f1f319c3b2b0\nAuthor: \u6f84\u6f6d <zty98751@alibaba-inc.com>\nDate:   Fri Jan 10 00:11:24 2025 +0800\n\n    Fix the crash caused by continuing the filter after recreating stream (#37627)\n    \n    After the recreatStream() call, both the\n    decode and encode filter chains will abort. Here, by checking if the\n    filter chain has aborted, it prevents the subsequent filters in the\n    filter chain from continuing.\n    \n    Risk Level: Low\n    Testing: Add tests to verify that when recreateStream is executed\n    during the decodeHeader/encodeHeader phase and returns StopIteration,\n    executing continueDecoding/continueEncoding does not proceed with the\n    processing of subsequent filters\n    \n    Runtime guard: envoy.reloadable_features.filter_chain_aborted_can_not_continue\n    Fixes #37625"
}

}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "    Risk Level: Low\n    Testing: Add tests to verify that when recreateStream is executed\n    during the decodeHeader/encodeHeader phase and returns StopIteration,\n    executing continueDecoding/continueEncoding does not proceed with the\n    processing of subsequent filters\n    \n    Runtime guard: envoy.reloadable_features.filter_chain_aborted_can_not_continue\n    Fixes #37625\n    \n    ---------\n    \n    Signed-off-by: zty98751 <zty98751@alibaba-inc.com>\n    Signed-off-by: \u6f84\u6f6d <zty98751@alibaba-inc.com>\n\n changelogs/current.yaml                      |  6 ++\n source/common/http/filter_manager.cc         |  8 ++-\n source/common/http/filter_manager.h          |  2 +\n source/common/runtime/runtime_features.cc    |  1 +\n test/common/http/conn_manager_impl_test_2.cc | 99 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++\n 5 files changed, 114 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)"
}

}

233. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/common/http/filter_manager.cc | sed -n '1280,1330p'",
  "description": "Read the encodeHeaders loop in filter_manager.cc HEAD"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "    // Here we handle the case where we have a header only response, but a filter adds a body\n    // to it. We need to not raise end_stream = true to further filters during inline iteration.\n    if (end_stream && buffered_response_data_ && continue_data_entry == encoder_filters_.end()) {\n      continue_data_entry = entry;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Check if the filter chain above did not remove critical headers or set malformed header values.\n  // We could do this at the codec in order to prevent other places than the filter chain from\n  // removing critical headers, but it will come with the implementation complexity.\n  // See the previous attempt (#15658) for detail, and for now we choose to protect only against\n  // filter chains.\n  const auto status = HeaderUtility::checkRequiredResponseHeaders(headers);\n  if (!status.ok()) {\n    // If the check failed, then we reply with BadGateway, and stop the further processing.\n    sendLocalReply(\n        Http::Code::BadGateway, status.message(), nullptr, absl::nullopt,\n        absl::StrCat(StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().FilterRemovedRequiredResponseHeaders,\n                     \"{\", StringUtil::replaceAllEmptySpace(status.message()), \"}\"));\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const bool modified_end_stream = (end_stream && continue_data_entry == encoder_filters_.end());\n  state_.non_100_response_headers_encoded_ = true;\n  filter_manager_callbacks_.encodeHeaders(headers, modified_end_stream);\n  if (state_.saw_downstream_reset_) {\n    return;\n  }\n  maybeEndEncode(modified_end_stream);\n\n  if (!modified_end_stream) {\n    maybeContinueEncoding(continue_data_entry);\n  }\n}\n\nvoid FilterManager::encodeMetadata(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter,\n                                   MetadataMapPtr&& metadata_map_ptr) {\n  filter_manager_callbacks_.resetIdleTimer();\n\n  StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator entry =\n      commonEncodePrefix(filter, false, FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent);\n\n  for (; entry != encoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\n    ENVOY_EXECUTION_SCOPE(trackedStream(), &(*entry)->filter_context_);\n    // If the filter pointed by entry has stopped for all frame type, stores metadata and returns.\n    // If the filter pointed by entry hasn't returned from encodeHeaders, stores newly added\n    // metadata in case encodeHeaders returns StopAllIteration. The latter can happen when headers\n    // callbacks generate new metadata.\n    if (!(*entry)->processed_headers_ || (*entry)->stoppedAll()) {\n      (*entry)->getSavedResponseMetadata()->emplace_back(std::move(metadata_map_ptr));"
}

Metadata is not sent on to\\n1381:       // the client.\\n1382:       return;\\n1383:     }\\n1384: \\n1385:     if (status == FilterMetadataStatus::ContinueAll && !(*entry)->canIterate()) {\\n1386:       if (std::next(entry) != encoder_filters_.end()) {\\n1387:         (*std::next(entry))->getSavedResponseMetadata()->emplace_back(std::move(metadata_map_ptr));\\n1388:       } else {\\n1389:         filter_manager_callbacks_.encodeMetadata(std::move(metadata_map_ptr));\\n1390:       }\\n1391: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc%3FL1290-1390\"}",
