236. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc | sed -n '1,50p'",
  "description": "Read first 50 lines of ext_authz.cc in HEAD"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "#include \"ext_authz.h\"\n#include \"source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\"\n\n#include <chrono>\n#include <optional>\n#include <string>\n#include <vector>\n\n#include \"envoy/config/core/v3/base.pb.h\"\n\n#include \"source/common/common/assert.h\"\n#include \"source/common/common/enum_to_int.h\"\n#include \"source/common/common/matchers.h\"\n#include \"source/common/http/utility.h\"\n#include \"source/common/router/config_impl.h\"\n\nnamespace Envoy {\nnamespace Extensions {\nnamespace HttpFilters {\nnamespace ExtAuthz {\n\nnamespace {\n\nusing MetadataProto = ::envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata;\nusing Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation;\nusing Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckResult;\n\nvoid fillMetadataContext(const std::vector<const MetadataProto*>& source_metadata,\n                         const std::vector<std::string>& metadata_context_namespaces,\n                         const std::vector<std::string>& typed_metadata_context_namespaces,\n                         MetadataProto& metadata_context) {\n  for (const auto& context_key : metadata_context_namespaces) {\n    for (const MetadataProto* metadata : source_metadata) {\n      if (metadata == nullptr) {\n        continue;\n      }\n      const auto& filter_metadata = metadata->filter_metadata();\n      if (const auto metadata_it = filter_metadata.find(context_key);\n          metadata_it != filter_metadata.end()) {\n        (*metadata_context.mutable_filter_metadata())[metadata_it->first] = metadata_it->second;\n        break;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  for (const auto& context_key : typed_metadata_context_namespaces) {\n    for (const MetadataProto* metadata : source_metadata) {\n      if (metadata == nullptr) {\n        continue;\n      }"
}
237. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc | sed -n '155,230p'",
  "description": "Read initiateCall function in ext_authz.cc HEAD"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "      allowed_headers_matcher_ = Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckRequestUtils::toRequestMatchers(\n          config.allowed_headers(), true, factory_context);\n    }\n  }\n  if (config.has_disallowed_headers()) {\n    disallowed_headers_matcher_ = Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckRequestUtils::toRequestMatchers(\n        config.disallowed_headers(), false, factory_context);\n  }\n}\n\nvoid FilterConfigPerRoute::merge(const FilterConfigPerRoute& other) {\n  // We only merge context extensions here, and leave boolean flags untouched since those flags are\n  // not used from the merged config.\n  auto begin_it = other.context_extensions_.begin();\n  auto end_it = other.context_extensions_.end();\n  for (auto it = begin_it; it != end_it; ++it) {\n    context_extensions_[it->first] = it->second;\n  }\n}\n\nvoid Filter::initiateCall(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\n  if (filter_return_ == FilterReturn::StopDecoding) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Now that we'll definitely be making the request, add filter state stats if configured to do so.\n  const Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterStateSharedPtr& filter_state =\n      decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().filterState();\n  if ((config_->emitFilterStateStats() || config_->filterMetadata().has_value())) {\n    if (!filter_state->hasData<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName())) {\n      filter_state->setData(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName(),\n                            std::make_shared<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(config_->filterMetadata()),\n                            Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterState::StateType::Mutable,\n                            Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterState::LifeSpan::Request);\n\n      // This may return nullptr (if there's a value at this name whose type doesn't match or isn't\n      // mutable, for example), so we must check logging_info_ is not nullptr later.\n      logging_info_ =\n          filter_state->getDataMutable<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName());\n    }\n    if (logging_info_ == nullptr) {\n      stats_.filter_state_name_collision_.inc();\n      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug,\n                       \"Could not find logging info at {}! (Did another filter already put data \"\n                       \"at this name?)\",\n                       *decoder_callbacks_, decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName());\n    }\n  }\n\n  absl::optional<FilterConfigPerRoute> maybe_merged_per_route_config;\n  for (const FilterConfigPerRoute& cfg :\n       Http::Utility::getAllPerFilterConfig<FilterConfigPerRoute>(decoder_callbacks_)) {\n    if (maybe_merged_per_route_config.has_value()) {\n      maybe_merged_per_route_config.value().merge(cfg);\n    } else {\n      maybe_merged_per_route_config = cfg;\n    }\n  }\n\n  Protobuf::Map<std::string, std::string> context_extensions;\n  if (maybe_merged_per_route_config) {\n    context_extensions = maybe_merged_per_route_config.value().takeContextExtensions();\n  }\n\n  // If metadata_context_namespaces or typed_metadata_context_namespaces is specified,\n  // pass matching filter metadata to the ext_authz service.\n  // If metadata key is set in both the connection and request metadata,\n  // then the value will be the request metadata value.\n  envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata metadata_context;\n  fillMetadataContext({&decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().dynamicMetadata(),\n                       &decoder_callbacks_->connection()->streamInfo().dynamicMetadata()},\n                      config_->metadataContextNamespaces(),\n                      config_->typedMetadataContextNamespaces(), metadata_context);\n\n  // Fill route_metadata_context from the selected route's metadata.\n  envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata route_metadata_context;"
}

239. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/extensions/filters/http/fault/fault_filter.cc | sed -n '200,260p'",
  "description": "Read the response rate limit section in fault_filter.cc HEAD"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "  absl::optional<uint64_t> rate_kbps =\n      fault_settings_->responseRateLimit()->rateKbps(&request_headers);\n  if (!rate_kbps.has_value()) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  if (!tryIncActiveFaults()) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  config_->stats().response_rl_injected_.inc();\n\n  response_limiter_ = std::make_unique<Envoy::Extensions::HttpFilters::Common::StreamRateLimiter>(\n      rate_kbps.value(), encoder_callbacks_->encoderBufferLimit(),\n      [this] { encoder_callbacks_->onEncoderFilterAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark(); },\n      [this] { encoder_callbacks_->onEncoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark(); },\n      [this](Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) {\n        encoder_callbacks_->injectEncodedDataToFilterChain(data, end_stream);\n      },\n      [this] { encoder_callbacks_->continueEncoding(); },\n      [](uint64_t, bool, std::chrono::milliseconds) {\n        // write stats callback.\n      },\n      config_->timeSource(), decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher(), decoder_callbacks_->scope());\n}\n\nbool FaultFilter::faultOverflow() {\n  const uint64_t max_faults = config_->runtime().snapshot().getInteger(\n      fault_settings_->maxActiveFaultsRuntime(), fault_settings_->maxActiveFaults().has_value()\n                                                     ? fault_settings_->maxActiveFaults().value()\n                                                     : std::numeric_limits<uint64_t>::max());\n  // Note: Since we don't compare/swap here this is a fuzzy limit which is similar to how the\n  // other circuit breakers work.\n  if (config_->stats().active_faults_.value() >= max_faults) {\n    config_->stats().faults_overflow_.inc();\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  return false;\n}\n\nbool FaultFilter::isDelayEnabled(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\n  const auto request_delay = fault_settings_->requestDelay();\n  if (request_delay == nullptr) {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  if (!downstream_cluster_delay_percent_key_.empty()) {\n    return config_->runtime().snapshot().featureEnabled(\n        downstream_cluster_delay_percent_key_, request_delay->percentage(&request_headers));\n  }\n  return config_->runtime().snapshot().featureEnabled(fault_settings_->delayPercentRuntime(),\n                                                      request_delay->percentage(&request_headers));\n}\n\nbool FaultFilter::isAbortEnabled(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\n  const auto request_abort = fault_settings_->requestAbort();\n  if (request_abort == nullptr) {\n    return false;\n  }"
}

245. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/common/runtime/runtime_features.cc | grep -B 5 -A 5 \"RUNTIME_GUARD\" | head -30",
  "description": "Check RUNTIME_GUARD macro definition in runtime_features.cc"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "#include \"absl/flags/commandlineflag.h\"\n#include \"absl/flags/flag.h\"\n#include \"absl/strings/match.h\"\n#include \"absl/strings/str_replace.h\"\n\n#define RUNTIME_GUARD(name) ABSL_FLAG(bool, name, true, \"\");        // NOLINT\n#define FALSE_RUNTIME_GUARD(name) ABSL_FLAG(bool, name, false, \"\"); // NOLINT\n\n// Add additional features here to enable the new code paths by default.\n//\n// Per documentation in CONTRIBUTING.md is expected that new high risk code paths be guarded\n// by runtime feature guards. If you add a guard of the form\n// RUNTIME_GUARD(envoy_reloadable_features_my_feature_name)\n// here you can guard code checking against \"envoy.reloadable_features.my_feature_name\".\n// Please note the swap of envoy_reloadable_features_ to envoy.reloadable_features.!\n//\n// if (Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\"envoy.reloadable_features.my_feature_name\")) {\n//   [new code path]\n// else {\n//   [old_code_path]\n// }\n//\n// Runtime features are true by default, so the new code path is exercised.\n// To make a runtime feature false by default, use FALSE_RUNTIME_GUARD, and add\n// a TODO to change it to true.\n//\n// If issues are found that require a runtime feature to be disabled, it should be reported\n// ASAP by filing a bug on github. Overriding non-buggy code is strongly discouraged to avoid the\n// problem of the bugs being found after the old code path has been removed.\nRUNTIME_GUARD(envoy_reloadable_features_allow_alt_svc_for_ips);"
}
246. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc | sed -n '413,460p'",
  "description": "Read updateLoggingInfo function fully in HEAD ext_authz.cc"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "void Filter::updateLoggingInfo() {\n  if (!config_->emitFilterStateStats()) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  if (logging_info_ == nullptr) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Latency is the only stat available if we aren't using envoy grpc.\n  if (start_time_.has_value()) {\n    logging_info_->setLatency(std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::microseconds>(\n        decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime() - start_time_.value()));\n  }\n\n  auto const* stream_info = client_->streamInfo();\n  if (stream_info == nullptr) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const auto& bytes_meter = stream_info->getUpstreamBytesMeter();\n  if (bytes_meter != nullptr) {\n    logging_info_->setBytesSent(bytes_meter->wireBytesSent());\n    logging_info_->setBytesReceived(bytes_meter->wireBytesReceived());\n  }\n  if (stream_info->upstreamInfo().has_value()) {\n    logging_info_->setUpstreamHost(stream_info->upstreamInfo()->upstreamHost());\n  }\n  absl::optional<Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr> cluster_info =\n      stream_info->upstreamClusterInfo();\n  if (cluster_info) {\n    logging_info_->setClusterInfo(std::move(*cluster_info));\n  }\n}\n\nvoid Filter::setEncoderFilterCallbacks(Http::StreamEncoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) {\n  encoder_callbacks_ = &callbacks;\n}\n\nvoid Filter::onDestroy() {\n  if (state_ == State::Calling) {\n    state_ = State::Complete;\n    client_->cancel();\n  }\n}\n\nCheckResult Filter::validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\n    Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation operation, absl::string_view key,"
}
247. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc | sed -n '470,520p'",
  "description": "Read the onComplete route cache clearing logic in HEAD ext_authz.cc"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "void Filter::onComplete(Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponsePtr&& response) {\n  state_ = State::Complete;\n  using Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus;\n  Stats::StatName empty_stat_name;\n\n  updateLoggingInfo();\n\n  if (!response->dynamic_metadata.fields().empty()) {\n    if (!config_->enableDynamicMetadataIngestion()) {\n      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n                       \"Response is trying to inject dynamic metadata, but dynamic metadata \"\n                       \"ingestion is disabled. Ignoring...\",\n                       *decoder_callbacks_);\n      stats_.ignored_dynamic_metadata_.inc();\n    } else {\n      // Add duration of call to dynamic metadata if applicable\n      if (start_time_.has_value() && response->status == CheckStatus::OK) {\n        ProtobufWkt::Value ext_authz_duration_value;\n        auto duration =\n            decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime() - start_time_.value();\n        ext_authz_duration_value.set_number_value(\n            std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(duration).count());\n        (*response->dynamic_metadata.mutable_fields())[\"ext_authz_duration\"] =\n            ext_authz_duration_value;\n      }\n      decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setDynamicMetadata(\"envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\",\n                                                          response->dynamic_metadata);\n    }\n  }\n\n  switch (response->status) {\n  case CheckStatus::OK: {\n    // Any changes to request headers or query parameters can affect how the request is going to be\n    // routed. If we are changing the headers we also need to clear the route\n    // cache.\n    if (config_->clearRouteCache() &&\n        (!response->headers_to_set.empty() || !response->headers_to_append.empty() ||\n         !response->headers_to_remove.empty() || !response->query_parameters_to_set.empty() ||\n         !response->query_parameters_to_remove.empty())) {\n      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"ext_authz is clearing route cache\", *decoder_callbacks_);\n      decoder_callbacks_->downstreamCallbacks()->clearRouteCache();\n    }\n\n    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n                     \"ext_authz filter added header(s) to the request:\", *decoder_callbacks_);\n    for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_set) {\n      CheckResult check_result = validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\n          Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation::SET, key, value);\n      switch (check_result) {\n      case CheckResult::OK:\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);"
}

249. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc | head -50",
  "description": "Read ext_authz.cc from HEAD - first 50 lines"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "#include \"ext_authz.h\"\n#include \"source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\"\n\n#include <chrono>\n#include <optional>\n#include <string>\n#include <vector>\n\n#include \"envoy/config/core/v3/base.pb.h\"\n\n#include \"source/common/common/assert.h\"\n#include \"source/common/common/enum_to_int.h\"\n#include \"source/common/common/matchers.h\"\n#include \"source/common/http/utility.h\"\n#include \"source/common/router/config_impl.h\"\n\nnamespace Envoy {\nnamespace Extensions {\nnamespace HttpFilters {\nnamespace ExtAuthz {\n\nnamespace {\n\nusing MetadataProto = ::envoy::config::core::v3::Metadata;\nusing Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation;\nusing Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckResult;\n\nvoid fillMetadataContext(const std::vector<const MetadataProto*>& source_metadata,\n                         const std::vector<std::string>& metadata_context_namespaces,\n                         const std::vector<std::string>& typed_metadata_context_namespaces,\n                         MetadataProto& metadata_context) {\n  for (const auto& context_key : metadata_context_namespaces) {\n    for (const MetadataProto* metadata : source_metadata) {\n      if (metadata == nullptr) {\n        continue;\n      }\n      const auto& filter_metadata = metadata->filter_metadata();\n      if (const auto metadata_it = filter_metadata.find(context_key);\n          metadata_it != filter_metadata.end()) {\n        (*metadata_context.mutable_filter_metadata())[metadata_it->first] = metadata_it->second;\n        break;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  for (const auto& context_key : typed_metadata_context_namespaces) {\n    for (const MetadataProto* metadata : source_metadata) {\n      if (metadata == nullptr) {\n        continue;\n      }"
}

For example, given:\n569:        // \"2\"}}) to the request to upstream server by only sets `headers_to_append`.\n593:    for (const auto& key : response->headers_to_remove) {\n628:    if (!response->response_headers_to_add.empty()) {\n630:                       *decoder_callbacks_, response->response_headers_to_add.size());\n631:      for (const auto& [key, value] : response->response_headers_to_add) {\n640:      response_headers_to_add_ = Http::HeaderVector{response->response_headers_to_add.begin(),\n641:                                                    response->response_headers_to_add.end()};\n644:    if (!response->response_headers_to_set.empty()) {\n646:                       *decoder_callbacks_, response->response_headers_to_set.size());\n647:      for (const auto& [key, value] : response->response_headers_to_set) {\n656:      response_headers_to_set_ = Http::HeaderVector{response->response_headers_to_set.begin(),\n657:                                                    response->response_headers_to_set.end()};\n660:    if (!response->response_headers_to_add_if_absent.empty()) {\n662:                       *decoder_callbacks_, response->response_headers_to_add_if_absent.size());\n663:      for (const auto& [key, value] : response->response_headers_to_add_if_absent) {\n672:      response_headers_to_add_if_absent_ =\n673:          Http::HeaderVector{response->response_headers_to_add_if_absent.begin(),\n674:                             response->response_headers_to_add_if_absent.end()};\n695:    absl::optional<Http::Utility::QueryParamsMulti> modified_query_parameters;\n696:    if (!response->query_parameters_to_set.empty()) {\n697:      modified_query_parameters = Http::Utility::QueryParamsMulti::parseQueryString(\n701:      for (const auto& [key, value] : response->query_parameters_to_set) {\n711:        modified_query_parameters->overwrite(key, value);"
}

  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "  switch (response->status) {\n  case CheckStatus::OK: {\n    // Any changes to request headers or query parameters can affect how the request is going to be\n    // routed. If we are changing the headers we also need to clear the route\n    // cache.\n    if (config_->clearRouteCache() &&\n        (!response->headers_to_set.empty() || !response->headers_to_append.empty() ||\n         !response->headers_to_remove.empty() || !response->query_parameters_to_set.empty() ||\n         !response->query_parameters_to_remove.empty())) {\n      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"ext_authz is clearing route cache\", *decoder_callbacks_);\n      decoder_callbacks_->downstreamCallbacks()->clearRouteCache();\n    }\n\n    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n                     \"ext_authz filter added header(s) to the request:\", *decoder_callbacks_);\n    for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_set) {\n      CheckResult check_result = validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\n          Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation::SET, key, value);\n      switch (check_result) {\n      case CheckResult::OK:\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\n        request_headers_->setCopy(Http::LowerCaseString(key), value);\n        break;\n      case CheckResult::IGNORE:\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Ignoring invalid header to set '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n                         key, value);\n        break;\n      case CheckResult::FAIL:\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"Rejecting invalid header to set '{}':'{}'.\", *decoder_callbacks_,\n                         key, value);\n        rejectResponse();\n        return;\n      }\n    }\n    for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_add) {\n      CheckResult check_result = validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\n          Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation::SET, key, value);\n      switch (check_result) {\n      case CheckResult::OK:\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);\n        request_headers_->addCopy(Http::LowerCaseString(key), value);"
}

{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "bool ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::canContinue() {\n  // It is possible for the connection manager to respond directly to a request even while\n  // a filter is trying to continue. If a response has already happened, we should not\n  // continue to further filters. A concrete example of this is a filter buffering data, the\n  // last data frame comes in and the filter continues, but the final buffering takes the stream\n  // over the high watermark such that a 413 is returned.\n  return !parent_.stopDecoderFilterChain();\n}\n\nbool ActiveStreamEncoderFilter::canContinue() {\n  // As with ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::canContinue() make sure we do not\n  // continue if a local reply has been sent or ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::recreateStream() is\n  // called, etc.\n  return !parent_.state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_ &&\n         (!Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n              \"envoy.reloadable_features.filter_chain_aborted_can_not_continue\") ||\n          !parent_.stopEncoderFilterChain());\n}\n\nBuffer::InstancePtr ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::createBuffer() {\n  auto buffer = dispatcher().getWatermarkFactory().createBuffer(\n      [this]() -> void { this->requestDataDrained(); },"
}

260. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/extensions/filters/http/fault/fault_filter.cc | sed -n '409,470p'",
  "description": "Read tryIncActiveFaults and onDestroy from fault_filter.cc"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "bool FaultFilter::tryIncActiveFaults() {\n  // Only charge 1 active fault per filter in case we are injecting multiple faults.\n  // Since we count at most one active fault per filter, we also allow multiple faults\n  // per filter without checking for overflow.\n  if (fault_active_) {\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  // We only check for overflow when attempting to perform a fault. Note that this means that a\n  // single request might increment the counter more than once if it tries to apply multiple faults,\n  // and it is also possible for it to fail the first check then succeed on the second (should\n  // another thread decrement the active fault gauge).\n  if (faultOverflow()) {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  // TODO(mattklein123): Consider per-fault type active fault gauges.\n  config_->stats().active_faults_.inc();\n  fault_active_ = true;\n\n  return true;\n}\n\nvoid FaultFilter::onDestroy() {\n  resetTimerState();\n  if (response_limiter_ != nullptr) {\n    response_limiter_->destroy();\n  }\n  if (fault_active_) {\n    config_->stats().active_faults_.dec();\n  }\n}\n\nbool FaultFilter::isResponseRateLimitConfigured() {\n  return fault_settings_->responseRateLimit() != nullptr;\n}\n\nvoid FaultFilter::postDelayInjection(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\n  resetTimerState();\n\n  // Delays can be followed by aborts\n  absl::optional<Http::Code> http_status;\n  absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status;\n  std::tie(http_status, grpc_status) = abortStatus(request_headers);\n\n  if (http_status.has_value()) {\n    abortWithStatus(http_status.value(), grpc_status);\n  } else {\n    // Should not continue to count as an active fault after the delay has elapsed if no other type\n    // of fault is active. As the delay timer is always done at this point and followed abort faults\n    // have been checked earlier, here we just check if there's a response rate limit configured.\n    ASSERT(fault_active_);\n    ASSERT(delay_timer_ == nullptr);\n    if (!isResponseRateLimitConfigured()) {\n      config_->stats().active_faults_.dec();\n      fault_active_ = false;\n    }\n\n    // Continue request processing.\n    decoder_callbacks_->continueDecoding();\n  }\n}"
}

262. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/common/http/header_utility.cc | sed -n '38,100p'",
  "description": "Read matchHeaders function in header_utility.cc"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "bool HeaderUtility::matchHeaders(const HeaderMap& request_headers,\n                                 const std::vector<HeaderDataPtr>& config_headers) {\n  // No headers to match is considered a match.\n  if (!config_headers.empty()) {\n    for (const HeaderDataPtr& cfg_header_data : config_headers) {\n      if (!cfg_header_data->matchesHeaders(request_headers)) {\n        return false;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  return true;\n}\n\nHeaderUtility::GetAllOfHeaderAsStringResult\nHeaderUtility::getAllOfHeaderAsString(const HeaderMap::GetResult& header_value,\n                                      absl::string_view separator) {\n  GetAllOfHeaderAsStringResult result;\n  // In this case we concatenate all found headers using a delimiter before performing the\n  // final match. We use an InlinedVector of absl::string_view to invoke the optimized join\n  // algorithm. This requires a copying phase before we invoke join. The 3 used as the inline\n  // size has been arbitrarily chosen.\n  // TODO(mattklein123): Do we need to normalize any whitespace here?\n  absl::InlinedVector<absl::string_view, 3> string_view_vector;\n  string_view_vector.reserve(header_value.size());\n  for (size_t i = 0; i < header_value.size(); i++) {\n    string_view_vector.push_back(header_value[i]->value().getStringView());\n  }\n  result.result_backing_string_ = absl::StrJoin(string_view_vector, separator);\n\n  return result;\n}\n\nHeaderUtility::GetAllOfHeaderAsStringResult\nHeaderUtility::getAllOfHeaderAsString(const HeaderMap& headers, const Http::LowerCaseString& key,\n                                      absl::string_view separator) {\n  GetAllOfHeaderAsStringResult result;\n  const auto header_value = headers.get(key);\n\n  if (header_value.empty()) {\n    // Empty for clarity. Avoid handling the empty case in the block below if the runtime feature\n    // is disabled.\n  } else if (header_value.size() == 1) {\n    result.result_ = header_value[0]->value().getStringView();\n  } else {\n    return getAllOfHeaderAsString(header_value, separator);\n  }\n\n  return result;\n}\n\nHeaderUtility::HeaderDataPtr\nHeaderUtility::createHeaderData(const envoy::config::route::v3::HeaderMatcher& config,\n                                Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& factory_context) {\n  switch (config.header_match_specifier_case()) {\n  case envoy::config::route::v3::HeaderMatcher::HeaderMatchSpecifierCase::kExactMatch:\n    return std::make_unique<HeaderDataExactMatch>(config);\n    break;\n  case envoy::config::route::v3::HeaderMatcher::HeaderMatchSpecifierCase::kSafeRegexMatch:\n    return THROW_OR_RETURN_VALUE(HeaderDataRegexMatch::create(config, factory_context),\n                                 std::unique_ptr<HeaderDataRegexMatch>);\n    break;\n  case envoy::config::route::v3::HeaderMatcher::HeaderMatchSpecifierCase::kRangeMatch:"
}

  "stdout": "Http::Status HeaderUtility::checkRequiredResponseHeaders(const Http::ResponseHeaderMap& headers) {\n  const absl::optional<uint64_t> status = Utility::getResponseStatusOrNullopt(headers);\n  if (!status.has_value()) {\n    return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n        absl::StrCat(\"missing required header: \", Envoy::Http::Headers::get().Status.get()));\n  }\n  return Http::okStatus();\n}\n\nbool HeaderUtility::isRemovableHeader(absl::string_view header) {\n  return (header.empty() || header[0] != ':') &&\n         !absl::EqualsIgnoreCase(header, Headers::get().HostLegacy.get());\n}\n\nbool HeaderUtility::isModifiableHeader(absl::string_view header) {\n  return (header.empty() || header[0] != ':') &&\n         !absl::EqualsIgnoreCase(header, Headers::get().HostLegacy.get());\n}\n\nHeaderUtility::HeaderValidationResult HeaderUtility::checkHeaderNameForUnderscores(\n    absl::string_view header_name,\n    envoy::config::core::v3::HttpProtocolOptions::HeadersWithUnderscoresAction\n        headers_with_underscores_action,\n    HeaderValidatorStats& stats) {\n  if (headers_with_underscores_action == envoy::config::core::v3::HttpProtocolOptions::ALLOW ||\n      !HeaderUtility::headerNameContainsUnderscore(header_name)) {\n    return HeaderValidationResult::ACCEPT;\n  }\n  if (headers_with_underscores_action =="
}
264. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/common/http/filter_manager.cc | sed -n '1,50p'",
  "description": "Read filter_manager.cc includes"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "#include \"source/common/http/filter_manager.h\"\n\n#include <functional>\n\n#include \"envoy/http/header_map.h\"\n#include \"envoy/matcher/matcher.h\"\n\n#include \"source/common/common/enum_to_int.h\"\n#include \"source/common/common/scope_tracked_object_stack.h\"\n#include \"source/common/common/scope_tracker.h\"\n#include \"source/common/http/codes.h\"\n#include \"source/common/http/header_map_impl.h\"\n#include \"source/common/http/header_utility.h\"\n#include \"source/common/http/utility.h\"\n\n#include \"matching/data_impl.h\"\n\nnamespace Envoy {\nnamespace Http {\n\nnamespace {\n\n// Shared helper for recording the latest filter used.\ntemplate <class Filters>\nvoid recordLatestDataFilter(typename Filters::Iterator current_filter,\n                            typename Filters::Element*& latest_filter, Filters& filters) {\n  // If this is the first time we're calling onData, just record the current filter.\n  if (latest_filter == nullptr) {\n    latest_filter = current_filter->get();\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // We want to keep this pointing at the latest filter in the filter list that has received the\n  // onData callback. To do so, we compare the current latest with the *previous* filter. If they\n  // match, then we must be processing a new filter for the first time. We omit this check if we're\n  // the first filter, since the above check handles that case.\n  //\n  // We compare against the previous filter to avoid multiple filter iterations from resetting the\n  // pointer: If we just set latest to current, then the first onData filter iteration would\n  // correctly iterate over the filters and set latest, but on subsequent onData iterations\n  // we'd start from the beginning again, potentially allowing filter N to modify the buffer even\n  // though filter M > N was the filter that inserted data into the buffer.\n  if (current_filter != filters.begin() && latest_filter == std::prev(current_filter)->get()) {\n    latest_filter = current_filter->get();\n  }\n}\n\n} // namespace\n\nvoid ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonContinue() {"
}

266. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/common/http/filter_manager.cc | sed -n '985,1010p'",
  "description": "Read the second runtime feature check in filter_manager.cc"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Resetting stream due to {}. onLocalReply requested reset.\", *this,\n                     details);\n    filter_manager_callbacks_.resetStream(Http::StreamResetReason::LocalReset, \"\");\n    return;\n  }\n\n  if (!filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders().has_value() &&\n      (!filter_manager_callbacks_.informationalHeaders().has_value() ||\n       (Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n            \"envoy.reloadable_features.local_reply_traverses_filter_chain_after_1xx\") &&\n        !(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsEncodingMask)))) {\n    // If the response has not started at all, or if the only response so far is an informational\n    // 1xx that has already been fully processed, send the response through the filter chain.\n\n    if (auto cb = filter_manager_callbacks_.downstreamCallbacks(); cb.has_value()) {\n      // The initial route maybe never be set or the cached route maybe cleared by the filters.\n      // This will force route refreshment if there is not a cached route to avoid potential\n      // route refreshment in the response filter chain.\n      cb->route(nullptr);\n    }\n\n    // We only prepare a local reply to execute later if we're actively\n    // invoking filters to avoid re-entrant in filters.\n    if (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsDecodingMask) {\n      prepareLocalReplyViaFilterChain(is_grpc_request, code, body, modify_headers, is_head_request,\n                                      grpc_status, details);"
}

268. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc | sed -n '413,445p'",
  "description": "Read updateLoggingInfo function in ext_authz.cc"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "void Filter::updateLoggingInfo() {\n  if (!config_->emitFilterStateStats()) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  if (logging_info_ == nullptr) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Latency is the only stat available if we aren't using envoy grpc.\n  if (start_time_.has_value()) {\n    logging_info_->setLatency(std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::microseconds>(\n        decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime() - start_time_.value()));\n  }\n\n  auto const* stream_info = client_->streamInfo();\n  if (stream_info == nullptr) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const auto& bytes_meter = stream_info->getUpstreamBytesMeter();\n  if (bytes_meter != nullptr) {\n    logging_info_->setBytesSent(bytes_meter->wireBytesSent());\n    logging_info_->setBytesReceived(bytes_meter->wireBytesReceived());\n  }\n  if (stream_info->upstreamInfo().has_value()) {\n    logging_info_->setUpstreamHost(stream_info->upstreamInfo()->upstreamHost());\n  }\n  absl::optional<Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr> cluster_info =\n      stream_info->upstreamClusterInfo();\n  if (cluster_info) {\n    logging_info_->setClusterInfo(std::move(*cluster_info));\n  }"
}

Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc | sed -n '445,520p'",

}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "  }\n}\n\nvoid Filter::setEncoderFilterCallbacks(Http::StreamEncoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) {\n  encoder_callbacks_ = &callbacks;\n}\n\nvoid Filter::onDestroy() {\n  if (state_ == State::Calling) {\n    state_ = State::Complete;\n    client_->cancel();\n  }\n}\n\nCheckResult Filter::validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\n    Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation operation, absl::string_view key,\n    absl::string_view value) const {\n  if (config_->validateMutations() && (!Http::HeaderUtility::headerNameIsValid(key) ||\n                                       !Http::HeaderUtility::headerValueIsValid(value))) {\n    return CheckResult::FAIL;\n  }\n  // Check header mutation is valid according to configured decoder mutation rules.\n  return config_->checkDecoderHeaderMutation(operation, Http::LowerCaseString(key), value);\n}\n\nvoid Filter::onComplete(Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::ResponsePtr&& response) {\n  state_ = State::Complete;\n  using Filters::Common::ExtAuthz::CheckStatus;\n  Stats::StatName empty_stat_name;\n\n  updateLoggingInfo();\n\n  if (!response->dynamic_metadata.fields().empty()) {\n    if (!config_->enableDynamicMetadataIngestion()) {\n      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n                       \"Response is trying to inject dynamic metadata, but dynamic metadata \"\n                       \"ingestion is disabled. Ignoring...\",\n                       *decoder_callbacks_);\n      stats_.ignored_dynamic_metadata_.inc();\n    } else {\n      // Add duration of call to dynamic metadata if applicable\n      if (start_time_.has_value() && response->status == CheckStatus::OK) {\n        ProtobufWkt::Value ext_authz_duration_value;\n        auto duration =\n            decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher().timeSource().monotonicTime() - start_time_.value();\n        ext_authz_duration_value.set_number_value(\n            std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(duration).count());\n        (*response->dynamic_metadata.mutable_fields())[\"ext_authz_duration\"] =\n            ext_authz_duration_value;\n      }\n      decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setDynamicMetadata(\"envoy.filters.http.ext_authz\",\n                                                          response->dynamic_metadata);\n    }\n  }\n\n  switch (response->status) {\n  case CheckStatus::OK: {\n    // Any changes to request headers or query parameters can affect how the request is going to be\n    // routed. If we are changing the headers we also need to clear the route\n    // cache.\n    if (config_->clearRouteCache() &&\n        (!response->headers_to_set.empty() || !response->headers_to_append.empty() ||\n         !response->headers_to_remove.empty() || !response->query_parameters_to_set.empty() ||\n         !response->query_parameters_to_remove.empty())) {\n      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"ext_authz is clearing route cache\", *decoder_callbacks_);\n      decoder_callbacks_->downstreamCallbacks()->clearRouteCache();\n    }\n\n    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n                     \"ext_authz filter added header(s) to the request:\", *decoder_callbacks_);\n    for (const auto& [key, value] : response->headers_to_set) {\n      CheckResult check_result = validateAndCheckDecoderHeaderMutation(\n          Filters::Common::MutationRules::CheckOperation::SET, key, value);\n      switch (check_result) {\n      case CheckResult::OK:\n        ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"'{}':'{}'\", *decoder_callbacks_, key, value);"
}

Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "bool FaultFilter::maybeSetupDelay(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\n  absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds> duration = delayDuration(request_headers);\n  if (duration.has_value() && tryIncActiveFaults()) {\n    delay_timer_ = decoder_callbacks_->dispatcher().createTimer(\n        [this, &request_headers]() -> void { postDelayInjection(request_headers); });\n    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"fault: delaying request {}ms\", duration.value().count());\n    delay_timer_->enableTimer(duration.value(), &decoder_callbacks_->scope());\n    recordDelaysInjectedStats();\n    decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::DelayInjected);\n    auto& dynamic_metadata = decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().dynamicMetadata();\n    (*dynamic_metadata.mutable_filter_metadata())[decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName()] =\n        fault_settings_->filterMetadata();\n    return true;\n  }\n  return false;\n}\n\nbool FaultFilter::maybeDoAbort(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\n  absl::optional<Http::Code> http_status;\n  absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status;\n  std::tie(http_status, grpc_status) = abortStatus(request_headers);\n\n  if (http_status.has_value() && tryIncActiveFaults()) {\n    abortWithStatus(http_status.value(), grpc_status);\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  return false;\n}\n\nvoid FaultFilter::maybeSetupResponseRateLimit(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\n  if (!isResponseRateLimitEnabled(request_headers)) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  absl::optional<uint64_t> rate_kbps =\n      fault_settings_->responseRateLimit()->rateKbps(&request_headers);\n  if (!rate_kbps.has_value()) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  if (!tryIncActiveFaults()) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  config_->stats().response_rl_injected_.inc();\n\n  response_limiter_ = std::make_unique<Envoy::Extensions::HttpFilters::Common::StreamRateLimiter>(\n      rate_kbps.value(), encoder_callbacks_->encoderBufferLimit(),\n      [this] { encoder_callbacks_->onEncoderFilterAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark(); },\n      [this] { encoder_callbacks_->onEncoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark(); },\n      [this](Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) {\n        encoder_callbacks_->injectEncodedDataToFilterChain(data, end_stream);\n      },\n      [this] { encoder_callbacks_->continueEncoding(); },"
}

Input
{
  "endLine": 470,
  "path": "source/extensions/filters/http/fault/fault_filter.cc",
  "repo": "github.com/envoyproxy/envoy",
  "startLine": 409
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"409: bool FaultFilter::tryIncActiveFaults() {\\n410:   // Only charge 1 active fault per filter in case we are injecting multiple faults.\\n411:   // Since we count at most one active fault per filter, we also allow multiple faults\\n412:   // per filter without checking for overflow.\\n413:   if (fault_active_) {\\n414:     return true;\\n415:   }\\n416: \\n417:   // We only check for overflow when attempting to perform a fault. Note that this means that a\\n418:   // single request might increment the counter more than once if it tries to apply multiple faults,\\n419:   // and it is also possible for it to fail the first check then succeed on the second (should\\n420:   // another thread decrement the active fault gauge).\\n421:   if (faultOverflow()) {\\n422:     return false;\\n423:   }\\n424: \\n425:   // TODO(mattklein123): Consider per-fault type active fault gauges.\\n426:   config_->stats().active_faults_.inc();\\n427:   fault_active_ = true;\\n428: \\n429:   return true;\\n430: }\\n431: \\n432: void FaultFilter::onDestroy() {\\n433:   resetTimerState();\\n434:   if (response_limiter_ != nullptr) {\\n435:     response_limiter_->destroy();\\n436:   }\\n437:   if (fault_active_) {\\n438:     config_->stats().active_faults_.dec();\\n439:   }\\n440: }\\n441: \\n442: bool FaultFilter::isResponseRateLimitConfigured() {\\n443:   return fault_settings_->responseRateLimit() != nullptr;\\n444: }\\n445: \\n446: void FaultFilter::postDelayInjection(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\\n447:   resetTimerState();\\n448: \\n449:   // Delays can be followed by aborts\\n450:   absl::optional<Http::Code> http_status;\\n451:   absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status;\\n452:   std::tie(http_status, grpc_status) = abortStatus(request_headers);\\n453: \\n454:   if (http_status.has_value()) {\\n455:     abortWithStatus(http_status.value(), grpc_status);\\n456:   } else {\\n457:     // Should not continue to count as an active fault after the delay has elapsed if no other type\\n458:     // of fault is active. As the delay timer is always done at this point and followed abort faults\\n459:     // have been checked earlier, here we just check if there's a response rate limit configured.\\n460:     ASSERT(fault_active_);\\n461:     ASSERT(delay_timer_ == nullptr);\\n462:     if (!isResponseRateLimitConfigured()) {\\n463:       config_->stats().active_faults_.dec();\\n464:       fault_active_ = false;\\n465:     }\\n466: \\n467:     // Continue request processing.\\n468:     decoder_callbacks_->continueDecoding();\\n469:   }\\n470: }\\n471: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/extensions/filters/http/fault/fault_filter.cc%3FL409-470\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "409: bool FaultFilter::tryIncActiveFaults() {\n410:   // Only charge 1 active fault per filter in case we are injecting multiple faults.\n411:   // Since we count at most one active fault per filter, we also allow multiple faults\n412:   // per filter without checking for overflow.\n413:   if (fault_active_) {\n414:     return true;\n415:   }\n416: \n417:   // We only check for overflow when attempting to perform a fault. Note that this means that a\n418:   // single request might increment the counter more than once if it tries to apply multiple faults,\n419:   // and it is also possible for it to fail the first check then succeed on the second (should\n420:   // another thread decrement the active fault gauge).\n421:   if (faultOverflow()) {\n422:     return false;\n423:   }\n424: \n425:   // TODO(mattklein123): Consider per-fault type active fault gauges.\n426:   config_->stats().active_faults_.inc();\n427:   fault_active_ = true;\n428: \n429:   return true;\n430: }\n431: \n432: void FaultFilter::onDestroy() {\n433:   resetTimerState();\n434:   if (response_limiter_ != nullptr) {\n435:     response_limiter_->destroy();\n436:   }\n437:   if (fault_active_) {\n438:     config_->stats().active_faults_.dec();\n439:   }\n440: }\n441: \n442: bool FaultFilter::isResponseRateLimitConfigured() {\n443:   return fault_settings_->responseRateLimit() != nullptr;\n444: }\n445: \n446: void FaultFilter::postDelayInjection(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\n447:   resetTimerState();\n448: \n449:   // Delays can be followed by aborts\n450:   absl::optional<Http::Code> http_status;\n451:   absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status;\n452:   std::tie(http_status, grpc_status) = abortStatus(request_headers);\n453: \n454:   if (http_status.has_value()) {\n455:     abortWithStatus(http_status.value(), grpc_status);\n456:   } else {\n457:     // Should not continue to count as an active fault after the delay has elapsed if no other type\n458:     // of fault is active. As the delay timer is always done at this point and followed abort faults\n459:     // have been checked earlier, here we just check if there's a response rate limit configured.\n460:     ASSERT(fault_active_);\n461:     ASSERT(delay_timer_ == nullptr);\n462:     if (!isResponseRateLimitConfigured()) {\n463:       config_->stats().active_faults_.dec();\n464:       fault_active_ = false;\n465:     }\n466: \n467:     // Continue request processing.\n468:     decoder_callbacks_->continueDecoding();\n469:   }\n470: }\n471: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/extensions/filters/http/fault/fault_filter.cc%3FL409-470"
  }

},\n216:       [this] { encoder_callbacks_->onEncoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark(); },\n217:       [this](Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) {\n218:         encoder_callbacks_->injectEncodedDataToFilterChain(data, end_stream);\n219:       },\n220:       [this] { encoder_callbacks_->continueEncoding(); },\n221: ",

  }
}

}

  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "6542256517 Stringmatcher: factory context for router header matcher (#33019)\n00536345a1 stream info: rename the extended response flag and response flag (#32105)\nc2ae221119 Add metadata to ext_proc and fault filter (#27003)\n230a1780c6 [Bandwidth-Limit] Fix bandwidth delay calculation logic (#24024)\n0986bb6d49 router: update all http filters to get per filter config by the http filter config name at first (#22002)\n8b8ece8adb [Bandwidth-Limit] Add response trailers for bandwidth delay (#18267)\n9dd8ae3c69 filters/http: remove well_known_names (#16871)\n0fae6970dd build: modify include paths to be more bazel friendly (#16711)\ne7abafd27b http: refactory Http::Utility::resolveMostSpecificPerFilterConfig method (#16673)\n38d1c1984f [filter]: Add option to disable fault filter stats that trace downstream server name (#16235)\n795a4f53f6 http: refactor out stream rate limiter to common (#14828)\n97c722fd2a common: add a thread safe token bucket (#14827)\neb0ada6548 fault injection: stop counting as active fault after delay elapsed (#14762)\n2f6870510b fault: only check for overflow when performing faults (#12843)\n2e6aed49f9 fault: fix decrementing of active_faults gauge (#12083)\n436732f1dd Buffer overflow watermark implementation - base (#11179)\n8ae7046a1e http: replacing more header accessors with the new Value helper (#11285)\nfd366655fd fault injection: add support for setting gRPC status (#10841)\na7f411c221 stats: add utilities to create stats from a vector of tokens, mixing dynamic and symbolic elements. (#10735)\n6cbbe0d328 fault: control % of requests faults are applied to with HTTP headers (#10724)"
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"38: bool HeaderUtility::matchHeaders(const HeaderMap& request_headers,\\n39:                                  const std::vector<HeaderDataPtr>& config_headers) {\\n40:   // No headers to match is considered a match.\\n41:   if (!config_headers.empty()) {\\n42:     for (const HeaderDataPtr& cfg_header_data : config_headers) {\\n43:       if (!cfg_header_data->matchesHeaders(request_headers)) {\\n44:         return false;\\n45:       }\\n46:     }\\n47:   }\\n48: \\n49:   return true;\\n50: }\\n51: \\n52: HeaderUtility::GetAllOfHeaderAsStringResult\\n53: HeaderUtility::getAllOfHeaderAsString(const HeaderMap::GetResult& header_value,\\n54:                                       absl::string_view separator) {\\n55:   GetAllOfHeaderAsStringResult result;\\n56:   // In this case we concatenate all found headers using a delimiter before performing the\\n57:   // final match. We use an InlinedVector of absl::string_view to invoke the optimized join\\n58:   // algorithm. This requires a copying phase before we invoke join. The 3 used as the inline\\n59:   // size has been arbitrarily chosen.\\n60:   // TODO(mattklein123): Do we need to normalize any whitespace here?\\n61: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/envoyproxy/envoy@8e3950f0ce278eec90f4abf2e8280f91a33d6c08/-/blob/source/common/http/header_utility.cc%3FL38-60\"}",

  }

285. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:source/common/http/filter_manager.cc | sed -n '960,1030p'",
  "description": "Read sendLocalReply in filter_manager.cc"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "void DownstreamFilterManager::sendLocalReply(\n    Code code, absl::string_view body,\n    const std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)>& modify_headers,\n    const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status, absl::string_view details) {\n  ASSERT(!state_.under_on_local_reply_);\n  const bool is_head_request = state_.is_head_request_;\n  const bool is_grpc_request = state_.is_grpc_request_;\n\n  // Stop filter chain iteration if local reply was sent while filter decoding or encoding callbacks\n  // are running.\n  if (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsDecodingMask) {\n    state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n  } else if (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsEncodingMask) {\n    state_.encoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n  }\n  // When independent half-close is enabled local reply always stops decoder filter chain.\n  if (filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled()) {\n    state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n  }\n\n  streamInfo().setResponseCodeDetails(details);\n  StreamFilterBase::LocalReplyData data{code, grpc_status, details, false};\n  onLocalReply(data);\n  if (data.reset_imminent_) {\n    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Resetting stream due to {}. onLocalReply requested reset.\", *this,\n                     details);\n    filter_manager_callbacks_.resetStream(Http::StreamResetReason::LocalReset, \"\");\n    return;\n  }\n\n  if (!filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders().has_value() &&\n      (!filter_manager_callbacks_.informationalHeaders().has_value() ||\n       (Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n            \"envoy.reloadable_features.local_reply_traverses_filter_chain_after_1xx\") &&\n        !(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsEncodingMask)))) {\n    // If the response has not started at all, or if the only response so far is an informational\n    // 1xx that has already been fully processed, send the response through the filter chain.\n\n    if (auto cb = filter_manager_callbacks_.downstreamCallbacks(); cb.has_value()) {\n      // The initial route maybe never be set or the cached route maybe cleared by the filters.\n      // This will force route refreshment if there is not a cached route to avoid potential\n      // route refreshment in the response filter chain.\n      cb->route(nullptr);\n    }\n\n    // We only prepare a local reply to execute later if we're actively\n    // invoking filters to avoid re-entrant in filters.\n    if (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsDecodingMask) {\n      prepareLocalReplyViaFilterChain(is_grpc_request, code, body, modify_headers, is_head_request,\n                                      grpc_status, details);\n    } else {\n      sendLocalReplyViaFilterChain(is_grpc_request, code, body, modify_headers, is_head_request,\n                                   grpc_status, details);\n    }\n  } else if (!state_.non_100_response_headers_encoded_) {\n    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Sending local reply with details {} directly to the encoder\", *this,\n                     details);\n    // In this case, at least the header and possibly the body has started\n    // processing through the filter chain, but no non-informational headers\n    // have been sent downstream. To ensure that filters don't get their\n    // state machine screwed up, bypass the filter chain and send the local\n    // reply directly to the codec.\n    //\n    sendDirectLocalReply(code, body, modify_headers, state_.is_head_request_, grpc_status);\n  } else {\n    // If we land in this branch, response headers have already been sent to the client.\n    // All we can do at this point is reset the stream.\n    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Resetting stream due to {}. Prior headers have already been sent\",\n                     *this, details);\n    // TODO(snowp): This means we increment the tx_reset stat which we weren't doing previously."
}

}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "0038ebbacf Fix the crash caused by continuing the filter after recreating stream (#37627)\n4fa73a88c9 filter chain: prefer vector over list for filter chain (#37665)\nb7c429f534 http: simplify the logic of upstream filter chain factory (#37518)\n3402e0c8c3 clang-tidy: Assorted source fixes (#37454)\nbc1ed87d6a http: unified the filter chain creation in the FilterManager (#37112)\nb1a2af6a29 http: allow trigger GOAWAY from l7 filters (#36306)\ne0c9f1e650 regex: removing exceptions (#37264)\n7cf53442e4 http: allow local replies to traverse the filter chain after 1xx headers (#37097)\n4c0975228a access_log: add UPSTREAM_HOST_NAME_WITHOUT_PORT variable (#37114)\ne3da7ebb16 router: use template method to avoid unused memory allocations in HeaderData (#36878)"
}

  "noOutputExpected": false,

  "stdout": "9c754412b9 fix potential null dereference in ext_authz (#36268)\na4ad9c2619 route: use reference wrapper for get all filter config (#36079)\n1eedeeed1a ext_authz: add logging options (#35698)\nb916786755 route: get all per filter config directly (#36028)\nff6ced12ef ext_authz: filter state access logging stats PREP (#34945)\n8646385431 add knob disable_dynamic_metadata_ingestion (#34691)\n7d8791802c exceptions: minor cleanup (#34655)\n74f327dc8b Add decoder header mutation rules (#34182)\n85d8cc7455 Add disallowed_headers knob to ext_authz config (#33349)\n1c6eba59db Add explicit header mutation validation ext_authz (#33244)"
}
294. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show b916786755 -- source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc | head -30",
  "description": "Show the diff of the commit that introduced the wrong include"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "commit b916786755867f01ec36c8165b4d8b0708b6075e\nAuthor: code <wbphub@gmail.com>\nDate:   Tue Sep 10 00:03:21 2024 +0800\n\n    route: get all per filter config directly (#36028)\n    \n    Commit Message: route: get all per filter config directly\n    Additional Description:\n    \n    1. used absl::string_view rather than `const std::string&` as input\n    parameter type. This allow the `mostSpecificPerFilterConfig()` and\n    `perFilterConfigs()` to take a string view as input parameter.\n    2. replace the `traversePerFilterConfig()` with `perFilterConfigs()`.\n    The `perFilterConfigs()` will return a vector (inlined) of configs\n    directly and needn't the caller to provide a callback function to\n    collect them. The new method is more straight forward and easier to use.\n    The new method didn't effect any exist logic, but reduce ~150 lines\n    code.\n    \n    Risk Level: low.\n    Testing: n/a.\n    Docs Changes: n/a.\n    Release Notes: n/a.\n    Platform Specific Features: n/a.\n    \n    ---------\n    \n    Signed-off-by: wangbaiping <wbphub@gmail.com>\n\ndiff --git a/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc b/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc"
}

  "stdout": "index d301d12689..92bde93d37 100644\n--- a/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc\n+++ b/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc\n@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@\n+#include \"ext_authz.h\"\n #include \"source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\"\n \n #include <chrono>\n@@ -175,11 +176,16 @@ void Filter::initiateCall(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\n     return;\n   }\n \n-  auto&& maybe_merged_per_route_config =\n-      Http::Utility::getMergedPerFilterConfig<FilterConfigPerRoute>(\n-          decoder_callbacks_, [](FilterConfigPerRoute& cfg_base, const FilterConfigPerRoute& cfg) {\n-            cfg_base.merge(cfg);\n-          });\n+  absl::optional<FilterConfigPerRoute> maybe_merged_per_route_config;\n+  for (const auto* cfg :\n+       Http::Utility::getAllPerFilterConfig<FilterConfigPerRoute>(decoder_callbacks_)) {\n+    ASSERT(cfg != nullptr);\n+    if (maybe_merged_per_route_config.has_value()) {\n+      maybe_merged_per_route_config.value().merge(*cfg);\n+    } else {\n+      maybe_merged_per_route_config = *cfg;\n+    }\n+  }\n \n   Protobuf::Map<std::string, std::string> context_extensions;\n   if (maybe_merged_per_route_config) {"
}
296. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show 7cf53442e4 --stat | head -20",
  "description": "Show what files the 1xx local reply commit changed"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "commit 7cf53442e41632514030cd4b662e94ada30bea2d\nAuthor: Paul Ogilby <pgal@google.com>\nDate:   Wed Nov 20 13:25:21 2024 -0500\n\n    http: allow local replies to traverse the filter chain after 1xx headers (#37097)\n    \n    If 1xx informational headers have been fully sent to the codec, and primary response headers have not yet arrived, it\n    is safe to send a local reply through the filter chain.\n    \n    Risk Level: Low\n    Testing: Integration tests\n    Docs Changes: None\n    Release Notes: None\n    \n    ---------\n    \n    Signed-off-by: Paul Ogilby <pgal@google.com>\n\n changelogs/current.yaml                            |  4 ++\n source/common/http/filter_manager.cc               |  8 +++-"
}
297. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show 0038ebbacf --stat | head -20",
  "description": "Show what files the canContinue fix commit changed"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "commit 0038ebbacfe7899ce1c0b76c5136f1f319c3b2b0\nAuthor: \u6f84\u6f6d <zty98751@alibaba-inc.com>\nDate:   Fri Jan 10 00:11:24 2025 +0800\n\n    Fix the crash caused by continuing the filter after recreating stream (#37627)\n    \n    After the recreatStream() call, both the\n    decode and encode filter chains will abort. Here, by checking if the\n    filter chain has aborted, it prevents the subsequent filters in the\n    filter chain from continuing.\n    \n    Risk Level: Low\n    Testing: Add tests to verify that when recreateStream is executed\n    during the decodeHeader/encodeHeader phase and returns StopIteration,\n    executing continueDecoding/continueEncoding does not proceed with the\n    processing of subsequent filters\n    \n    Runtime guard: envoy.reloadable_features.filter_chain_aborted_can_not_continue\n    Fixes #37625"
}
298. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show 0038ebbacf -- source/common/http/filter_manager.cc | head -80 | tail -60",
  "description": "Show the full diff of the canContinue fix in filter_manager.cc"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "commit 0038ebbacfe7899ce1c0b76c5136f1f319c3b2b0\nAuthor: \u6f84\u6f6d <zty98751@alibaba-inc.com>\nDate:   Fri Jan 10 00:11:24 2025 +0800\n\n    Fix the crash caused by continuing the filter after recreating stream (#37627)\n    \n    After the recreatStream() call, both the\n    decode and encode filter chains will abort. Here, by checking if the\n    filter chain has aborted, it prevents the subsequent filters in the\n    filter chain from continuing.\n    \n    Risk Level: Low\n    Testing: Add tests to verify that when recreateStream is executed\n    during the decodeHeader/encodeHeader phase and returns StopIteration,\n    executing continueDecoding/continueEncoding does not proceed with the\n    processing of subsequent filters\n    \n    Runtime guard: envoy.reloadable_features.filter_chain_aborted_can_not_continue\n    Fixes #37625\n    \n    ---------\n    \n    Signed-off-by: zty98751 <zty98751@alibaba-inc.com>\n    Signed-off-by: \u6f84\u6f6d <zty98751@alibaba-inc.com>\n\ndiff --git a/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc b/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc\nindex cc4fb77ec5..665c0572a9 100644\n--- a/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc\n+++ b/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc\n@@ -347,8 +347,12 @@ bool ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::canContinue() {\n \n bool ActiveStreamEncoderFilter::canContinue() {\n   // As with ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::canContinue() make sure we do not\n-  // continue if a local reply has been sent.\n-  return !parent_.state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_;\n+  // continue if a local reply has been sent or ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::recreateStream() is\n+  // called, etc.\n+  return !parent_.state_.encoder_filter_chain_complete_ &&\n+         (!Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n+              \"envoy.reloadable_features.filter_chain_aborted_can_not_continue\") ||\n+          !parent_.stopEncoderFilterChain());\n }\n \n Buffer::InstancePtr ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::createBuffer() {"
}

Input
{
  "command": "git show 9c754412b9 -- source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc | head -80 | tail -60",
  "description": "Show what the null dereference fix changed in ext_authz.cc"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "    Risk Level: low\n    Testing: unit tested\n    Docs Changes: none\n    Release Notes: none\n    Platform Specific Features: none\n    \n    ---------\n    \n    Signed-off-by: antoniovleonti <leonti@google.com>\n\ndiff --git a/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc b/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc\nindex 962aede2c2..7874c8a94b 100644\n--- a/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc\n+++ b/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc\n@@ -180,15 +180,25 @@ void Filter::initiateCall(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers) {\n   // Now that we'll definitely be making the request, add filter state stats if configured to do so.\n   const Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterStateSharedPtr& filter_state =\n       decoder_callbacks_->streamInfo().filterState();\n-  if ((config_->emitFilterStateStats() || config_->filterMetadata().has_value()) &&\n-      !filter_state->hasData<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName())) {\n-    filter_state->setData(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName(),\n-                          std::make_shared<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(config_->filterMetadata()),\n-                          Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterState::StateType::Mutable,\n-                          Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterState::LifeSpan::Request);\n+  if ((config_->emitFilterStateStats() || config_->filterMetadata().has_value())) {\n+    if (!filter_state->hasData<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName())) {\n+      filter_state->setData(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName(),\n+                            std::make_shared<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(config_->filterMetadata()),\n+                            Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterState::StateType::Mutable,\n+                            Envoy::StreamInfo::FilterState::LifeSpan::Request);\n \n-    logging_info_ =\n-        filter_state->getDataMutable<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName());\n+      // This may return nullptr (if there's a value at this name whose type doesn't match or isn't\n+      // mutable, for example), so we must check logging_info_ is not nullptr later.\n+      logging_info_ =\n+          filter_state->getDataMutable<ExtAuthzLoggingInfo>(decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName());\n+    }\n+    if (logging_info_ == nullptr) {\n+      stats_.filter_state_name_collision_.inc();\n+      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug,\n+                       \"Could not find logging info at {}! (Did another filter already put data \"\n+                       \"at this name?)\",\n+                       *decoder_callbacks_, decoder_callbacks_->filterConfigName());\n+    }\n   }\n \n   absl::optional<FilterConfigPerRoute> maybe_merged_per_route_config;\n@@ -405,6 +415,10 @@ void Filter::updateLoggingInfo() {\n     return;\n   }\n \n+  if (logging_info_ == nullptr) {\n+    return;\n+  }\n+\n   // Latency is the only stat available if we aren't using envoy grpc.\n   if (start_time_.has_value()) {\n     logging_info_->setLatency(std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::microseconds>("
}

}
308. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "{\n  \"review\": {\n    \"summary\": \"Code review of four Envoy HTTP filter chain files: ext_authz.cc, filter_manager.cc, header_utility.cc, and fault_filter.cc. One bug was identified: a spurious relative header include introduced in ext_authz.cc by a refactoring commit that changed per-filter config retrieval.\",\n    \"findings\": [\n      {\n        \"file\": \"source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc\",\n        \"line\": 1,\n        \"severity\": \"error\",\n        \"category\": \"incorrect_include\",\n        \"description\": \"Spurious relative include '#include \\\"ext_authz.h\\\"' was added on line 1 by commit b916786755 (route: get all per filter config directly). This relative include path is incorrect for Bazel's include path rules and duplicates the correct absolute include on line 2 ('#include \\\"source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\\\"'). In Bazel builds, include paths are workspace-relative, so a bare 'ext_authz.h' would not resolve correctly and would cause a compile failure. The fix is to remove the spurious line 1.\",\n        \"fix\": \"Remove the line '#include \\\"ext_authz.h\\\"' from the top of the file. The correct absolute-path include already exists on line 2.\"\n      }\n    ],\n    \"files_reviewed\": [\n      {\n        \"file\": \"source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc\",\n        \"status\": \"has_bugs\",\n        \"notes\": \"One confirmed bug: spurious relative include on line 1 introduced by commit b916786755. All logic in onComplete(), updateLoggingInfo(), initiateCall(), and the route cache clearing condition are correct for the v1.33.0 codebase.\"\n      },\n      {\n        \"file\": \"source/common/http/filter_manager.cc\",\n        \"status\": \"no_bugs\",\n        \"notes\": \"ActiveStreamEncoderFilter::canContinue() correctly adds a runtime-flag-gated check for encoder_filter_chain_aborted_ (envoy.reloadable_features.filter_chain_aborted_can_not_continue), which defaults to true via RUNTIME_GUARD. ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::canContinue() correctly uses !parent_.stopDecoderFilterChain() which already checked decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ before this PR. The sendLocalReply local_reply_traverses_filter_chain_after_1xx runtime guard is also correctly implemented.\"\n      },\n      {\n        \"file\": \"source/common/http/header_utility.cc\",\n        \"status\": \"no_bugs\",\n        \"notes\": \"matchHeaders() implements correct AND-logic (all header matchers must match), isRemovableHeader() and checkRequiredResponseHeaders() are correctly implemented and match the reference implementation.\"\n      },\n      {\n        \"file\": \"source/extensions/filters/http/fault/fault_filter.cc\",\n        \"status\": \"no_bugs\",\n        \"notes\": \"tryIncActiveFaults() correctly guards against double-counting (fault_active_ flag), onDestroy() correctly decrements active_faults_ gauge only if fault_active_ is true, postDelayInjection() correctly decrements the gauge and resets fault_active_ when no follow-on faults are active. maybeSetupDelay() and maybeDoAbort() are correctly implemented.\"\n      }\n    ]\n  }\n}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/review.json"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\n  \"review\": {\n    \"summary\": \"Code review of four Envoy HTTP filter chain files: ext_authz.cc, filter_manager.cc, header_utility.cc, and fault_filter.cc. One bug was identified: a spurious relative header include introduced in ext_authz.cc by a refactoring commit that changed per-filter config retrieval.\",\n    \"findings\": [\n      {\n        \"file\": \"source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc\",\n        \"line\": 1,\n        \"severity\": \"error\",\n        \"category\": \"incorrect_include\",\n        \"description\": \"Spurious relative include '#include \\\"ext_authz.h\\\"' was added on line 1 by commit b916786755 (route: get all per filter config directly). This relative include path is incorrect for Bazel's include path rules and duplicates the correct absolute include on line 2 ('#include \\\"source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\\\"'). In Bazel builds, include paths are workspace-relative, so a bare 'ext_authz.h' would not resolve correctly and would cause a compile failure. The fix is to remove the spurious line 1.\",\n        \"fix\": \"Remove the line '#include \\\"ext_authz.h\\\"' from the top of the file. The correct absolute-path include already exists on line 2.\"\n      }\n    ],\n    \"files_reviewed\": [\n      {\n        \"file\": \"source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc\",\n        \"status\": \"has_bugs\",\n        \"notes\": \"One confirmed bug: spurious relative include on line 1 introduced by commit b916786755. All logic in onComplete(), updateLoggingInfo(), initiateCall(), and the route cache clearing condition are correct for the v1.33.0 codebase.\"\n      },\n      {\n        \"file\": \"source/common/http/filter_manager.cc\",\n        \"status\": \"no_bugs\",\n        \"notes\": \"ActiveStreamEncoderFilter::canContinue() correctly adds a runtime-flag-gated check for encoder_filter_chain_aborted_ (envoy.reloadable_features.filter_chain_aborted_can_not_continue), which defaults to true via RUNTIME_GUARD. ActiveStreamDecoderFilter::canContinue() correctly uses !parent_.stopDecoderFilterChain() which already checked decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ before this PR. The sendLocalReply local_reply_traverses_filter_chain_after_1xx runtime guard is also correctly implemented.\"\n      },\n      {\n        \"file\": \"source/common/http/header_utility.cc\",\n        \"status\": \"no_bugs\",\n        \"notes\": \"matchHeaders() implements correct AND-logic (all header matchers must match), isRemovableHeader() and checkRequiredResponseHeaders() are correctly implemented and match the reference implementation.\"\n      },\n      {\n        \"file\": \"source/extensions/filters/http/fault/fault_filter.cc\",\n        \"status\": \"no_bugs\",\n        \"notes\": \"tryIncActiveFaults() correctly guards against double-counting (fault_active_ flag), onDestroy() correctly decrements active_faults_ gauge only if fault_active_ is true, postDelayInjection() correctly decrements the gauge and resets fault_active_ when no follow-on faults are active. maybeSetupDelay() and maybeDoAbort() are correctly implemented.\"\n      }\n    ]\n  }\n}\n",
  "filePath": "/workspace/review.json",
  "originalFile": null,
  "structuredPatch": [],
  "type": "create"
}

{
  "command": "# Check the specific diff for the files we changed\ngit diff -- source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc source/common/http/filter_manager.cc source/common/http/header_utility.cc source/extensions/filters/http/fault/fault_filter.cc",
  "description": "Show diff only for the 4 target files"
}

  "interrupted": false,

  "stdout": "diff --git a/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc b/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc\nindex 7874c8a94b..89ca26ef66 100644\n--- a/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc\n+++ b/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc\n@@ -1,4 +1,3 @@\n-#include \"ext_authz.h\"\n #include \"source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\"\n \n #include <chrono>"

Input
{
  "command": "# Verify file sizes and first lines\necho \"=== ext_authz.cc (fixed) ===\"\nwc -l /workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc\nhead -3 /workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc\n\necho \"\"\necho \"=== filter_manager.cc (unchanged) ===\"\nwc -l /workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc\n\necho \"\"\necho \"=== header_utility.cc (unchanged) ===\"\nwc -l /workspace/source/common/http/header_utility.cc\n\necho \"\"\necho \"=== fault_filter.cc (unchanged) ===\"\nwc -l /workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/fault/fault_filter.cc\n\necho \"\"\necho \"=== review.json ===\"\ncat /workspace/review.json | python3 -m json.tool > /dev/null && echo \"Valid JSON\" || echo \"Invalid JSON\"",
  "description": "Final verification of all files"
}

        Code Changes (1)
        
1. WRITE /workspace/review.json
{
  "review": {
    "summary": "Code review of four Envoy HTTP filter chain files: ext_authz.cc, filter_manager.cc, header_utility.cc, and fault_filter.cc. One bug was identified: a spurious relative header include introduced in ext_authz.cc by a refactoring commit that changed per-filter config retrieval.",
    "findings": [
      {
        "file": "source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc",
        "line": 1,
        "severity": "error",
        "category": "incorrect_include",
        "description": "Spurious relative include '#include \"ext_authz.h\"' was added on line 1 by commit b916786755 (route: get all per filter config directly). This relative include path is incorrect for Bazel's include path rules and duplicates the correct absolute include on line 2 ('#include \"source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.h\"'). In Bazel builds, include paths are workspace-relative, so a bare 'ext_authz.h' would not resolve correctly and would cause a compile failure. The fix is to remove the spurious line 1.",
        "fix": "Remove the line '#include \"ext_authz.h\"' from the top of the file. The correct absolute-path include already exists on line 2."
      }
    ],
    "files_reviewed": [
      {
        "file": "source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc",
        "status": "has_bugs",
        "notes": "One confirmed bug: spurious relative include on line 1 introduced by commit b916786755. All logic in onComplete(), updateLoggingInfo(), initiateCall(), and the route cache clearing condition are correct for the v1.33.0 codebase."
      },
      {
        "file": "source/common/http/filter_manager.cc",
        "status": "no_bugs",
        "notes": "ActiveStreamEncoderFilter::canContinue() correctly adds a runtime-flag-gated check for encode
... (truncated)

  if [ "$first_line" != '#include "ext_authz.h"' ]; then
    echo "First commit WITHOUT the wrong include: $commit"
    git log --oneline -1 $commit
    break
  fi
done

197. $ git show HEAD:source/extensions/filters/common/ext_authz/ext_authz.h | grep -n "grpc_status\|headers_to_add\|saw_invalid" | head -20

git show HEAD:source/common/http/filter_manager.cc | wc -l
200. $ git show HEAD:source/common/http/filter_manager.cc | sed -n '1,20p'
201. $ git show HEAD:source/common/http/filter_manager.h | grep -n "runtime_features\|runtime_impl\|runtime.h" | head -10
202. $ git log --oneline -10 -- source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc source/common/http/filter_manager.cc source/common/http/header_utility.cc source/extensions/filters/http/fault/fault_filter.cc
203. $ git log --oneline -10 -- source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc

205. $ git show b916786755 -- source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc | head -60 | tail -30
206. $ git show 7cf53442e4 --stat | head -20
207. $ git show 0038ebbacf --stat | head -20
208. $ git show 0038ebbacf -- source/common/http/filter_manager.cc | head -80 | tail -60
209. $ git show 9c754412b9 -- source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc | head -80 | tail -60
210. $ git show HEAD:source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc > /tmp/ext_authz_head.cc
wc -l /tmp/ext_authz_head.cc
211. $ head -5 /tmp/ext_authz_head.cc
212. $ # Remove the buggy first line and write to the working tree file
tail -n +2 /tmp/ext_authz_head.cc > /workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc
head -5 /workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc
213. $ # Copy the other 3 files unchanged from HEAD
git show HEAD:source/common/http/filter_manager.cc > /workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc
git show HEAD:source/common/http/header_utility.cc > /workspace/source/common/http/header_utility.cc
git show HEAD:source/extensions/filters/http/fault/fault_filter.cc > /workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/fault/fault_filter.cc

# Verify they're now non-empty
wc -l /workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc
wc -l /workspace/source/common/http/header_utility.cc
wc -l /workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/fault/fault_filter.cc
wc -l /workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc
214. $ head -3 /workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc
echo "---"
# Make sure the wrong include doesn't appear anywhere
grep -n '#include "ext_authz.h"' /workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc || echo "BUG FIXED: wrong include not found"
215. $ git show HEAD:source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc | sed -n '505,511p'

ls /workspace/tests/ 2>/dev/null && bash /workspace/tests/test.sh 2>&1 || echo "No test script found"

# Verify the git diff looks correct
git diff --stat
217. $ # Check the specific diff for the files we changed
git diff -- source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc source/common/http/filter_manager.cc source/common/http/header_utility.cc source/extensions/filters/http/fault/fault_filter.cc
218. $ # Verify file sizes and first lines
echo "=== ext_authz.cc (fixed) ==="
wc -l /workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc
head -3 /workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/ext_authz/ext_authz.cc

echo ""
echo "=== filter_manager.cc (unchanged) ==="
wc -l /workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc

echo ""
echo "=== header_utility.cc (unchanged) ==="
wc -l /workspace/source/common/http/header_utility.cc

echo ""
echo "=== fault_filter.cc (unchanged) ==="
wc -l /workspace/source/extensions/filters/http/fault/fault_filter.cc

echo ""
echo "=== review.json ==="
cat /workspace/review.json | python3 -m json.tool > /dev/null && echo "Valid JSON" || echo "Invalid JSON"
